3::miaandthesevendayplan.title: Mia and the Seven-Day Plan
3::miaandthesevendayplan.teaching_focus: Practice forming the future with 'will' (+ base verb), using different subjects and simple objects. Drills: substitute subjects, change the action, add short time words (e.g., today, tomorrow). Also practice speaking and short written plans.
3::miaandthesevendayplan.questions_type:
Reading for Details: 1.How many days is Mia's plan?(Seven) 2.Is Mia's room messy or tidy?(Messy) 3.What is on Sunday?(Piano show) 4.What will Mia do first?(Homework) 5.How long will she play piano each day?(One hour)Reading

Skill: 1.Does Mia clean her room on Tuesday or Sunday?(Tuesday) 2.What does Mia do after she makes her plan?(She does homework first) 3.Which task happens every day?(Practice piano)Reading Strategy: 1.Will Mia be busy or lazy this week?(Busy) 2.Will the plan help Mia?(Yes) 3.Will Mia play well on Sunday?(Yes)

Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What is the story about? 2.(Inference)Why is Mia worried? 3.(Vocabulary)What does "finish" mean? 4.(Feelings)How does Mia feel at the end? 5.(Connection)What is on your plan this week?
3::miaandthesevendayplan.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Is Mia's room messy or tidy?(Messy) 2.(On)How many days is Mia's plan?(Seven) 3.(On)Does Mia clean her room on Tuesday or Sunday?(Tuesday) 4.(On)Will the plan help her feel better?(Yes) 5.(Above)Why does Mia feel proud?(Because she finished her tasks/plan)
3::miaandthesevendayplan.go_description: 以时间为轴线，按顺序呈现事件的线性图形组织器
3::miaandthesevendayplan.go_usage: 标注从 Monday 到 Sunday，Mia 每天计划做的任务

3::fromsunrisetobedtime.title: From Sunrise to Bedtime
3::fromsunrisetobedtime.teaching_focus: Practice time expressions and the present simple for routines; swap different times, subjects and verbs to make new sentences; speaking and asking/answering 'What happens at ...?' for fluency.
3::fromsunrisetobedtime.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.When does the sky become bright?(At sunrise) 2.What do people do in the morning?(Wake and eat breakfast) 3.Where is the sun at noon?(High and bright) 4.What colors is the sky at sunset?(Orange and pink) 5.Is the world loud or quiet at bedtime?(Quiet)Reading Skill: 1.Name one afternoon activity.(Play and learn) 2.Is "rest" morning or evening activity?(Evening) 3.How many parts of the day are in the book?(Six/Eight)Reading Strategy: 1.Is this book about weather or the day?(The day) 2.Is "sleep" at start or end?(End) 3.Does sky change colors quickly or slowly?(Slowly)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What happens during a whole day? 2.(Inference)Can we see the sun at bedtime? 3.(Vocabulary)What is the opposite of "bright"? 4.(Fact/Opinion)"The sky turns orange" is fact? 5.(Connection)What is your favorite time of day?
3::fromsunrisetobedtime.rr_questions: 1.(Under)When does the sky become bright?(At sunrise) 2.(On)What do people do in the morning?(Wake and eat breakfast) 3.(On)Is the world loud or quiet at bedtime?(Quiet) 4.(On)Is this book about weather or the day?(The day) 5.(Above)Can we see the sun at bedtime?Why not?(No,because it is dark/night)
3::fromsunrisetobedtime.go_description: 以圆形循环结构呈现一天从早到晚时段变化的图形组织器
3::fromsunrisetobedtime.go_usage: 在圆环上标注从 Sunrise 到 Bedtime 各个时段的活动

3::thebigfamilyreunion.title: The Big Family Reunion
3::thebigfamilyreunion.teaching_focus: Practice 'has' for features and 'wears' for clothes; substitution with different people and items; speaking and matching activities (listen and point to the person).
3::thebigfamilyreunion.reading_skill: 识别人物特征 (Story Elements: Character)
3::thebigfamilyreunion.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'family reunion' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thebigfamilyreunion.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thebigfamilyreunion.go_description: 以人物为中心,向外辐射其外貌特征的放射状图形组织器
3::thebigfamilyreunion.go_usage: 中心写 Relatives,分支写 Uncle Bob (beard)、Cousin Jack (hat)、Grandma Nina (scarf)及其特征

3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.title: Tommy and the Race Against Bedtime
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.teaching_focus: Students can practice subject + verb + reported words, simple time phrases and substitution (change the time or words). Good for speaking: ask and answer 'What does the clock say?' and make short oral sentences.
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.core_vocab_raw: on time, take a shower, brush teeth, soon
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.What is Tommy drawing?(A big star) 2.What toy is Tommy playing with?(A dinosaur) 3.Does Tommy want to go to bed?(No) 4.Where does Tommy run?(The bathroom) 5.What time is Tommy in bed?(9:30)Reading Skill: 1.Scan Page6:Find three things Tommy does.(Shower,brush teeth,run) 2.Scan "surprised":Which page?(Page4/5) 3.Scan Tommy's pet:What is it?(A dog/The toy dinosaur)Reading Strategy: 1.What time is it on Page5?(Nine) 2.Is Tommy early or late?(Late/Soon) 3.Is 9:30 on time for Tommy?(Yes)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What is Tommy's "race"? 2.(Inference)Why does Tommy run to the bathroom? 3.(Vocabulary)What does "soon" mean? 4.(Feelings)Why does Tommy smile at the end? 5.(Connection)What time do you go to bed?
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.rr_questions: 1.(Under)What is Tommy drawing?(A big star) 2.(On)What time is Tommy in bed?(9:30) 3.(On)Is he early or late?(Late/Soon) 4.(On)Name two things Tommy does to get ready.(Shower,brush teeth) 5.(Above)Why does Tommy smile at the end?(He won his "race"/was on time)
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.go_description: 以时钟表盘为载体，呈现对应时间行为的图形组织器
3::tommyandtheraceagainstbedtime.go_usage: 画出 9:00 和 9:30 的时钟，并对应写下 Tommy 在做的事情

3::seasonalchanges.title: Seasonal Changes
3::seasonalchanges.teaching_focus: Practice 'In + time/season' + 'there are' with plural nouns. Focus on season words, simple nouns, and substitution drills (change the season or the things). Use questions like 'What is there in spring?' and short spoken answers.
3::seasonalchanges.core_vocab_raw: parts of the world, autumn, hot days, cold days
3::seasonalchanges.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.How many seasons?(Four) 2.What come out in spring?(Animals) 3.What do people do in summer?(Swim and have fun) 4.What color are leaves in autumn?(Yellow) 5.What color is snow in winter?(White)Reading Skill: 1.Contrast summer and winter days.(Hot vs Cold) 2.Compare spring and autumn:What changes?(Leaves/Nature) 3.Which season is cool?(Autumn)Reading Strategy: 1.Visualize winter:What do you see?(Snow/Animals hiding) 2.Draw autumn:What color is wind?(Cool/Yellow leaves) 3.Which page is most colorful?(Autumn/Spring)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What does the year do? 2.(Inference)Why do animals hide in winter? 3.(Vocabulary)What does "joy" mean? 4.(Gist)About one country or many parts? 5.(Connection)Which season do you like best?
3::seasonalchanges.rr_questions: 1.(Under)How many seasons are there?(Four) 2.(On)What color are leaves in autumn?(Yellow) 3.(On)Contrast summer and winter days.(Hot vs Cold) 4.(On)Draw winter:What is on the ground?(White snow) 5.(Above)Why do some animals hide in winter?(Because it is cold/to sleep)
3::seasonalchanges.go_description: 以四分圆盘结构呈现四季特征的圆形图形组织器
3::seasonalchanges.go_usage: 在转盘四个区域分别画出春夏秋冬的代表色（如白色雪、黄色叶子）

3::beingagoodfriend.title: Being a Good Friend
3::beingagoodfriend.teaching_focus: Practice subject + simple present verb with a fixed subject ('A good friend'). Use substitution drills to try different action verbs (share, listen, help, care), build short spoken answers, and teach meaning with gestures and examples.
3::beingagoodfriend.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'listener' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::beingagoodfriend.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::beingagoodfriend.go_description: 以主题为中心,向外辐射关键特征的圆形图形组织器
3::beingagoodfriend.go_usage: 中心写 Good Friend,四个分支分别写 shares、listens、helps、says sorry

3::miagetsdressed.title: Mia Gets Dressed
3::miagetsdressed.teaching_focus: Practice the present simple action verb with third person (-s), vocabulary for clothes and colors, making short full sentences, and substitution drills (change the subject or the clothing). Use gestures or real clothes for speaking practice.
3::miagetsdressed.core_vocab_raw: miss, sweater, look for, colorful clothes
3::miagetsdressed.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.What color is Mia's sweater?(Red) 2.What does Mia put on after sweater?(Blue pants) 3.What color are her socks?(Green and purple) 4.What is Mia missing?(The other shoe) 5.Who has Mia's shoe?(The puppy)Reading Skill: 1.Scan Page4:Name two items.(Hat and scarf) 2.Scan "basket":Who is near it?(The puppy) 3.How many colors in text?(Red,blue,green,purple,yellow,pink)Reading Strategy: 1.Do you ever lose your shoes?Where? 2.Do you like colorful clothes? 3.Do you have a pet?Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What is Mia doing? 2.(Inference)How did shoe get in basket? 3.(Vocabulary)What does "missing" mean? 4.(Feelings)How does Mia feel when find shoe? 5.(Prediction)Will Mia go outside now?
3::miagetsdressed.rr_questions: 1.(Under)What color is Mia's sweater?(Red) 2.(On)Who has Mia's shoe?(The puppy) 3.(On)Which page is "basket" on?(Page6) 4.(On)Do you ever lose your shoes?Where?(Personal connection) 5.(Above)How did the shoe get in the basket?(The puppy took it there)
3::miagetsdressed.go_description: 以人物身体轮廓为载体，标注对应服饰位置的图形组织器
3::miagetsdressed.go_usage: 画一个 Mia，让学生根据文本将 red sweater, blue pants 等贴在正确位置

3::whywewearclothes.title: Why We Wear Clothes
3::whywewearclothes.sentence_pattern: We wear clothes to [do something / show a reason].
3::whywewearclothes.teaching_focus: Practice the infinitive 'to + verb' for giving reasons, making short purpose sentences, substituting different verb phrases, and speaking simple answers to 'Why do we wear clothes?'
3::whywewearclothes.core_vocab_raw: wear clothes, style, keep ... warm, take care of
3::whywewearclothes.questions_type:
Reading for Details: 1.What help people every day?(Clothes) 2.Why wear clothes in cold?(To keep warm) 3.What does "block the hot sun" mean?(Stay safe from sun) 4.Who wears safe clothes for work?(Firefighters) 5.What is like small house for body?(Clothes)Reading

Skill: 1.Main idea of Page4?(Staying clean and dry) 2.Main reason for book?(Why we wear clothes) 3.Is "style" for work or happy?(Feeling happy)Reading

Strategy: 1.What question does author ask on Page1? 2.Ask about firefighters' clothes. 3.Why take care of clothes?Other Comprehension: 1.(Vocabulary)What does "block" mean? 2.(Inference)Can clothes be important for jobs? 3.(Analogy)Why clothes like house? 4.(Fact)Name one thing clothes do. 5.(Connection)What clothes do you wear today?
3::whywewearclothes.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Who wears safe work clothes?(Firefighters) 2.(On)Why wear clothes in cold?(To keep warm) 3.(On)Main idea of Page4?(Staying clean and dry) 4.(On)What do clothes do in sun?(Block the hot sun) 5.(Above)Why clothes like small house?(They protect you)
3::whywewearclothes.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
3::whywewearclothes.go_description: 中心为主旨、向外辐射细节的放射状图形组织器
3::whywewearclothes.go_usage: 中心写 “Why we wear clothes”，分支出 “Warm”, “Sun”, “Clean”, “Safe”

3::mybrotherssecretfort.title: My Brother's Secret Fort
3::mybrotherssecretfort.teaching_focus: Practice present simple (third person -s), making short statements, using place phrases (in the ...), and swapping nouns to make new sentences for speaking and writing drills.
3::mybrotherssecretfort.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'fort' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::mybrotherssecretfort.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::mybrotherssecretfort.go_description: 呈现故事四要素的线性图形组织器
3::mybrotherssecretfort.go_usage: 填写 Character（Tommy, Mia）、Setting（Tommy's room）、Problem（secret fort）、Solution（birthday card）

3::clothesforeverykindofweather.title: Clothes for Every Kind of Weather
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.teaching_focus: Practice subject + wears + clothing, and short condition phrases (when, for, on, in). Good for vocabulary (coat, boots, sunglasses, raincoat) and speaking drills where students substitute names, clothes, and weather.
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.reading_skill: 匹配关系 (Matching Weather to Clothes)
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.What does Tommy look at every day?(The weather) 2.What wear when rain?(A raincoat) 3.How feel in sunglasses?(Cool) 4.When wear boots?(For muddy days) 5.What on rain hat?(A duck beak)Reading Skill: 1.Match sunny→?(Sunglasses) 2.Match snow→?(Warm hat) 3.Match muddy→?(Boots)Reading Strategy: 1.Wear swimsuit in snow?(No) 2.Where is Tommy going?(Outside) 3.Is rain hat funny?(Yes)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)How choose clothes? 2.(Inference)Why dog laugh? 3.(Vocabulary)What "muddy days"? 4.(Feelings)Happy or sad about rain? 5.(Connection)What wear when rain?
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.rr_questions: 1.(Under)What wear when rain?(A raincoat) 2.(On)Match sunny→?(Sunglasses) 3.(On)Where is Tommy going?(Outside) 4.(On)How feel in sunglasses?(Cool) 5.(Above)Why dog laugh at hat?(It has duck beak/looks funny)
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.go_description: 两列对比结构，用于两组信息匹配对应的表格型图形组织器
3::clothesforeverykindofweather.go_usage: 左边写 Weather（Rainy, Sunny, Snowy），右边写对应的 Clothing

3::differentclothesforwork.title: Different Clothes for Work
3::differentclothesforwork.teaching_focus: Practice saying what you wear for different activities using the same structure; swap jobs and clothing words; build simple clothing vocabulary and short speaking answers.
3::differentclothesforwork.questions_type:
Reading for Details: 1.What wear for work?(A uniform) 2.What go with suit?(A tie) 3.What "badge" for?(Work/Scrubs) 4.What wear with helmet?(Boots) 5.How many outfits a week?(Three or more)

Reading Skill: 1.Scan "apron":Which job?(Chef/Cook/Worker) 2.Scan "safe":Why work clothes safe?(Protect you) 3.Scan "scrubs":Who wear?(Doctors/Nurses)Reading

Strategy: 1.What police wear?(Uniform/badge) 2.Where see suit and tie? 3.Why builder need helmet?(To be safe)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What text about? 2.(Inference)Why work clothes clean? 3.(Vocabulary)What "outfit"? 4.(Fact)All jobs same clothes? 5.(Connection)What wear for work grow up?
3::differentclothesforwork.rr_questions: 1.(Under)What go with suit?(A tie) 2.(On)Which job use apron?(Chef/Cook/Worker) 3.(On)What "outfit"?(A set of clothes) 4.(On)Why work clothes clean?(Tidy and safe) 5.(Above)Why need 3+ outfits a week?(They get dirty/busy)
3::differentclothesforwork.go_description: 以行列结构呈现职业与制服对应关系的表格型图形组织器
3::differentclothesforwork.go_usage: 列表格记录：Firefighter-Safety clothes, Chef-Apron, Doctor-Scrubs

3::howfamiliesaredifferent.title: How Families Are Different
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.teaching_focus: Practice subject 'Some families' + plural verbs, make simple statements about routines or habits, substitute different verbs and short phrases, and speak about differences in family life.
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.core_vocab_raw: look different, go on walks, far apart, video call
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'look different' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.go_description: 以多分支结构呈现各类家庭特征的放射状图形组织器
3::howfamiliesaredifferent.go_usage: 中心写 Families,分支写:big families / small families / cook together / play games / go on walks / live far apart--每支下写对应例子

3::thetinyteamatwork.title: The Tiny Team at Work
3::thetinyteamatwork.teaching_focus: Practice 'There are' with plural nouns, use adjectives before nouns, build simple 'that' clauses (that + verb + object). Good for substitution drills, speaking practice (describe roles), and short question-and-answer work.
3::thetinyteamatwork.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.What tiny team do?(Work all day) 2.Which cells find germs?(White cells) 3.What red cells carry?(Oxygen) 4.What germ do to nose?(Tickle it) 5.What sound made?(Achoo!)Reading Skill: 1.Which cells "brave"?(Fight germs) 2.Which cells "happy" at end?(All/The person) 3.What color carry oxygen cells?(Red)Reading Strategy: 1.White cells like police?(Yes) 2.Draw brave cell fighting germ. 3.How nose tickle feel?Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)How body stay safe? 2.(Inference)Why feel worried? 3.(Vocabulary)What "germ"? 4.(Feelings)How feel at end? 5.(Connection)What do when sneeze?
3::thetinyteamatwork.rr_questions: 1.(Under)What color carry oxygen cells?(Red) 2.(On)Which cells find germs?(White cells) 3.(On)What sound when germ tickle nose?(Achoo!) 4.(On)White cells like police:What catch?(Germs) 5.(Above)Why feel worried on Page5?(Because a germ came in)
3::thetinyteamatwork.go_description: 按类别分支呈现信息的层级式图形组织器
3::thetinyteamatwork.go_usage: 分支列出：Red cells (Oxygen), White cells (Find germs), Brave cells (Fight)

3::youramazingbody.title: Your Amazing Body
3::youramazingbody.teaching_focus: Practice the subject 'Your body', simple present verbs, and naming body parts/things. Use substitution drills to build speaking accuracy and confidence.
3::youramazingbody.questions_type:
Reading for Details: 1.What body make?(Blood and new cells) 2.How many heart beats a day?(100,000) 3.How many breaths a day?(20,000) 4.Biggest organ?(Skin) 5.How far blood vessels run?(12,000 miles)

Reading Skill: 1.Scan 20,000:What for?(Breaths) 2.Scan "night":Body stop?(No) 3.Scan "organ":Which part?(Skin)

Reading Strategy: 1.3 words:What heart do?(Beats all day) 2.What "machine" in book?(The body) 3.Why body "busy"?(Do many things at once)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)Why body like machine? 2.(Inference)Body work when asleep? 3.(Vocabulary)What "blood vessels"? 4.(Fact)Skin an organ? 5.(Connection)Can feel heart beating?
3::youramazingbody.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Biggest organ?(Skin) 2.(On)Heart beats a day?(100,000) 3.(On)20,000:What for?(Breaths) 4.(On)3 words:What heart do?(Beats all day) 5.(Above)Why body like busy machine?(Works day and night to help you live)
3::youramazingbody.graphic_organizer: Body Fact File (身体事实档案)
3::youramazingbody.go_description: 以档案形式整理关键数据与事实的结构化图形组织器
3::youramazingbody.go_usage: 用数字标注重点，如：100,000 (Heart beats), 20,000 (Breaths)

3::thetreehouseclub.title: The Treehouse Club
3::thetreehouseclub.teaching_focus: Students practice the phrase 'This + noun + is just for + noun/pronoun' to say who something is for, make simple substitutions (game, room, gift) and use short spoken responses or role-play about inclusion/exclusion.
3::thetreehouseclub.reading_skill: 识别问题与解决 (Problem and Solution)
3::thetreehouseclub.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'treehouse' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thetreehouseclub.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thetreehouseclub.go_description: 以两格结构呈现故事冲突与解决方法的图形组织器
3::thetreehouseclub.go_usage: 左格写 Problem（Tommy won't let new boy join）,右格写 Solution（Mia convinces Tommy, new rule written）

3::mybusybody.title: My Busy Body
3::mybusybody.teaching_focus: Practice 'can' for ability, use and to link two actions, substitute different verbs (especially body actions), short speaking drills and choral repetition for fluency and pronunciation.
3::mybusybody.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.How feel running circles?(Excited) 2.Clapping quiet or loud?(Loud) 3.What can wiggle?(Fingers) 4.Body sound like drum?(Hiccups) 5.What make calm?(Slow breathing)Reading Skill: 1.Name 3 actions.(Run,clap,wiggle,hop,jump) 2.Which action loud?(Clapping) 3.Which action slow?(Breathing)Reading Strategy: 1.When feel excited? 2.Can make funny face? 3.Like hop or jump more?Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What book about? 2.(Inference)Why hiccups surprise? 3.(Vocabulary)What "calm" mean? 4.(Feelings)How feel hop and jump? 5.(Connection)Try 3 slow breaths:feel calm?
3::mybusybody.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Clapping quiet or loud?(Loud) 2.(On)Name 3 actions.(Run,clap,wiggle,hop) 3.(On)Body sound like drum?(Hiccups) 4.(On)Slow breathing makes me____.(Calm) 5.(Above)How feel hop and jump?Why?(Strong and free)
3::mybusybody.go_description: 以树干树枝结构收集归类动作词的树状图形组织器
3::mybusybody.go_usage: 收集文中所有 I can 引导的动作词：run, clap, wiggle, hop, jump

3::bonesmusclesandmore.title: Bones, Muscles, and More
3::bonesmusclesandmore.teaching_focus: Practice making simple subject + verb + object phrases with 'Your...' and 'helps you ...'. Focus on substitution (change the body part and the action), correct verb 'helps' with singular subjects, short speaking drills, and answering questions like 'What helps you ...?'
3::bonesmusclesandmore.questions_type:
Reading for Details: 1.What give body shape?(Bones) 2.What pull bones to move?(Muscles) 3.What help bend run jump?(Joints) 4.How many bones in body?(206) 5.Smallest bone where?(In your ear)

Reading Skill: 1.Muscles work with?(Bones) 2.Biggest bone where?(In leg) 3.Be careful not to?(Hurt bones)Reading

Strategy: 1.Why need joints?(To bend) 2.What if no bones?(No shape) 3.How muscles move?(They pull)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)How body move? 2.(Inference)Move without muscles? 3.(Vocabulary)What "bend" mean? 4.(Fact)Bone in ear? 5.(Connection)Find knee joint?
3::bonesmusclesandmore.rr_questions: 1.(Under)How many bones?(206) 2.(On)What pull bones to move?(Muscles) 3.(On)Smallest bone where?(In your ear) 4.(On)Why need joints?(To bend and help run) 5.(Above)What if no bones?(No shape/cannot stand)
3::bonesmusclesandmore.go_description: 呈现事物结构与其功能对应关系的双列表格型图形组织器

3::homesaroundtheworld.title: Homes Around the World
3::homesaroundtheworld.teaching_focus: Practice present simple (build/builds), subject and verb agreement, vocabulary for homes and materials, and substitution drills (change the home and the material). Also use it to make simple questions: What do people build from X?
3::homesaroundtheworld.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
What do some people use to build igloos?

They use blocks of hard snow.



Why do people build stilt houses over water?

To keep themselves dry.



Where do people build bamboo houses?

In warm places.



What kind of people live in tents?

People who move often.



What do skyscrapers do in big cities?

They reach high into the sky.
3::homesaroundtheworld.rr_questions:
1. (Below-Level) What is one kind of home in this text?



2. (On-Level) What do people use to build igloos?



3. (On-Level) Why do people build stilt houses over water?



4. (On-Level) Where do people build bamboo houses?



5. (Above-Level) How does the text show that homes are connected to the environment?
3::homesaroundtheworld.go_description: 按类别分支呈现不同房屋类型的层级图形组织器
3::homesaroundtheworld.go_usage: 主干写 Homes Around the World,分支:Igloo (ice/snow) / Bamboo house (warm places) / Tent (moving people) / Stilt house (coastal) / Skyscraper (big cities)

3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.title: The Great Animal Hide-and-Seek
3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.teaching_focus: Practice the present continuous with simple animals and places, use prepositions of place (on, in, behind, under), and make short spoken sentences by changing the subject, action and location.
3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.What puzzle?(Animal puzzle) 2.Where fox hide?(Behind big tree) 3.Goat standing on?(Red roof) 4.Hippo in pool or grass?(In pool) 5.Where zebra?(On crosswalk)Reading Skill: 1.Find animal under something.(Frog under rug) 2.Fox in front or behind tree?(Behind) 3.Zebra standing where?(On crosswalk)Reading Strategy: 1.Can find all animals?(Yes) 2.Animal under rug?(Yes,frog) 3.Finish puzzle?(Yes)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What Tommy doing? 2.(Inference)Zebra real or puzzle? 3.(Vocabulary)What "crosswalk"? 4.(Feelings)How feel at end? 5.(Connection)Like puzzles?
3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Goat standing where?(On red roof) 2.(On)Find animal under green rug.(Frog) 3.(On)Fox in front or behind tree?(Behind) 4.(On)Zebra standing where?(On crosswalk) 5.(Above)Zebra real or puzzle?(A puzzle piece)
3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.go_description: 以场景地图呈现位置与方位关系的视觉图形组织器
3::thegreatanimalhideandseek.go_usage: 画一棵树、一座房子，根据文本标注：fox (behind tree), goat (on roof)

3::animalsallaroundus.title: Animals All Around Us
3::animalsallaroundus.teaching_focus: Practice 'There is' for existence, short adjectives, -ing forms to say actions, and simple place words (on, in, near). Use substitution drills: change the adjective, noun, action or location for speaking and listening practice.
3::animalsallaroundus.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Where look and listen?(Outside) 2.Little bird doing?(Singing) 3.Squirrel color?(Gray) 4.Yellow bee doing?(Humming) 5.Caterpillar eating?(Leaves)Reading Skill: 1.Animal in yard?(Big dog) 2.Animal on floor?(Cute cat) 3.Two animals in trees.(Bird,squirrel)Reading Strategy: 1.Dog sound?(Bark) 2.Caterpillar sound?(Crunch,crunch) 3.Bee near flower:What color?(Yellow)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)Where animals live? 2.(Inference)Why watch with care? 3.(Vocabulary)What "purring"? 4.(Fact)Squirrel running or sleeping? 5.(Connection)Animals near your house?
3::animalsallaroundus.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Squirrel color?(Gray) 2.(On)Animal in yard?(Big dog) 3.(On)Caterpillar eating sound?(Crunch,crunch) 4.(On)Yellow bee doing?(Humming near flowers) 5.(Above)Why watch with care?(To be kind/not hurt them)
3::animalsallaroundus.graphic_organizer: Animal Observation Log (动物观察记录)
3::animalsallaroundus.go_description: 按动物类别整理行为与声音的记录型图形组织器
3::animalsallaroundus.go_usage: 按动物分类，记录它们的声音（bark, pur, hum）和动作（singing, running）

3::thehauntedattic.title: The Haunted Attic
3::thehauntedattic.teaching_focus: Practice past 'was' + adjective to express feelings, use 'scared to' + verb to show hesitation, swap subjects and verbs for speaking drills, and short oral sentences about fears and actions.
3::thehauntedattic.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'attic' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thehauntedattic.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thehauntedattic.go_description: 呈现故事四要素的线性图形组织器
3::thehauntedattic.go_usage: 填写 Characters（Tommy, Mia, cat）、Setting（attic）、Problem（strange noise / Tommy scared）、Solution（tiny lost cat found）

3::theforestolympics.title: The Forest Olympics
3::theforestolympics.teaching_focus: Practice plural subject + verb (they search), the preposition of place 'in', and place vocabulary. Good for substitution drills, short speaking answers, and simple yes/no or wh- questions (Do they search in the trees? Where do they search?).
3::theforestolympics.core_vocab_raw: finishing tape, excited, search, fall asleep
3::theforestolympics.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Why animals excited?(Forest race day) 2.Who hops in race?(Rabbit) 3.Turtle fast or slow?(Slow but brave) 4.What missing?(Race ribbon) 5.Who has ribbon?(Raccoon)Reading Skill: 1.First:search ribbon or laugh?(Search) 2.Search first where?(In trees) 3.Last search place?(Bushes/Raccoon)Reading Strategy: 1.Why race not start?(Missing ribbon) 2.Raccoon in race?(No,asleep) 3.Why everyone laugh?(Raccoon asleep with ribbon)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)Animals do when ribbon lost? 2.(Inference)Raccoon mean to take ribbon? 3.(Vocabulary)What "ribbon"? 4.(Feelings)How feel about raccoon? 5.(Connection)Lose something found funny place?
3::theforestolympics.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Who hops in race?(Rabbit) 2.(On)What missing before race?(Race ribbon) 3.(On)First:search or laugh?(Search) 4.(On)Where find ribbon?(With raccoon) 5.(Above)Raccoon mean to take ribbon?(No,he just fell asleep)
3::theforestolympics.go_description: 以链条环节呈现故事发展先后顺序的线性图形组织器
3::theforestolympics.go_usage: 记录故事发展：Race start -> Ribbon lost -> Search trees/grass -> Find raccoon

3::animalhomesandhabits.title: Animal Homes and Habits
3::animalhomesandhabits.teaching_focus: Practice subject + simple present verb (lives), use of 'a' with singular nouns, naming animals and places, substitution drill (change the animal and home), speaking questions: 'Where does the ___ live?' and short answers.
3::animalhomesandhabits.reading_skill: 匹配关系 (Matching Animal to Home)
3::animalhomesandhabits.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Where fox live?(In den) 2.Bird lay in nest?(Eggs) 3.Bee make?(Honey) 4.Beaver build?(Dams) 5.Frog eat?(Small insects)Reading Skill: 1.Match beaver→?(Lodge) 2.Match bee→?(Hive) 3.Match frog→?(Pond)Reading Strategy: 1.Seen bird nest?What made of? 2.Ants small or big home?(Big/thousands) 3.Why animals need homes?(Sleep/be safe)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)All animals same home? 2.(Inference)Why ant colony big home? 3.(Vocabulary)What "lodge"? 4.(Fact)Frog live where? 5.(Connection)Your home like?
3::animalhomesandhabits.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Where fox live?(In den) 2.(On)Match beaver→?(Lodge/Dams) 3.(On)Bird lay in nest?(Eggs) 4.(On)Bee make?(Honey) 5.(Above)Why ant colony big home?(Thousands of ants)
3::animalhomesandhabits.go_description: 呈现动物与其栖息地对应匹配关系的表格型图形组织器
3::animalhomesandhabits.go_usage: 连线或列表格：Fox-Den, Bird-Nest, Bee-Hive, Beaver-Lodge

3::howhousesarebuilt.title: How Houses Are Built
3::howhousesarebuilt.teaching_focus: Practice the plural subject 'We' with simple present verbs, show sequence with 'step by step', and swap different verbs and objects for speaking and substitution drills.
3::howhousesarebuilt.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. What do we build first?



2. What do walls make inside the house?



3. What does the roof keep out?



4.  What do doors let in?



5. What do windows let in?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'foundation' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::howhousesarebuilt.rr_questions:
1. (On-Level) What do we build first when we make a house?



2. (On-Level) Why do we add a roof to the house?



3. (On-Level) What do windows do for the house?



4. (Below-Level) Name two materials we use to build walls.



5. (Above-Level) Why does the text say that building a house takes many months?
3::howhousesarebuilt.go_description: 以链条结构呈现建造步骤的线性图形组织器
3::howhousesarebuilt.go_usage: 节点依次写:Foundation → Walls → Roof → Doors & Windows → Paint → Move in

3::thefeatherinthewind.title: The Feather in the Wind
3::thefeatherinthewind.teaching_focus: Practice present simple for he/she/it (adds -s), using 'to' for direction, quick substitution of nouns, and speaking drills (change the subject or object to make new sentences).
3::thefeatherinthewind.core_vocab_raw: feather, blow, reach for, ask ... for help
3::thefeatherinthewind.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Where small squirrel?(In park) 2.What see in grass?(A feather) 3.Wind blow feather first?(Into tree) 4.Feather fall on whose nose?(Monkey's) 5.Feather fly end where?(Into pond)Reading Skill: 1.Order:1.Tree 2.Monkey 3.Pond 2.Run to pond before or after monkey sneeze?(After) 3.Who help squirrel at end?(Bird)Reading Strategy: 1.Squirrel jump into pond?(No) 2.Monkey keep feather?(No,sneeze) 3.Bird give feather back?(Yes)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)Squirrel trying to do? 2.(Inference)Why ask bird for help? 3.(Vocabulary)What "suddenly" mean? 4.(Feelings)How feel feather in pond? 5.(Connection)Do if see beautiful feather?
3::thefeatherinthewind.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Where small squirrel?(In park) 2.(On)Order feather trip:Monkey,Tree,Pond.(1.Tree,2.Monkey,3.Pond) 3.(On)Squirrel jump into pond?(No) 4.(On)Feather fall on whose nose?(Monkey's) 5.(Above)Why ask bird for help?(Bird can fly/squirrel can't swim)
3::thefeatherinthewind.go_description: 以路线轨迹呈现事物移动过程的视觉图形组织器
3::thefeatherinthewind.go_usage: 画出羽毛飘过的路线：Grass -> Tree -> Monkey's nose -> Pond

3::mammals.title: Mammals
3::mammals.teaching_focus: Practice 'There are' for saying that groups exist, add short verb phrases (eat, live, fly), and swap the noun or action to make new sentences for speaking and substitution drills.
3::mammals.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Name 3 mammal places.(Forests,deserts,cities) 2.What some mammals eat?(Plants or meat) 3.Which mammal can fly?(Bats) 4.Which fit in one hand?(Mouse) 5.Mammals care babies?(Yes)Reading Skill: 1.Categorize whale→(swim),bat→(fly) 2.Categorize mouse→(small),elephant→(big) 3.Categorize groups or alone?(Both)Reading Strategy: 1.Many mammals?(Yes) 2.Book about eat or live?(How they live) 3.Elephants small?(No,tons)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What special mammals? 2.(Inference)Why care babies help? 3.(Vocabulary)What "weighs several tons"? 4.(Fact)Mammals live in desert? 5.(Connection)Humans mammals?
3::mammals.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Which fit in one hand?(Mouse) 2.(On)Categorize whale→(swim),bat→(fly) 3.(On)Mammals care babies?(Yes) 4.(On)Book about eat or live?(How they live) 5.(Above)Why care babies help live well?(Babies safe and grow)
3::mammals.go_description: 用交叠圆圈呈现事物异同的对比型图形组织器
3::mammals.go_usage: 比较两类哺乳动物（如 Bats vs. Whales）或 Mammals in Groups vs. Alone

3::morningchaos.title: Morning Chaos
3::morningchaos.teaching_focus: Practice negation with can't + verb, use of possessive adjectives (my/your/his/her/our), simple object vocabulary, and substitution for speaking drills (change subject or object).
3::morningchaos.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'backpack' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::morningchaos.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::morningchaos.go_description: 按时间顺序呈现早晨事件的线性图形组织器
3::morningchaos.go_usage: 从左到右标注:Wake up → Can't find homework → Can't find socks → Can't find backpack → Hear bus → Forget lunchbox → Jump on bus

3::savethesilverfish.title: Save the Silver Fish
3::savethesilverfish.teaching_focus: Practice common movement verbs and prepositions of place/direction (to, in, through, into). Students can swap subjects, verbs and places, and ask/answer 'Where?' questions with short spoken sentences.
3::savethesilverfish.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Cause and Effect)
3::savethesilverfish.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Who is Carlos?(Silver fish) 2.Who friend?(Mandy turtle) 3.Why Carlos scared?(Big heron comes) 4.Where Carlos hide?(In leaves) 5.Who dig tunnel?(Young lizard)Reading Skill: 1.Why Mandy worried?(Friend scared) 2.Why lizard dig tunnel?(Help Carlos to pond) 3.Effect swim tunnel?(Carlos safe)Reading Strategy: 1.Carlos good hider?(No,cannot hide well) 2.Special about lizard?(Wears tiny hat) 3.Heron friend or danger?(Danger)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)How Carlos safe? 2.(Inference)Mandy good friend? 3.(Vocabulary)What "heron"? 4.(Feelings)How feel at end? 5.(Connection)How help friends?
3::savethesilverfish.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Who is Carlos?(Silver fish) 2.(On)Why Carlos scared?(Big heron comes) 3.(On)Carlos hide well?(False) 4.(On)Who dig tunnel to pond?(Young lizard) 5.(Above)How Carlos safe?(Friends Mandy and lizard helped)
3::savethesilverfish.go_description: 呈现原因 — 行为 — 结果逻辑关系的流程图形组织器
3::savethesilverfish.go_usage: 左边写 Cause (Heron comes)，中间写 Action (Lizard digs tunnel)，右边写 Effect (Carlos is safe)

3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.title: Reptiles: Cold-Blooded Lives
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.teaching_focus: Practice using the plural subject with the verb are, making simple descriptions and definitions, and substituting different noun or adjective phrases to talk about facts (drill: What are reptiles? They are ...).
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.phonics_rule: the vowel e in closed syllables
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Reptiles blood kind?(Cold blood) 2.What cover reptile body?(Scales) 3.Name 3 reptiles.(Snakes,lizards,turtles) 4.Lizard tail do?(Drop and regrow) 5.Turtle live how long?(Eighty years)Reading Skill: 1.Fact:reptiles live land and water?(Yes) 2.All reptiles lay eggs inside?(No) 3.Reptiles warm or cold cool weather?(Cold)Reading Strategy: 1.Why reptiles have scales?(Protect) 2.How lizard run fast desert? 3.Surprising about lizard tail?Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What reptiles like? 2.(Inference)Cold outside:active? 3.(Vocabulary)What "regrow" mean? 4.(Gist)Reptiles special? 5.(Connection)Touched turtle or lizard?
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Reptiles blood kind?(Cold blood) 2.(On)Fact:live land and in water?(Yes) 3.(On)Name 3 reptiles.(Snakes,lizards,turtles) 4.(On)Surprising lizard tail?(Drop and regrow) 5.(Above)Cold outside:reptile active?(No,cold/slow)
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.go_description: 中心为主题、向外扩展关键事实的放射状图形组织器
3::reptilescoldbloodedlives.go_usage: 中心写 Reptiles，周围写特征：Cold blood, Scales, Lay eggs

3::staystrongandhealthy.title: Stay Strong and Healthy
3::staystrongandhealthy.teaching_focus: Practice giving simple advice and reasons using the imperative plus a short benefit phrase. Students can swap verbs and objects (Eat/Drink/Wash + food/water/hands) and make speaking drills: say the action, then add 'for' + a short noun (vitamins, energy, health).
3::staystrongandhealthy.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
3::staystrongandhealthy.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'healthy' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::staystrongandhealthy.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::staystrongandhealthy.go_description: 以主旨为中心,向外辐射支撑细节的放射状图形组织器
3::staystrongandhealthy.go_usage: 中心写 Strong & Healthy,分支写:Vegetables & fruit / Protein & grains / Dairy / Water / Exercise / Sleep / Hand-washing

3::echofun.title: Echo Fun
3::echofun.teaching_focus: Practice saying sentences about sounds, swap different sound words, focus on the verb 'hear' with plural subject 'they', and simple word order (Subject + verb + object).
3::echofun.core_vocab_raw: woods, echo, hold one's hand, surprised
3::echofun.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.Where Tommy and Mia walking?(In woods) 2.Where stop?(At cave) 3.Mia hold hand with?(Dad) 4.What say "hello" hear?(Hello back) 5.Sound bounce off?(The rock)Reading Skill: 1.What call back names?(Echo) 2.First sound inside?(Small sound) 3.Who explain echo?(Dad)Reading Strategy: 1.Imagine dark cave:how feel? 2.Draw sound bounce off rock. 3.How look clap and play?(Laughing)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)What echo? 2.(Inference)Why hold Dad's hand? 3.(Vocabulary)What "bounce off" mean? 4.(Feelings)How feel at end? 5.(Connection)Heard echo?Where?
3::echofun.rr_questions: 1.(Under)Where stop?(At cave) 2.(On)What call back names?(Echo) 3.(On)Sound bounce off?(Rock) 4.(On)Imagine dark cave:how feel?(Visualizing) 5.(Above)Why hold Dad's hand?(A little scared)
3::echofun.go_description: 以气泡形式整理各类感官信息的图形组织器
3::echofun.go_usage: 记录在山洞里的感官：Hear (Echo), Feel (Hold hand), See (Dark cave)

3::howwelearnabouttheworld.title: How We Learn About the World
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.teaching_focus: Practice body-part words, simple verbs for senses, and the structure 'We use our ___ to ___'. Use substitution drills, speaking repetition, and simple yes/no or question forms (Do we use our nose to smell?).
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.core_vocab_raw: five senses, tongue, bitter, stay safe
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.reading_skill: 匹配器官与感官 (Matching Organ to Sense)
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.questions_type: Reading for Details: 1.How many senses?(Five) 2.Eyes see colors?(One million) 3.Nose for?(Smell food and flowers) 4.Skin feel?(Hot and cold) 4.Tongue 4 tastes.(Sweet,salty,sour,bitter)Reading Skill: 1.Match ears→(Hear sounds) 2.Match tongue→(Taste flavors) 3.Match skin→(Feel)Reading Strategy: 1.Sweet food you like? 2.Loud sound at school? 3.Senses help stay safe?(Smell fire/smoke)Other Comprehension: 1.(Main Idea)How learn world? 2.(Inference)See everything dark? 3.(Vocabulary)What "bitter" taste? 4.(Fact)Senses work every day? 5.(Connection)Most important sense today?
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.rr_questions: 1.(Under)How many senses?(Five) 2.(On)Match ears→(Hear sounds) 3.(On)Tongue 4 tastes.(Sweet,salty,sour,bitter) 4.(On)Senses help stay safe?(Smell fire/smoke) 5.(Above)See everything dark?Why?(No,eyes need light)
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.graphic_organizer: Five Senses Tree Map (五感树状图)
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.go_description: 以五大分支呈现五感与功能的树状层级图形组织器
3::howwelearnabouttheworld.go_usage: 五个分支对应五个器官，并标注功能：Eyes-See, Ears-Hear, Tongue-Taste 等

3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.title: The Boy Who Never Brushed His Teeth
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.teaching_focus: Practice starting a sentence with a time phrase ('That night') and using past simple 'dreamed of' to describe dreams or imaginations; students can swap subjects and objects to make new sentences and speak about their own dreams.
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Cause and Effect)
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'dentist' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.go_description: 以两列对应格式呈现行为与结果的图形组织器
3::theboywhoneverbrushedhisteeth.go_usage: 左列写 Cause（ate lots of candy / never brushed / bad dream）,右列写 Effect（teeth turned black / dentist drilled / Tommy woke up and brushed）

3::ourfivesensesatwork.title: Our Five Senses at Work
3::ourfivesensesatwork.teaching_focus: 练习 I can + 动词 + 名词；感官动词的使用（see, hear, smell等）。
3::ourfivesensesatwork.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where are Tommy and Mia with their family? (At the park) • What color is the kite Tommy sees? (Red) • Who hears a bird tweet near the trees? (Mia) • What can Mom smell from the blanket? (Warm bread) • What does the dog want to do? (Taste yummy food) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Which sense does Tommy use to see the kite? (Sight/Eyes) • (Skill) Does Dad use his ears or his hands to feel the grass? (Hands/Touch) • (Skill) Match: Smell -> ? (Warm bread) • (Strategy-Visualizing) Close your eyes. What sound does a "tweet" make? • (Strategy-Visualizing) Draw the picnic blanket. What is on it? • (Strategy-Visualizing) How does the "soft grass" feel in your mind? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What are they doing in the park? (Using senses during a picnic) • (Inference) Is the dog hungry? (Yes, he wants to taste the food) • (Vocabulary) What does "yummy" mean? (Tastes good) • (Feelings) How does the family feel at the end? (Happy/Laughing) • (Connection) What is one thing you can smell in your kitchen?
3::ourfivesensesatwork.rr_questions: • (Under) What color is the kite? (Red) • (On) Who hears the bird tweet? (Mia) • (On) Match: Dad -> (Touch soft grass). • (On) Can Tommy see a kite or a plane? (A kite) • (Above) Why does everyone laugh at the dog? (Because he "says" he wants to eat all the food)
3::ourfivesensesatwork.go_description: 中心为主旨、向外辐射五种感官细节的放射状图形组织器。
3::ourfivesensesatwork.go_usage: 中心写“Picnic”，五个分支分别画出或写下文中提到的事物：See (Red kite), Hear (Bird tweet), Smell (Warm bread), Touch (Soft grass), Taste (Yummy food)。

3::usingoursenses.title: Using Our Senses
3::usingoursenses.teaching_focus: 介词 with 的用法；身体部位词汇；描述触觉。
3::usingoursenses.reading_skill: 器官功能匹配 (Matching Organ to Function)
3::usingoursenses.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• How many senses do we use to know the world? (Five) • What can you see with your eyes? (Colors) • Can you hear music with your nose? (No, with ears) • What can you feel with your skin? (A warm hug) • Name one thing that feels "bumpy". (A rock) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What part of the body do you use to taste fruit? (Tongue) • (Skill) Is skin used for hearing or feeling? (Feeling) • (Skill) Which body part helps you stay safe from a hot stove? (Skin/Touch) • (Strategy-Connecting) What is something "fuzzy" you have at home? • (Strategy-Connecting) When did you last give someone a "warm hug"? • (Strategy-Connecting) Do your senses help you at school? How? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) Why are our senses important? (They help us know the world and stay safe) • (Inference) If you have a cold and cannot smell, will food taste the same? (No) • (Vocabulary) What does "bumpy" mean? (Not smooth) • (Fact) Is it a fact that all senses work together? (Yes) • (Connection) Which sense do you use most when playing a game?
3::usingoursenses.rr_questions: • (Under) What can you see with eyes? (Colors) • (On) Match: Tongue -> (Taste sweet fruit). • (On) How many senses are in the book? (Five) • (On) Can you feel a "bumpy" rock with your ears? (No, with skin) • (Above) How do senses help you "stay safe"? (e.g., Seeing a car or feeling something hot)
3::usingoursenses.go_description: 呈现事物结构（身体部位）与其功能（感官）对应关系的双列表格型图形组织器。
3::usingoursenses.go_usage: 左列记录 Senses (See, Hear, Smell, Taste, Feel)；右列对应 Body Parts (Eyes, Ears, Nose, Tongue, Skin)。

3::whysleepmatters.title: Why Sleep Matters
3::whysleepmatters.teaching_focus: Practice the structure 'Sleep helps your ___ ___' with simple nouns and verbs. Focus on the word 'helps', the possessive 'your', subject-verb order, and making new sentences by changing the noun (body part) and the verb (action). Use it for speaking and substitution drills.
3::whysleepmatters.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
3::whysleepmatters.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'energy' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::whysleepmatters.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::whysleepmatters.go_description: 以主旨为中心,向外辐射睡眠好处的放射状图形组织器
3::whysleepmatters.go_usage: 中心写 Why Sleep Matters,分支写:energy / body growth / brain learning / memory / healthy / calm & happy

3::themagickitesfourseasons.title: The Magic Kite's Four Seasons
3::themagickitesfourseasons.teaching_focus: 季节词汇；方位/路径介词（into, through, over）；描述天气。
3::themagickitesfourseasons.core_vocab_raw: land, puddle, fly over, fly though
3::themagickitesfourseasons.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• What does Mia have? (A magic kite) • Where does the kite land in spring? (A small puddle) • Where does the kite fly in summer? (Over the beach) • What color are the leaves in fall? (Red and orange) • What does the kite fly through in winter? (White snow) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What season comes after summer in the book? (Fall) • (Skill) Does the kite see red leaves before or after the snow? (Before) • (Skill) Which season has "cool wind"? (Fall) • (Strategy-Predicting) Look at the title. Will the kite stay in the house? (No) • (Strategy-Predicting) If it is spring, what will the children wear? (Raincoats) • (Strategy-Predicting) Does the kite like to travel? (Yes, it sees everything) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What does the magic kite see? (The changes of four seasons) • (Inference) Why is the kite "magic"? (It can fly all year/sees everything) • (Vocabulary) What is a "puddle"? (A small pool of water on the ground) • (Feelings) Does Mia like her kite? (Yes, she loves it) • (Connection) What would you like a magic kite to see?
3::themagickitesfourseasons.rr_questions: • (Under) What color is the snow? (White) • (On) What season has red and orange leaves? (Fall) • (On) Put in order: 1. Summer beach 2. Winter snow 3. Spring puddle. • (On) Do children swim in winter or summer? (Summer) • (Above) Why do the children wear raincoats in spring? (Because the kite flies into puddles/it rains)
3::themagickitesfourseasons.go_description: 以圆形循环结构呈现季节或时段变化的图形组织器。
3::themagickitesfourseasons.go_usage: 在圆环的四个象限标注 Spring, Summer, Fall, Winter，并根据文本写下风筝在每个季节看到的代表性场景（如 Puddle, Beach, Red leaves, Snow）。

3::whywehaveseasons.title: Why We Have Seasons
3::whywehaveseasons.teaching_focus: because 引导的原因状语从句；地球公转与季节的关系。
3::whywehaveseasons.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Cause and Effect)
3::whywehaveseasons.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• What does the Earth move around? (The sun) • Does the Earth stand straight or have a tilt? (Has a tilt) • What does the part tilting toward the sun get? (Light and heat) • Which season is the part tilted away from the sun in? (Winter) • How does the Earth move? (Around and around) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Why is it summer in one part of Earth? (It tilts toward the sun) • (Skill) What is the effect of being far from the sun? (It is cold/winter) • (Skill) What causes the seasons to change? (Earth moving around the sun) • (Strategy-Questioning) Ask a question about the Earth's "tilt". • (Strategy-Questioning) Why do we need light and heat? • (Strategy-Questioning) Does the sun move or does the Earth move? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What causes seasons? (Earth’s tilt and movement around the sun) • (Inference) If Earth didn't tilt, would we have seasons? (Probably not) • (Vocabulary) What does "tilt" mean? (To lean to one side) • (Fact) "The Earth moves in a big circle." Is this a fact? (Yes) • (Connection) Is it summer or winter for you right now?
3::whywehaveseasons.rr_questions: • (Under) What gives Earth heat? (The sun) • (On) Is winter when we are near or far from the sun's direct light? (Far) • (On) The Earth moves in a ______. (Circle) • (On) One part gets lots of light. Is that summer or winter? (Summer) • (Above) Explain why seasons change. (Because the Earth moves and has a tilt)
3::whywehaveseasons.go_description: 呈现“原因—行为—结果”逻辑关系的流程图形组织器。
3::whywehaveseasons.go_usage: 左框写 Cause (Earth tilts toward sun)；中框写 Action (Gets lots of light)；右框写 Effect (Summer)。

3::fromgardentotable.title: From Garden to Table
3::fromgardentotable.teaching_focus: Practice using help + verb to talk about actions and cooperation; students can try different subjects and objects and notice forms (help/helped/helps) and the base verb after help.
3::fromgardentotable.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'carefully' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::fromgardentotable.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::fromgardentotable.go_description: 以链条结构呈现食材从园中到餐桌过程的图形组织器
3::fromgardentotable.go_usage: 节点:Garden (pick tomatoes, carrots, beans) → Wash → Cut → Cook (soup & salad) → Eat together

3::journeytotheblueocean.title: Journey to the Blue Ocean
3::journeytotheblueocean.teaching_focus: 练习 see + 名词 + that 引导的定语从句；海洋生物词汇。
3::journeytotheblueocean.reading_skill: 识别动物行为 (Identifying Animal Actions)
3::journeytotheblueocean.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where are Tommy and Mia? (On a boat in the ocean) • What are the dolphins doing? (Jumping over waves) • Do the whales sing or dance? (Sing songs) • Where do the sea turtles swim to? (To the sand) • Where do small fish hide? (In the bright coral) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Which animal is the biggest in the story? (Whales) • (Skill) Match: Dolphins -> (Jump over waves). • (Skill) What action do the turtles do? (Swim) • (Strategy-Visualizing) Imagine a "bright coral". What colors do you see? • (Strategy-Visualizing) How does the song of a big whale sound in your mind? • (Strategy-Visualizing) Draw Tommy and Mia on the boat. Are they happy? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What is the ocean like? (A big home for many animals) • (Inference) Why do small fish hide in the coral? (To be safe/hide) • (Vocabulary) What is "coral"? (A home for fish in the ocean) • (Feelings) How does Tommy feel about the ocean? (He loves it) • (Connection) Would you like to go on a boat? What animal do you want to see?
3::journeytotheblueocean.rr_questions: • (Under) What color is the ocean? (Blue) • (On) Who jumps over waves? (Dolphins) • (On) Where do the sea turtles go? (To the sand) • (On) Small fish hide in the ______. (Coral) • (Above) Why does Mia say the ocean is "like a big home"? (Because many animals live there together)
3::journeytotheblueocean.go_description: 中心为主体、向外展示多个角色及其对应动作的放射状图形组织器。
3::journeytotheblueocean.go_usage: 中心写“Ocean”，周围分支写出动物及其动作：Dolphins-jump, Whales-sing, Sea turtles-swim, Small fish-hide。

3::animalsinthedark.title: Animals in the Dark
3::animalsinthedark.teaching_focus: 动词不定式 to do 表示目的；夜行动物特征描述。
3::animalsinthedark.reading_skill: 识别身体特征功能 (Identifying Functions)
3::animalsinthedark.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• When are some animals active? (At night) • What do owls have to see in the dark? (Big eyes) • Where do bats live? (In dark caves) • What do raccoons use to find food? (Strong paws) • When do these animals sleep? (When it is light) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What is the function of an owl's big eyes? (To see in the dark) • (Skill) What do bats use sound for? (To hunt for bugs) • (Skill) Raccoons use their paws to ______. (Find food) • (Strategy-Questioning) Ask a question about how bats hunt. • (Strategy-Questioning) Why is night "busy" for these animals? • (Strategy-Questioning) Can we see as well as an owl at night? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What are nocturnal animals? (Animals active at night with special ways to move) • (Inference) Are bats quiet or loud when they use sound to hunt? (Probably make sounds/echoes) • (Vocabulary) What does "active" mean? (Moving and doing things) • (Fact) "Bats live in dark caves." Is this a fact? (Yes) • (Connection) Do you ever wake up when it is dark? What do you hear?
3::animalsinthedark.rr_questions: • (Under) When do owls see? (At night) • (On) Raccoons have strong ______. (Paws) • (On) Bats use sound to hunt for ______. (Bugs) • (On) True or False: These animals sleep when it is dark. (False) • (Above) Why is night a "busy time" for an owl? (Because they hunt and move when we sleep)
3::animalsinthedark.go_description: 呈现事物组成部分及其特定功能的表格型图形组织器。
3::animalsinthedark.go_usage: 对应记录夜行动物的身体秘密：Owls-Big eyes-See in dark; Bats-Sound-Hunt; Raccoons-Strong paws-Find food。

3::fromfarmtofork.title: From Farm to Fork
3::fromfarmtofork.teaching_focus: Practice using the prepositions from...to..., simple place words, and subject-verb agreement (travel/travels). Pupils can swap words to make new sentences.
3::fromfarmtofork.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'crop' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::fromfarmtofork.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::fromfarmtofork.go_description: 以箭头连接各环节的线性流程图形组织器
3::fromfarmtofork.go_usage: Farm (plant seeds) → Grow crops → Harvest → Factory (wash & pack) → Shops & markets → Home (cook & eat)

3::thelittleseedsbigdream.title: The Little Seed’s Big Dream
3::thelittleseedsbigdream.teaching_focus: 序列词 First, Then, Finally；植物生长过程动词。
3::thelittleseedsbigdream.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where does the small seed sleep? (In the dirt) • What do the roots drink? (Water) • What color is the sprout? (Green) • What grows after the sprout? (Strong stem and leaves) • What is at the top of the plant at the end? (A yellow flower) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What happens first: a flower grows or a root grows? (Root) • (Skill) Put in order: 1. Sprout 2. Seed 3. Flower. (2-1-3) • (Skill) What does the sprout reach for? (The sun) • (Strategy-Predicting) Look at Page 1. Will the seed stay in the dirt forever? (No) • (Strategy-Predicting) Mia waters the seed. What will happen? (It will grow) • (Strategy-Predicting) The plant has a flower. Will it be beautiful? (Yes) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How does a seed change? (It grows into a beautiful plant) • (Inference) Why does the sprout reach for the sun? (Plants need sun to grow) • (Vocabulary) What is a "stem"? (The middle part of a plant that holds it up) • (Feelings) How does the little seed feel at the end? (Happy) • (Connection) Have you ever planted a seed? What was it?
3::thelittleseedsbigdream.rr_questions: • (Under) What color is the flower? (Yellow) • (On) What do the roots do? (Drink water) • (On) Does the seed grow roots or leaves first? (Roots) • (On) The sprout is ______. (Green) • (Above) Why is the little seed now a "beautiful plant"? (Because it grew roots, a stem, and a flower)
3::thelittleseedsbigdream.go_description: 以链条环节呈现事物生长或故事发展先后顺序的线性图形组织器。
3::thelittleseedsbigdream.go_usage: 记录植物生长的五个阶段：Seed -> Roots -> Sprout -> Stem/Leaves -> Flower。

3::howwegrowandchange.title: How We Grow and Change
3::howwegrowandchange.teaching_focus: when 引导的时间状语从句；过去时 (was/were)；成长变化。
3::howwegrowandchange.reading_skill: 比较过去与现在 (Compare and Contrast)
3::howwegrowandchange.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Do all living things change? (Yes) • What could you do when you were a baby? (Crawl and cry) • What did you get after being a baby? (First teeth) • What did you learn as a toddler? (Walk and talk) • Who are you now? (A student) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Contrast: Baby (Crawl) vs. Student (?). (Strong muscles/Learn) • (Skill) When did you learn to eat? (After getting first teeth) • (Skill) What part of your body is stronger now? (Muscles) • (Strategy-Connecting) Can you still crawl? Do you still cry like a baby? • (Strategy-Connecting) What is one thing you can do now that a baby cannot? • (Strategy-Connecting) Will you be taller next year? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How do people grow? (They change from babies to students and get stronger) • (Inference) Why do students have stronger muscles than babies? (They walk, run, and grow) • (Vocabulary) What is a "toddler"? (A young child who is learning to walk) • (Fact) "You will grow even more as you get older." Is this true? (Yes) • (Connection) Look at a photo of yourself as a baby. What is different?
3::howwegrowandchange.rr_questions: • (Under) What could a baby do? (Crawl and cry) • (On) As a toddler, you learned to ______ and ______. (Walk and talk) • (On) You are a ______ now. (Student) • (On) True or False: You got your teeth before you could crawl. (False) • (Above) How will you change when you are older? (I will grow even more/get stronger)
3::howwegrowandchange.go_description: 两列对比结构，用于呈现同一主体在不同阶段或状态下异同的表格型组织器。
3::howwegrowandchange.go_usage: 左列标题为“When I was a baby” (crawl, cry)；右列标题为“Now I am a student” (walk, talk, stronger muscles)。

3::thebestsandwichever.title: The Best Sandwich Ever
3::thebestsandwichever.teaching_focus: Practice subject + verb agreement with 'puts' (he/she/name + puts), naming food items, using the preposition 'on' and building short, repeatable sentences. Use substitution drills (change the person, the thing, or the place) and simple questions: 'Who puts ___?' 'What does she put on the ___?'
3::thebestsandwichever.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'pickle' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thebestsandwichever.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thebestsandwichever.go_description: 以链条结构呈现三明治制作过程的图形组织器
3::thebestsandwichever.go_usage: 节点:Bread on plate → Ham & cheese → Pickles & jam → Banana & lettuce → Mia tries → Best sandwich ever!

3::tommysbusycityday.title: Tommy's Busy City Day
3::tommysbusycityday.teaching_focus: 简单过去时（went, saw, fed, ate）；城市地名及活动。
3::tommysbusycityday.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where did Tommy go with his parents? (New York) • What time did they arrive? (In the morning) • What did they see first? (Tall buildings) • What did Tommy feed in Central Park? (Ducks) • What did they eat in the small cafe? (Pizza) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Scan the text. Find the place with "old paintings". (Museums) • (Skill) How did they get to the Statue of Liberty? (By ferry) • (Skill) Where was Tommy at the very end of the day? (In bed/Asleep) • (Strategy-Skimming) Look at the names of places. Is this city busy or quiet? (Busy) • (Strategy-Skimming) Is the book about a farm or a city? (A city) • (Strategy-Skimming) Find the word "tired". Who feels that way? (Tommy) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What did Tommy do in New York? (Visited many places and had a busy day) • (Inference) Why did Tommy fall asleep so quickly? (He was very tired from walking) • (Vocabulary) What is a "ferry"? (A boat that moves people across water) • (Feelings) Was the trip fun for Tommy? (Yes) • (Connection) If you go to a big city, what do you want to see?
3::tommysbusycityday.rr_questions: • (Under) What did Tommy eat? (Pizza) • (On) Where did they see old paintings? (Museums) • (On) Did they go to Central Park before or after the ferry? (Before) • (On) Tommy saw ______ buildings. (Tall) • (Above) Why was Tommy's day "busy"? (Because he went to museums, a park, a ferry, and a cafe in one trip)
3::tommysbusycityday.go_description: 以路线轨迹呈现人物在不同地点移动过程的视觉图形组织器。
3::tommysbusycityday.go_usage: 画出一条城市街道线，依次标注 Tommy 游览的地点：Tall buildings -> Museums -> Central Park -> Ferry -> Cafe。

3::howacityworks.title: How a City Works
3::howacityworks.teaching_focus: There are + 名词 + that 定语从句；城市系统词汇。
3::howacityworks.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• How many people do some cities have? (Several million) • What move people fast in a city? (Buses, trains, and taxis) • What do pipes bring to the city? (Clean water) • What carry electricity? (Wires) • What do garbage trucks do? (Take away garbage) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What is the function of city wires? (To carry electricity) • (Skill) What system helps keep the city clean? (Garbage trucks) • (Skill) Offices are usually in ______ buildings. (Tall) • (Strategy-Questioning) Ask a question about the pipes under the street. • (Strategy-Questioning) Why do we need trains and taxis? • (Strategy-Questioning) How does a city "run"? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How does a city work? (Many systems like transport and water work together) • (Inference) If the electricity wires broke, what would happen? (Lights and machines would stop) • (Vocabulary) What are "pipes"? (Tubes that move water or gas) • (Fact) Cities have offices and buildings. (Yes) • (Connection) How do you get around in your city?
3::howacityworks.rr_questions: • (Under) What move people fast? (Buses/Trains/Taxis) • (On) Pipes bring ______ water. (Clean) • (On) What use electricity? (Lights and machines) • (On) Wires carry ______. (Electricity) • (Above) What happens if the garbage trucks stop working? (The city gets dirty/garbage stays)
3::howacityworks.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
3::howacityworks.go_description: 中心为主旨、向外辐射支撑细节的放射状图形组织器。
3::howacityworks.go_usage: 中心写“How a City Works”，分支列出四大系统及其功能：Transport (Buses/Trains), Water (Pipes), Electricity (Wires), Garbage (Trucks)。

3::wheredoesitcomefrom.title: Where Does It Come From?
3::wheredoesitcomefrom.teaching_focus: Students can practice food vocabulary and sources, ask and answer 'Where does X come from?', use substitution to make many sentences, and notice the preposition 'from' and the simple present form 'comes'.
3::wheredoesitcomefrom.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'wheat' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::wheredoesitcomefrom.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::wheredoesitcomefrom.go_description: 以食物-来源对应形式呈现信息的表格型图形组织器
3::wheredoesitcomefrom.go_usage: 两列对应:Food (milk / eggs / bread / ham / fish) ↔ Source (cows / hens / wheat / pigs / ocean or river)

3::thetoyswakeup.title: The Toys Wake Up!
3::thetoyswakeup.teaching_focus: 练习主+谓+地点；单数主语谓语加-s；常用方位介词。
3::thetoyswakeup.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where are the toys at the start? (On the shelf) • What does the little car do? (Rolls off and zooms) • Where does the doll spin in a circle? (On the rug) • Where does the kite glide? (Above the open book) • Who calls Tommy? (Mia) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Which toy "bounces"? (The toys/They bounce down stairs) • (Skill) Name two toys that move on wheels. (Car, etc.) • (Skill) Is the doll spinning on the floor or the rug? (Rug) • (Strategy-Visualizing) Imagine the toys having a party. What sound do they make? • (Strategy-Visualizing) Draw the kite gliding over a book. • (Strategy-Visualizing) How does "confetti" look in the air? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What happened in Mia's dream? (The toys escaped and played) • (Inference) Was this a real story? (No, it was a dream) • (Vocabulary) What does "glide" mean? (To move smoothly) • (Feelings) How did Mia feel when she woke up? (Surprised/Sneezing) • (Connection) If your toys could move, what would they do?
3::thetoyswakeup.rr_questions: • (Under) Where does the doll spin? (On the rug) • (On) What does the little car do? (Rolls off and zooms) • (On) The kite glides ______ the open book. (Above) • (On) Did Mia see the toys before or after she woke up? (Before/In dream) • (Above) How do we know it was a dream? (Mia woke up at the end/Toys threw confetti)
3::thetoyswakeup.go_description: 以树干树枝结构收集并归类特定主题词汇（如动作动词）的图形组织器。
3::thetoyswakeup.go_usage: 在“树枝”上收集文中所有描述玩具动作的词：rolls off, zooms, spins, glides, bounces。

3::thescienceofmovingthings.title: The Science of Moving Things
3::thescienceofmovingthings.teaching_focus: 练习 A + noun + moves + object + place 事实陈述句。
3::thescienceofmovingthings.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• What are the three ways things move? (Push, pull, and roll) • What moves a toy car across the floor? (A push) • What moves a wagon toward you? (A pull) • What help things roll smoothly? (Wheels) • Where does a box move far with a push? (On smooth ice) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Categorize: Wagon -> (Pull). Ball -> (Roll). • (Skill) Which way needs a rope to be easier? (Pull) • (Skill) Does a heavy load move better with or without a wheel? (With a wheel) • (Strategy-Connecting) When do you "push" something at school? (e.g., a chair) • (Strategy-Connecting) Have you ever pulled a wagon? Was it heavy? • (Strategy-Connecting) What happens if you try to roll a square box? (It won't work well) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What are the ways to move things? (Push, pull, and roll use different forces) • (Inference) Why is ice good for pushing a box? (It is smooth/less friction) • (Vocabulary) What is a "load"? (A heavy thing to carry) • (Fact) "A wheel moves a heavy load with less force." Is this true? (Yes) • (Connection) Try pushing a toy car on a rug and on a table. Which is faster?
3::thescienceofmovingthings.rr_questions: • (Under) A ______ moves a ball down a ramp. (Roll) • (On) What helps you pull more easily? (A rope) • (On) A wagon moves ______ you when you pull it. (Toward) • (On) Which is fastest: Push, Pull, or Roll? (Depends on the test/Roll) • (Above) Why do wheels make moving heavy things easier? (Because they roll instead of slide)
3::thescienceofmovingthings.go_description: 按类别分支呈现信息层级关系的图形组织器。
3::thescienceofmovingthings.go_usage: 顶层写“Ways Things Move”，下层分出 Push, Pull, Roll 三类，并在每类下方写出对应的物体示例（如 Toy car, Wagon, Ball）。

3::thetalentshow.title: The Talent Show
3::thetalentshow.teaching_focus: Practice saying intentions with 'wants to' and a verb; use different people and actions to build speaking confidence. Focus on subject + 'wants' (third person) and the infinitive 'to + verb' for simple substitution drills and short roleplays.
3::thetalentshow.reading_skill: 识别人物情感变化 (Story Elements: Emotion)
3::thetalentshow.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'trick' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thetalentshow.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thetalentshow.go_description: 按故事进程呈现人物情感变化的线性图形组织器
3::thetalentshow.go_usage: 节点:Before show (nervous) → Practice (determined) → On stage (ready) → After (proud)

3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.title: Tommy at the Whirly-Twirly Fair
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.teaching_focus: 使用 first/then/finally 排列动作；练习讲述简单故事。
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.core_vocab_raw: take a deep breath, dizzy, Ferris wheel, juggle
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Who is at the fair with Tommy? (His family) • What is the clown doing? (Waving and juggling oranges) • What ride does Tommy try first? (The big wheel) • What does Tommy hold when he is on the Whirly-Twirly? (The bar) • How does Tommy feel on the fast ride? (Dizzy) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Put in order: 1. Whirly-Twirly 2. Big wheel 3. Small car. (2-3-1) • (Skill) What did Tommy do before he tried the fast ride? (Took a deep breath) • (Skill) What happened "finally"? (Tommy tried the Whirly-Twirly) • (Strategy-Monitoring) Why was Tommy scared at first? (The rides looked scary/fast) • (Strategy-Monitoring) Did Tommy like the ride at the end? (Yes, he rode again) • (Strategy-Monitoring) Is 9:30 mentioned? (No, monitoring text details) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How did Tommy enjoy the fair? (He tried different rides and overcame his fear) • (Inference) Why did he take a deep breath? (He was brave but a little nervous) • (Vocabulary) What does "dizzy" mean? (Feeling like the room is spinning) • (Feelings) Is Tommy happy at the end? (Yes, rides with joy) • (Connection) What is your favorite ride at a park?
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.rr_questions: • (Under) What did the clown juggle? (Oranges) • (On) What ride did Tommy try first? (The big wheel) • (On) Tommy felt ______ on the fast ride. (Dizzy) • (On) Did he ride the Whirly-Twirly before or after the big wheel? (After) • (Above) Why did Tommy ride again at the end? (Because he found it fun)
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.go_description: 以时间为轴线，按先后顺序呈现事件序列的线性图形组织器。
3::tommyatthewhirlytwirlyfair.go_usage: 使用 First, Then, Finally 标注 Tommy 玩游乐项目的顺序：Big wheel -> Small car -> Whirly-Twirly。

3::howridesmove.title: How Rides Move
3::howridesmove.teaching_focus: 练习 There is/are，单复数及 that 引导的简单定语从句。
3::howridesmove.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where do rides take people? (Up and down at parks) • What rides use wheels on the ground? (Bumper cars) • What guide coaster and train cars? (Tracks) • What do chains and belts do? (Lift people high/pull cars up hills) • What does a motor do? (Makes parts turn) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What is the function of gravity in a park? (Pulls rides down the hills) • (Skill) What do workers check to keep people safe? (Motors and brakes) • (Skill) Tracks keep cars on the ______. (Path) • (Strategy-Questioning) Ask a question about bumper cars. • (Strategy-Questioning) How does a motor help a ride? • (Strategy-Questioning) Why do we need brakes? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How do park rides move? (Using wheels, tracks, motors, and gravity) • (Inference) Can a roller coaster move without gravity? (It needs gravity to go down) • (Vocabulary) What are "brakes"? (Parts that stop a machine) • (Fact) "Tracks guide coaster cars." Is this a fact? (Yes) • (Connection) Which ride in the book sounds the most exciting?
3::howridesmove.rr_questions: • (Under) What pulls rides down hills? (Gravity) • (On) What do bumper cars use? (Wheels) • (On) What do workers check? (Motors and brakes) • (On) Chains pull cars ______ hills. (Up) • (Above) Why do riders "smile after the ride"? (Because they feel safe and had fun)
3::howridesmove.go_description: 呈现机器或系统各组成部分与其对应作用的图形组织器。
3::howridesmove.go_usage: 对应记录：Wheels (Roll on ground), Tracks (Guide cars), Motor (Make parts turn), Gravity (Pull down hills)。

3::trysomethingnew.title: Try Something New!
3::trysomethingnew.teaching_focus: Practice 'can' for ability/possibility, substitute different verbs and nouns to name hobbies, make questions (Can you ...?) and short answers (Yes, I can./No, I can't.), and use with people or places (with friends, in a park).
3::trysomethingnew.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'hobby' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::trysomethingnew.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::trysomethingnew.go_description: 以主题为中心,向外辐射不同爱好种类的圆形图形组织器
3::trysomethingnew.go_usage: 中心写 Hobbies,分支写:sports / music / drawing & painting / reading / collecting things

3::apostcardfromfaraway.title: A Postcard from far away
3::apostcardfromfaraway.teaching_focus: 区分 There is/are 单复数；添加形容词和介词短语描述。
3::apostcardfromfaraway.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Who sent the postcard? (Grandma) • Which country is she in? (Egypt) • What is in the desert? (A tall pyramid) • What is the funny camel wearing? (A red hat) • Who helped Grandma get her map back? (A kind boy) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Scan for the river's name. What is it?
