(The Nile) • (Skill) Are there amazing museums in Cairo or London? (Cairo) • (Skill) What color is the sun in the sky? (Golden) • (Strategy-Predicting) Look at the postcard. Who is it for? (Mia) • (Strategy-Predicting) Grandma says "I will bring you a surprise". What could it be? • (Strategy-Predicting) Will Grandma have more fun? (Yes, she is traveling) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What is Grandma doing in Egypt? (Visiting landmarks and traveling down the Nile) • (Inference) Was Grandma's map lost forever? (No, the boy helped her) • (Vocabulary) What is a "pyramid"? (A tall stone building in Egypt) • (Feelings) Does Grandma miss Mia? (Yes, she says "I miss you") • (Connection) If you could send a postcard, where would it be from?
3::apostcardfromfaraway.rr_questions: • (Under) Where is Grandma? (Egypt) • (On) What does the camel wear? (A red hat) • (On) Where are the amazing museums? (In Cairo) • (On) Grandma is on a boat on the ______. (Nile) • (Above) Why did Grandma need help in the market? (Because her map blew away)
3::apostcardfromfaraway.graphic_organizer: Postcard Concept Map (明信片概念图)
3::apostcardfromfaraway.go_description: 模拟明信片布局，整合视觉图像（图片）与关键细节（文字）的组织器。
3::apostcardfromfaraway.go_usage: 在明信片“图片区”画出 Egypt 的代表物（Pyramid, Camel, Nile），在“文字区”写下 Grandma 的经历。

3::allkindsofhomes.title: All Kinds of Homes
3::allkindsofhomes.teaching_focus: 练习 There are + 名词 + that + 动词短语；学习基础从句。
3::allkindsofhomes.core_vocab_raw: cabin, cottage, be made of, float
3::allkindsofhomes.questions_type:
(Skill - Contrast) How is a cabin in the mountains different from an apartment in a tall building?



(Skill - Compare) What do floating homes and tiny homes on wheels have in common?



(Skill - Compare) How are a cottage and a cabin similar?



(Skill - Classify) Which homes are movable and which are fixed in one place?



(Strategy - Skimming) Look at the title or first sentence. Is this text about building a home or different kinds of homes?



(Strategy - Skimming) Look at the text quickly. Are all homes the same or are they different?



(Strategy - Scanning) Find the word "cozy" in the text. What home does it describe?



(Strategy - Using Pictures) If there were pictures, would you see a home on water? (Yes)



(Strategy - Predicting) Before reading, what do you think the text will be about? (Different homes)
3::allkindsofhomes.rr_questions:
1. (On-Level) What materials are some homes made of?



2. (On-Level) Where can small cottages be found?



3. (On-Level) What kind of home floats on water like a boat?



4. (Below-Level) Name one type of home mentioned in the text.



5. (Above-Level) How does the text show that homes can be very different from each other?
3::allkindsofhomes.go_description: 通过交叠的圆圈展示不同事物之间共同点与独特点的对比型图形组织器。
3::allkindsofhomes.go_usage: 比较 Tents (Moveable) 和 Brick houses (Permanent)，交叠处写下它们的共同目标：Keep people safe and warm。

3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.title: The Soccer Star's Big Game
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.teaching_focus: Practice subject + verb + to + person. Focus on third-person 's' (he/she/it passes), using 'to' for giving or sending, making short action sentences, and simple substitution drills for speaking and role play.
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.reading_skill: 识别问题与解决 (Problem and Solution)
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'cheer' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.go_description: 以两格结构呈现故事冲突与解决方法的图形组织器
3::thesoccerstarsbiggame.go_usage: 左格写 Problem（Tommy slipped / other team scored / felt sad）,右格写 Solution（Mia encouraged / Tommy kept going / passed to Ben / team won）

3::greetingsaroundtheworld.title: Greetings Around the World
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.teaching_focus: 练习问候语、语言名称及对应动作；词汇替换练习。
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.reading_skill: 动作与语言匹配 (Matching Action to Language)
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Who came to school today? (A new child) • How did she look at first? (Sad and quiet) • What did Aimee say? ("Hello") • What did Tangtang say? ("Ni hao") • How did Minji wave? (With both hands) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Match: Spanish -> ("Hola"). • (Skill) Match: Japanese -> (Bowed softly). • (Skill) What action did Juan do? (Big wave and a wink) • (Strategy-Connecting) How do you say "hello" to your friends? • (Strategy-Connecting) Have you ever been the "new child" in a group? • (Strategy-Connecting) Can you say "Ni hao"? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How did the children make the new girl feel welcome? (By using greetings from different languages) • (Inference) Did the new girl feel better at the end? (Yes, she smiled and felt happy) • (Vocabulary) What is a "wink"? (To close one eye quickly) • (Feelings) Why was the girl sad at first? (Maybe she was lonely/new) • (Connection) What is one greeting you know in another language?
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.rr_questions: • (Under) What did Juan say? ("Hola") • (On) Who said "Salam"? (Ali) • (On) Match: Korean -> ("Annyeong"). • (On) Did the girl smile at the start or the end? (At the end) • (Above) Why did the children teach her different greetings? (To make her feel welcome and happy)
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.go_description: 以行列结构呈现不同文化背景下语言与动作匹配关系的表格型图形组织器。
3::greetingsaroundtheworld.go_usage: 列表对应：Spanish (Hola - big wave), Chinese (Ni hao - smile), Japanese (Konnichiwa - bow)。

3::foodaroundtheworld.title: Food Around the World
3::foodaroundtheworld.teaching_focus: 练习 There be 结构及主谓一致；掌握基础方位介词。
3::foodaroundtheworld.core_vocab_raw: dish, corn, noodle soup, be served with
3::foodaroundtheworld.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• How much of the world eats rice? (More than half) • Is bread always soft? (No, can be crusty) • Where does corn come from? (The Americas) • What food is quick to cook? (Noodles) • What is fish often served with? (Rice and vegetables) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Categorize: Rice -> (Asia and Africa). Corn -> (Americas). • (Skill) Which food feeds animals too? (Corn) • (Skill) Where do people eat a lot of fish? (Near rivers and coasts) • (Strategy-Prior Knowledge) What color is corn? (Yellow/White) • (Strategy-Prior Knowledge) Have you eaten noodles? What was in them? • (Strategy-Prior Knowledge) Why do people use spices? (To make food taste better/hot) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) How does food connect people? (People eat different dishes but share stories through food) • (Inference) Is rice important for the world? (Yes, half the world eats it) • (Vocabulary) What does "crusty" mean? (Hard on the outside) • (Fact) Noodles are quick to cook. (Yes) • (Connection) What is your favorite dish from another country?
3::foodaroundtheworld.rr_questions: • (Under) Where is corn from? (The Americas) • (On) More than ______ the world eats rice. (Half) • (On) Match: Fish -> (Rivers and coasts). • (On) Bread can be ______ or ______. (Soft or crusty) • (Above) Why does the author say "food connects people"? (Because food helps people share stories)
3::foodaroundtheworld.graphic_organizer: World Food Map (全球美食分类图)
3::foodaroundtheworld.go_description: 按地理区域或食材类别整理信息的层级式图形组织器。
3::foodaroundtheworld.go_usage: 将食物按地区分类：Asia/Africa (Rice), Americas (Corn), Coasts (Fish)。

3::sportsaregoodforyou.title: Sports Are Good for You!
3::sportsaregoodforyou.teaching_focus: Practice using an activity as the subject, correct verb form (help vs helps), and 'your' + a body part or ability. Students can swap activities and benefits to make many short sentences, ask questions (What helps your ___?), and use the form in speaking and writing.
3::sportsaregoodforyou.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
3::sportsaregoodforyou.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'confidence' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::sportsaregoodforyou.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::sportsaregoodforyou.go_description: 以主旨为中心,向外辐射运动好处的放射状图形组织器
3::sportsaregoodforyou.go_usage: 中心写 Benefits of Sports,分支:strong heart / muscle growth / balance & speed / teamwork / confidence / brain focus

3::thegingerbreadman.title: The Gingerbread Man
3::thegingerbreadman.teaching_focus: 练习主语代词、现在时动词单三形式及介词 past。
3::thegingerbreadman.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Who bakes the gingerbread man? (Grandma) • Where does he jump from? (The tray) • Name two animals he runs past. (Cow and horse) • Why does he stop at the river? (He cannot swim well) • Who says "I can help you"? (The fox) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Put in order: 1. River 2. Tray 3. Cow. (2-3-1) • (Skill) Does he run past the horse before or after the cow? (After) • (Skill) What happens when the fox opens his mouth? (The gingerbread man is gone) • (Strategy-Predicting) Grandma opens the oven. What will happen? (The man runs away) • (Strategy-Predicting) The fox looks hungry. Is he a real friend? (No) • (Strategy-Predicting) Will the gingerbread man get home? (No) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What is the story about? (A gingerbread man who runs away but gets tricked by a fox) • (Inference) Was the fox being kind? (No, he wanted to eat him) • (Vocabulary) What is a "tray"? (A flat thing used for baking) • (Feelings) How did the gingerbread man feel when he was on the fox's back? (Happy/Safe - at first) • (Connection) What is a "trick" you know from another story?
3::thegingerbreadman.rr_questions: • (Under) Who made the gingerbread man? (Grandma) • (On) What was the problem at the river? (He could not swim) • (On) The gingerbread man said, "Catch me if you ______!" (Can) • (On) Who ate the gingerbread man? (The fox) • (Above) Why did the fox tell the gingerbread man to jump on his back? (To get him into the middle of the river and eat him)
3::thegingerbreadman.go_description: 记录故事主要情节及发展先后顺序的线性图形组织器。
3::thegingerbreadman.go_usage: 记录逃跑路线：Grandma's Tray -> Cow -> Horse -> River -> Fox's back。

3::folktales.title: Folk Tales
3::folktales.teaching_focus: 练习复数主语的一般现在时，学习制作事实陈述短句。
3::folktales.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details: • How are they passed? (From voice to voice) • What do folk tales often teach? (A small lesson) • What makes a folk tale fun? (A surprise or trick) • Can one tale have many versions? (Yes, ten or more) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) What is the main idea of folk tales? (Stories that travel and teach lessons) • (Skill) Do folk tales change or stay the same? (They change) • (Skill) What do stories keep alive? (Culture) • (Strategy-Questioning) Ask a question about "versions" of a story. • (Strategy-Questioning) Why do people change the words? • (Strategy-Questioning) What is a "lesson" in a story? 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What are folk tales? (Ancient stories passed down that change over time) • (Inference) Why can a story "grow like a tree"? (People add new details) • (Vocabulary) What is a "version"? (A different way of telling the same story) • (Fact) Some travel across two or more countries. (Yes) • (Connection) Do you know a story with a "trick" in it?
3::folktales.rr_questions: • (Under) Folk tales teach a small ______. (Lesson) • (On) How are folk tales passed? (By voice) • (On) One tale can have ______ or more versions. (Ten) • (On) Do children still listen to them? (Yes) • (Above) Why do folk tales change when they travel to new countries? (Because people add local details)
3::folktales.go_description: 中心为核心主题、向外扩展其关键事实特征的放射状图形组织器。
3::folktales.go_usage: 中心写“Folk Tales”，周围写下它们的共同点：Teach lessons, Travel far, Change words, Keep culture alive。

3::officerbucklesbusyday.title: Officer Buckle's Busy Day
3::officerbucklesbusyday.teaching_focus: Practice subject + verb + object + place in pastt simple (note 'spotted' for he/she). Use it to teach common place words (behind, under, on, in, near). Activities: substitution drills, picture prompts (Where is the ___?), and short role plays.
3::officerbucklesbusyday.reading_skill: 识别问题与解决 (Problem and Solution)
3::officerbucklesbusyday.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'safety day' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::officerbucklesbusyday.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::officerbucklesbusyday.go_description: 以两格结构呈现故事冲突与解决方法的图形组织器
3::officerbucklesbusyday.go_usage: 左格写 Problem（small dog lost / has red collar）;右格写 Solution（Mia found dog behind tree / owner called / girl reunited with dog）

3::goldilocksandthethreebears.title: Goldilocks and the Three Bears
3::goldilocksandthethreebears.teaching_focus: 练习现在进行时描述近期动作，注意缩略形式。
3::goldilocksandthethreebears.questions_type: 1. Reading for Details:• Where did Goldilocks walk? (In the forest) • How many bowls were on the table? (Three) • Which bowl was "just right"? (The baby bowl) • Which chair was too soft? (The small chair) • Where did the bears find Goldilocks? (In the baby's bed) 2. Reading Skill & Strategy:• (Skill) Match: Big bowl -> (Too hot). Small bowl -> (Too cold). • (Skill) Put in order: 1. Chairs 2. Bowls 3. Beds. (2-1-3) • (Skill) What did Baby Bear say first? ("Someone's been eating my porridge") • (Strategy-Monitoring) Does Goldilocks live in the wooden house? (No, she saw it and went in) • (Strategy-Monitoring) Why did she run away? (She was surprised/scared by the bears) • (Strategy-Monitoring) Is the story "just right" at the end? (No, she runs away) 3. Other Comprehension:• (Main Idea) What did Goldilocks do in the bears' house? (She tried their food, chairs, and beds) • (Inference) Was Goldilocks invited into the house? (No, she opened the door quietly) • (Vocabulary) What is "porridge"? (A hot cereal/food in a bowl) • (Feelings) How did the three bears feel when they came home? (Surprised/Upset) • (Connection) If you were a bear, what would you say to Goldilocks?
3::goldilocksandthethreebears.rr_questions: • (Under) How many bears are in the story? (Three) • (On) Which bed was Goldilocks sleeping in? (The baby bed) • (On) Match: Big chair -> (Too hard). • (On) Did the bears find her in the kitchen or bedroom? (Bedroom) • (Above) Why did Goldilocks try the baby's things last? (Because the big and small ones were not right for her)
3::goldilocksandthethreebears.go_description: 多维度比较不同主体（三只熊）对同一类物品不同偏好的表格型图形组织器。
3::goldilocksandthethreebears.go_usage: 纵轴列出 Papa Bear, Mama Bear, Baby Bear；横轴列出 Bowls, Chairs, Beds；格内填入对应特征 (Hot/Cold, Hard/Soft, Just right)。

3::theantandthegrasshopper.title: The Ant and the Grasshopper
3::theantandthegrasshopper.teaching_focus: Practice making cause–result sentences, use 'so' with a comma, and substitute different causes and results.
3::theantandthegrasshopper.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Cause and Effect)
3::theantandthegrasshopper.questions_type: Details, Skill, Strategy, Other, Assessment (共21题)
3::theantandthegrasshopper.rr_questions: 1. (Under) Does the grasshopper play a fiddle or a drum? 2. (On) Why is the ant busy all day? 3. (On) What was the grasshopper's mistake? 4. (On) Did the ant say "No" to the grasshopper? 5. (Above) Why is it important to "plan for winter" like the ant?
3::theantandthegrasshopper.go_description: 展示“原因—行为—结果”的逻辑链条
3::theantandthegrasshopper.go_usage: 左框写 Cause (Winter comes)；中框写 Action (Ant shares soup)；右框写 Effect (They are happy)

3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.title: Helping Hands in Our Neighborhood
3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.teaching_focus: Practice subject + keep/keeps + a person or people (object), and simple extra information. Focus on subject-verb agreement (keep vs. keeps) and swapping subjects and objects for speaking practice.
3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'sick' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.go_description: 以职业-职责对应形式呈现社区帮手信息的表格型图形组织器
3::helpinghandsinourneighborhood.go_usage: 两列:Helper (police / firefighters / doctors / cleaning crews / volunteers) ↔ Job (keep safe / put out fires / help sick / keep tidy / share time)

3::miaandherspanishfriend.title: Mia and Her Spanish Friend
3::miaandherspanishfriend.teaching_focus: Practice using the sentence pattern "..., then ..." to describe actions in order.
3::miaandherspanishfriend.core_vocab_raw: lively, suggest, seafood, as good as
3::miaandherspanishfriend.reading_skill: 识别文化细节 (Identifying Cultural Details)
3::miaandherspanishfriend.questions_type: Details, Skill, Strategy, Other, Assessment (共21题)
3::miaandherspanishfriend.rr_questions: 1. (Under) What country is Lucia from? 2. (On) What does Lucia miss from home? 3. (On) What do the girls play together? 4. (On) Is the seafood paella good or bad? 5. (Above) Why did Mia choose a Spanish restaurant for Lucia?
3::miaandherspanishfriend.go_description: 用于比较 Lucia 家乡与 Mia 所在地的异同
3::miaandherspanishfriend.go_usage: 左侧记录 Lucia's Spain；右侧记录 Mia's Home (Guitar music, Spanish restaurant)

3::discoveringcanada.title: Discovering Canada
3::discoveringcanada.teaching_focus: Practice saying 'There are more than...' with plural nouns and a place: forming plural nouns, using numbers.
3::discoveringcanada.core_vocab_raw: country, time zone, official language, second largest
3::discoveringcanada.reading_skill: 数据与事实检索 (Scanning for Data)
3::discoveringcanada.questions_type: Details, Skill, Strategy, Other, Assessment (共21题)
3::discoveringcanada.rr_questions: 1. (Under) Is Canada a big country? 2. (On) Name one animal that lives in Canada's forests. 3. (On) Does Canada have more or fewer lakes than other countries? 4. (On) What can visitors see in Canada? 5. (Above) Why does the book say "Canada welcomes everyone"?
3::discoveringcanada.go_description: 中心为主旨、向外辐射关键事实的放射状图形
3::discoveringcanada.go_usage: 中心写“Canada”，分支连出：Languages, Lakes, Oceans, Animals

3::thecleanupday.title: The Clean-Up Day
3::thecleanupday.teaching_focus: Practice the phrasal verb 'pick up', plural objects, and prepositions of place (from, near, by). Use substitution drills to build fluency: change the subject, objects, and places; practise short spoken sentences and simple questions (What do they pick up? Where do they pick it up?).
3::thecleanupday.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'trash' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::thecleanupday.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::thecleanupday.go_description: 以链条结构呈现公园清洁活动各步骤的线性图形组织器
3::thecleanupday.go_usage: 节点:Meet at park → Pick up bottles & paper → Pick up cans → Plant flowers → Neighbors join → Carry bags to bin → Park looks clean again

3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.title: The Llama Who Climbed to the Clouds
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.teaching_focus: Practice describing gradual change with 'smaller and smaller', use present tense observations.
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.core_vocab_raw: path, llama, steep, be proud of
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.reading_skill: 识别空间顺序 (Sequencing Spatial Order)
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.questions_type: Details, Skill, Strategy, Other, Assessment (共21题)
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.rr_questions: 1. (Under) What animal is Lina? 2. (On) Where are the Andes mountains? 3. (On) Why does Lina keep climbing? 4. (On) Does the boy help Lina or just talk to her? 5. (Above) Why do the houses look "smaller and smaller" to Lina?
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.go_description: 以路线轨迹呈现人物在不同高度移动过程的图形
3::thellamawhoclimbedtotheclouds.go_usage: 画上升的山路，标注：Bottom (Home) -> Middle (Steep path) -> Top (Soft clouds)

3::discoveringnewzealand.title: Discovering New Zealand
3::discoveringnewzealand.teaching_focus: Practice making short lists with 'There are' + plural nouns + 'for people to visit'; focus on pronunciation, commas and using 'and'.
3::discoveringnewzealand.questions_type: Details, Skill, Strategy, Other, Assessment (共21题)
3::discoveringnewzealand.rr_questions: 1. (Under) How many main islands are there? 2. (On) What is the native name for New Zealand? 3. (On) Name one language people speak there. 4. (On) Is New Zealand in the Atlantic or Pacific ocean? 5. (Above) Why is New Zealand called an "island country"?
3::discoveringnewzealand.go_description: 按类别分支呈现国家不同特征的层级式图形
3::discoveringnewzealand.go_usage: 顶层写“New Zealand”，下层分出：Islands, Nature, Animals

3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.title: Why Do We Have Libraries and Museums?
3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.teaching_focus: Practice using 'can' to talk about ability or possibility, substitute different verbs and objects, and make short answers and questions (Can I...? Yes, you can.).
3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.questions_type:
Reading for Details:
1. Who are the main characters?
2. Where does the story happen?
3. What is the problem?
4. How is the problem solved?

Comprehension:
1. (Inference) How did the character feel at the end? Why?
2. (Vocabulary) What does 'history' mean in the story?
3. (Connection) Has something like this happened to you?
4. (Opinion) What would you do in the character's place?
3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does the story take place?
2. (On) What problem does the main character have?
3. (On) How is the problem solved?
4. (On) How does the character feel at the end?
5. (Above) Why do you think the character made that choice?
3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.go_description: 以两个重叠圆圈呈现图书馆与博物馆异同点的对比型图形组织器
3::whydowehavelibrariesandmuseums.go_usage: 左圈 Library（borrow books / computers / maps & videos / events & crafts）,右圈 Museum（old bones, tools, art / history & culture / music & stories）,中间交集（help community / bring people to learn）

4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.title: Mia and Tommy Travel the World
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.teaching_focus: Practice using 'There is' to introduce people or places, add simple adjectives, and place them with prepositional phrases (in/on/over/with). Good for speaking, picture description, and substitution drills.
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What are Mia and Tommy looking at? (Travel photos from the year before)
• Name two landforms in the photos. (Snowy mountain, blue ocean, hot desert, rocky canyon, green valley)
• Which photo was missing? (The one from the Grand Canyon)
• Who has the missing photo? (Grandma)
• How did Mom send it? (By mail)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name three adjectives used to describe the landforms. (Tall snowy, blue, wide hot, deep rocky, green)
• Why was the photo missing? (Mom mailed it to Grandma)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Mia and Tommy look at travel photos and discover one is missing)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) How do you think Mia and Tommy felt when they found out where the photo was? • (Vocabulary) What does the word valley mean? • (Feelings) How did both children feel at the end? • (Connection) Have you ever lost a photo or a special thing? What happened?
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two landforms Mia and Tommy saw in the photos. (Mountain, ocean, desert, canyon, valley)
2. (On) Which photo was missing from the collection? (The one from the Grand Canyon)
3. (On) Who has the missing photo? (Grandma)
4. (On) How did Mom send the photo to Grandma? (By mail)
5. (Above) How do you think Mia and Tommy felt when they found out where the photo was? Why? (Surprised and happy, because they thought it was lost but it was safe with Grandma)
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.go_description: 中心写 Travel Photos，每个气泡写一个地形及其形容词：Snowy Mountain, Blue Ocean, Hot Desert, Rocky Canyon, Green Valley
4::miaandtommytraveltheworld.go_usage: 引导学生将文中用 'There is a [adj] [noun]' 描述的每张照片可视化，并从文本中提取形容词与地形名填入对应气泡

4::oceansriversandlakes.title: Oceans, Rivers, and Lakes
4::oceansriversandlakes.teaching_focus: Students can practice making simple definitions, swapping nouns to describe places, asking 'Where is X?' and answering with 'A. . . is where. . . meets. . .'. This builds speaking and sentence-substitution skills with basic vocabulary.
4::oceansriversandlakes.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• How much of Earth do oceans cover? (About seventy percent)
• What is a river? (A long, flowing stream)
• What is a coastline? (Where land meets water)
• What does a delta make? (Rich soil for plants)
• What must we do for bodies of water? (Protect them for the future)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name three bodies of water in the book. (Ocean, river, lake, coastline, delta)
• What is one difference between an ocean and a lake? (An ocean is very large; a lake is surrounded by land)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Bodies of water are important for life and must be protected)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think we must protect bodies of water? • (Vocabulary) What does protect mean? • (Fact) Name one thing rivers do for the Earth. • (Connection) Which body of water have you visited? What was it like?
4::oceansriversandlakes.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How much of Earth do oceans cover? (About seventy percent)
2. (On) What is a coastline? (Where land meets water)
3. (On) Name three bodies of water from the book. (Ocean, river, lake, coastline, delta)
4. (On) What does a delta make? (Rich soil for plants)
5. (Above) Why do you think the author says we must protect bodies of water? (Because all living things need water to survive)
4::oceansriversandlakes.go_description: 每格写一个水体名称（Ocean, River, Lake, Coastline, Delta），下方填入文中给出的定义句及特征
4::oceansriversandlakes.go_usage: 引导学生找出文本中 'A [noun] is. . .' 的定义句，理解各水体本质区别

4::thesubteacher.title: The Sub Teacher
4::thesubteacher.teaching_focus: Practice reporting speech and short dialogues using names or pronouns, quoting simple sentences, and expressing feelings and opinions.
4::thesubteacher.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thesubteacher.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "absent" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "substitute" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "strict" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::thesubteacher.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What is the name of the substitute teacher? (Mr. Bob Johnson)
2. (On) How did the class feel when he first walked in? (They were afraid — they thought he would be strict)
3. (On) What did Mr. Johnson use to teach math? (An old abacus)
4. (On) How did Tommy feel about the lesson by the end? (He loved it — he said it was the best math lesson)
5. (Above) What do you think this story teaches us about judging people before we know them? (Accept: we should not judge someone before we get to know them)
4::thesubteacher.go_description: 左列写 "Before"（担心/害怕严格），右列写 "After"（喜欢/欣赏），中间写 Mr. Johnson 的行为
4::thesubteacher.go_usage: 引导学生从文中找出情感变化的线索词（feared, wow, I like），对比同学们对代课老师看法的前后转变

4::thedesertfriends.title: The Desert Friends
4::thedesertfriends.teaching_focus: Practice past simple movement verbs (walked, climbed), the preposition 'over', using 'and' to join actions, and swapping place nouns. Good for substitution drills and spoken retelling of simple past events.
4::thedesertfriends.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thedesertfriends.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the two friends in the story? (A desert lizard and a camel)
• What do they need? (An oasis)
• What did they do to search? (Walked over dunes and climbed rocks)
• What did they find? (A green pool with palms)
• What did the camel do at the pool? (Used its hump to store water)

2. Reading Skill:
• Why did they leave to search? (Their water was low)
• How do you know they were tired? (The text says they searched under rocks and felt tired)
• How did the story end? (They found the oasis and rested in the shade together)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why is the camel's hump useful in the desert? • (Vocabulary) What does oasis mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the friends felt when they saw the green pool? • (Connection) Have you ever had to look hard for something you needed? What did you do?
4::thedesertfriends.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What do the lizard and camel need? (An oasis)
2. (On) What did they do to search for water? (Walked over dunes and climbed rocks)
3. (On) What did the camel use its hump for? (To store water)
4. (On) How do you know the friends were tired? (The text says they searched under rocks and felt tired)
5. (Above) How do you think the friends felt when they finally saw the green pool? Why? (Very happy and relieved, because they had been searching for a long time)
4::thedesertfriends.go_description: 左框写 Problem（Water is low / They need an oasis）；中框写 Actions（Walk over dunes, Climb rocks, Search）；右框写 Solution（Find a green pool, Use hump to store water, Rest in shade）
4::thedesertfriends.go_usage: 帮助学生理清故事的核心矛盾和解决过程，理解骆驼驼峰的生存意义

4::grandcanyon.title: Grand Canyon
4::grandcanyon.teaching_focus: Practice 'There are' for existence, using 'like' to give examples, and adding a location with 'at the. . .'. Good for substitution drills (change the noun, examples, or place), speaking short descriptions, and building plural vocabulary.
4::grandcanyon.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::grandcanyon.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• How long is the Grand Canyon? (About 277 miles)
• How deep is it? (About one mile)
• Name two animals that live there. (Deer, eagles, lizards, snakes)
• Name one plant found there. (Cactus or pine trees)
• What do the rock layers tell us? (Earth's history)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is special about the rock layers? (They tell Earth's history)
• Name two things visitors can do there. (Walk on trails, take photos, look at rock layers)
• What is the main idea of the book? (The Grand Canyon is a large, old, and beautiful natural place)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think we should help protect the canyon? • (Vocabulary) What does layer mean? • (Fact) Name one animal and one plant found at the Grand Canyon. • (Connection) Would you like to visit the Grand Canyon? What would you do there?
4::grandcanyon.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How long is the Grand Canyon? (About 277 miles)
2. (On) Name two animals that live at the Grand Canyon. (Deer, eagles, lizards, or snakes)
3. (On) What do the rock layers tell us? (Earth's history)
4. (On) What can visitors do at the Grand Canyon? (Walk on trails and take photos)
5. (Above) Why do you think the author asks readers to help protect the canyon? (Because it is a special natural place and needs to be kept safe for the future)
4::grandcanyon.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
4::grandcanyon.go_description: 中心写 The Grand Canyon，向外辐射四个分支：Rocks (layers, history), Plants (cactus, pine), Animals (sheep, condors), Visitors (trails, safe stops)
4::grandcanyon.go_usage: 引导学生在阅读前提问'大峡谷有什么特别之处'，阅读后将文中各类细节归入对应分支

4::howeducationchanged.title: How Education Changed
4::howeducationchanged.teaching_focus: Practice contrasting past and present using "In the past... but today..." to show how things have changed over time.
4::howeducationchanged.reading_skill: 识别变化与发展 (Identifying Change Over Time)
4::howeducationchanged.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "tutor" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "scroll" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "compulsory" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::howeducationchanged.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What did students write on before paper books were common? (A slate)
2. (On) Why did only rich children go to school in the past? (Poor families needed their children to work, and there were no free schools)
3. (On) What does "compulsory" mean in this text? (Something everyone must do by law — all children must attend school)
4. (On) Name one way schools today are different from schools in the past. (Accept: printed books / free for all / many grades in separate rooms / school buses)
5. (Above) Why do you think making school compulsory was an important change for society? (Accept: it gave all children, not just the rich, a chance to learn and succeed)
4::howeducationchanged.go_description: 左列标 "Schools in the Past"，右列标 "Schools Today"，各填3-4个关键对比点
4::howeducationchanged.go_usage: 用T-Chart帮助学生整理文章对比结构，强化"In the past... but today..."句型

4::thedesertanimalrescue.title: The Desert Animal Rescue
4::thedesertanimalrescue.teaching_focus: Practice placing a time clause at the sentence start ('Before leaving, . . .'), using the -ing form after 'before', and using past simple verbs with 'they' for talking about events in order.
4::thedesertanimalrescue.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What was wrong with the red fox? (It had a sore paw)
• What did Mia do to help? (Helped the staff clean its paw)
• What does a desert turtle store? (Water)
• What did the children do at the end? (Wrote notes about the animals they saw)
• What is special about the desert animal rescue? (It helps injured desert animals)

2. Reading Skill:
• Why did the turtle hide under a rock? (To stay cool)
• Name two animals the children saw. (A red fox, a lizard, a desert turtle)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Mia's class visits a desert animal rescue and learns how to help animals)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the children wrote notes about the animals? • (Vocabulary) What does rescue mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the children felt at the rescue? • (Connection) If you visited an animal rescue, which animal would you most want to help? Why?
4::thedesertanimalrescue.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What was wrong with the red fox? (It had a sore paw)
2. (On) What does a desert turtle store in its body? (Water)
3. (On) What did Mia do to help the fox? (Helped the staff clean its paw)
4. (On) What did the children do at the end of the visit? (Wrote notes about the animals they saw)
5. (Above) Why do you think the children wrote notes about the animals they saw? (To remember what they learned and show they cared about the animals)
4::thedesertanimalrescue.go_description: 三列对应：Person（Mia, Ben, Lina, Children）| Animal（Red fox, Lizard, Desert turtle, Thirsty animals）| Action（Clean paw, Observe, Found turtle, Gave water and food）
4::thedesertanimalrescue.go_usage: 帮助学生梳理故事中每个角色与动物的互动，理解救助行为的多样性

4::rainforestanimals.title: Rainforest Animals
4::rainforestanimals.teaching_focus: Practice using 'must' to show obligation and 'as it' to give a simple reason; short clauses in the present tense and subject-verb agreement with 'it'.
4::rainforestanimals.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where do millions of insects live? (In the trees and on the ground)
• What do tall tree roots do? (Hold the soil)
• Name one animal that uses tools. (Birds)
• What do capuchin monkeys do? (Use rocks to crack nuts)
• What must people do to protect the rainforest? (Stop cutting down trees)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two animals that live in the rainforest. (Insects, frogs, birds, monkeys)
• Is 'the rainforest is amazing' a fact or an opinion? (An opinion)
• What is the main idea of the book? (The rainforest is full of animals and plants that need to be protected)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why is it important to stop cutting down rainforest trees? • (Vocabulary) What does protect mean? • (Fact or Opinion) Is 'there are millions of insects in the rainforest' a fact or an opinion? • (Connection) Would you like to visit a rainforest? What would you most want to see?
4::rainforestanimals.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two animals that live in the rainforest. (Insects, frogs, birds, or monkeys)
2. (On) What do the roots of tall trees do for the rainforest? (Hold the soil)
3. (On) Is 'the rainforest is an amazing place' a fact or an opinion? (An opinion)
4. (On) What must people do to protect the rainforest? (Stop cutting down trees)
5. (Above) Why is it important to protect the rainforest? (Because many animals and plants live there and they help keep our planet healthy)
4::rainforestanimals.go_description: 左列写 Animal Facts（Insects in trees, Frogs jump on wet leaves, Birds use tools, Cats hunt at night, Ants grow fungus）；右列写 Why We Must Protect（Keeps animals and people healthy）
4::rainforestanimals.go_usage: 帮助学生区分文中的动物事实信息与作者的保护主张，理解非虚构文本的说明结构

4::thelibrary.title: The Library
4::thelibrary.teaching_focus: Practice using "can" to describe abilities and available actions at a community place, encouraging students to list activities they enjoy.
4::thelibrary.reading_skill: 识别地点与功能 (Identifying Place and Function)
4::thelibrary.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "borrow" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "shelf" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "librarian" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::thelibrary.rr_questions:
1. (Below) Why did Mia and Tommy go to the library? (They needed books for a science project)
2. (On) What did Ms. Lee show them to help find books? (How to search the catalog and sort by title)
3. (On) What does "overdue" mean? (A borrowed book that has not been returned on time)
4. (On) What happens when a book is overdue? (You must return it and may need to pay a small fine)
5. (Above) Why is it a useful skill to know how to use a library catalog? (Accept: it helps you find the right information quickly without needing to search every shelf)
4::thelibrary.go_description: 中心写 "Library"，向外放射写 5 个可以在图书馆做的活动
4::thelibrary.go_usage: 引导学生将文本中提到的图书馆功能与自己的生活经验联系，扩展可联想的活动词汇

4::asafariadventure.title: A Safari Adventure
4::asafariadventure.teaching_focus: Practice the pattern 'Mia saw' + noun phrase + 'that' + verb phrase to make simple past relative clauses; good for substitution drills and describing actions.
4::asafariadventure.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did Mia's family ride on the safari? (A small, open jeep)
• What did the giraffe eat? (Leaves from a high tree)
• What did the elephant do with its trunk? (Sprayed water)
• What did the lion do? (Sat in the sun and yawned)
• What happened to Tommy's hat? (The monkey took it)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name three animals Mia saw on the safari. (A giraffe, an elephant, a lion, a monkey)
• How do you know Tommy was surprised? (The monkey took his hat and he did not expect it)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Mia's family goes on a safari and sees many wild animals)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the monkey took Tommy's hat? • (Vocabulary) What does safari mean? • (Feelings) How did Mia feel during the safari? • (Connection) Which safari animal would you most like to see? Why?
4::asafariadventure.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did Mia's family ride on the safari? (A small, open jeep)
2. (On) What did the elephant do with its trunk? (Sprayed water)
3. (On) Name three animals Mia saw on the safari. (Giraffe, elephant, lion, monkey)
4. (On) What happened to Tommy's hat? (The monkey took it)
5. (Above) Why do you think the monkey took Tommy's hat? (It was curious or wanted to play with it)
4::asafariadventure.go_description: 填写四格：Characters（Mia, family, giraffe, elephant, lion, zebra）| Setting（Safari, open jeep）| Events（Saw animals → Wind blew hat → Zebra chased hat）| Ending（Mia was happy the zebra had fun）
4::asafariadventure.go_usage: 引导学生完整梳理故事的人物、场景、事件发展与结局，感受故事的幽默转折

4::oceananimals.title: Ocean Animals
4::oceananimals.teaching_focus: Students can practice the structure 'Some + plural noun + live in + place' to describe habitats. They can swap places and short descriptions (adjectives or prepositional phrases) to make new sentences and ask/answer where animals live.
4::oceananimals.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where do coral reef animals live? (In shallow reefs near the shore)
• Name one deep ocean animal. (A whale or a giant squid)
• Name one middle ocean animal. (A dolphin or a tuna)
• What do colorful fish do in coral reefs? (Live there)
• Name one ocean zone from the book. (Shallow reef, middle zone, or deep ocean)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two ocean zones where animals live. (Shallow reef, middle zone, deep ocean)
• Name one animal from each zone. (Reef: colorful fish; Middle: dolphin; Deep: whale)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Ocean animals live in different zones of the ocean)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do different animals live in different parts of the ocean? • (Vocabulary) What does zone mean? • (Fact) Name one animal and where it lives in the ocean. • (Connection) Which ocean animal do you think is the most interesting? Why?
4::oceananimals.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where do coral reef animals live? (In shallow reefs near the shore)
2. (On) Name two ocean zones where animals live. (Shallow reef, middle zone, deep ocean)
3. (On) Name one animal from the deep ocean. (A whale or a giant squid)
4. (On) What do colorful fish do in coral reefs? (Live there)
5. (Above) Why do you think different animals live in different parts of the ocean? (Because they need different amounts of light, temperature, and food)
4::oceananimals.go_description: 顶层写 Ocean Habitats，下方分出五个区域：Shallow Reefs（Corals, colorful fish）, Kelp Forests（Many fish）, Deep Water（Glowing fish）, Cold Poles（Seals, penguins）, Warm Equator（Turtles, tropical fish）
4::oceananimals.go_usage: 帮助学生按海洋区域整理动物信息，理解不同环境对应不同生物

4::thebestpetforyou.title: The Best Pet for You
4::thebestpetforyou.teaching_focus: Practice giving advice with "Before you get a [noun], think about..." — a decision-making structure that links a choice to its key requirements.
4::thebestpetforyou.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "leash" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "groom" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "veterinarian" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::thebestpetforyou.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What must every dog owner have for safe walks? (A strong leash)
2. (On) Why do long-haired cats need to be groomed every few days? (To keep their coat tidy and free of tangles)
3. (On) What does a veterinarian do for your pet once a year? (Gives it a health check and the right vaccinations)
4. (On) What is a microchip and why is it important? (A tiny chip under the skin that helps find your pet if it gets lost)
5. (Above) Which pet from the text do you think is best for a family with young children? Explain your choice. (Accept any pet with a clear reason)
4::thebestpetforyou.go_description: 5格步骤图：Step 1 Choose pet → Step 2 Buy supplies (leash/tank) → Step 3 Book a vet → Step 4 Get a microchip → Step 5 Build a daily routine
4::thebestpetforyou.go_usage: 把"养宠物"视为一个流程，用步骤图梳理顺序，为每步添加"Before you…think about…"理由

4::visitingscotland.title: Visiting Scotland
4::visitingscotland.teaching_focus: Practice past 'were' + adjective + to-infinitive to talk about feelings and discoveries; students can swap the final noun phrase to make new sentences and speak about things they saw.
4::visitingscotland.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:

• Where did Mia and Tommy go with their family? (They went to Scotland.)

• What stood beside the blue loch? (A tall stone castle.)

• What were men doing on the green hill? (They were playing bagpipes.)

• What did the cheeky sheep do? (It grabbed their paper map and ran away.)




2. Reading Skill:

• Name two Scottish traditional things they saw. (Men playing bagpipes, men dancing in kilts)

• How do you know local people were friendly? (A kind woman gave back their map and shared tea with them.)

• What is the main idea of the story? (Mia and Tommy travelled in Scotland and experienced local culture.)




3. Other Comprehension:

• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about?

(It tells about Mia and Tommy’s wonderful trip and warm experience in Scotland.)

• (Inference) How did they feel when they lost their map?

(They felt worried and tired.)

• (Vocabulary) What does the word "cheeky" mean?

(It means naughty.)

• (Feelings) How did they feel about this Scottish trip?

(They thought it was unforgettable and pleasant.)

• (Connection) Have you ever got help from a stranger? What happened?

(Yes, I once lost my pen and a stranger helped me find it.)
4::visitingscotland.rr_questions:
1.(Under) Where did the family go? (P2) ⭐⭐

(Scotland)

2.(On) What did the family see by the blue loch? (P3) ⭐⭐⭐

(Ancient stone castle)

3.(On) What clothes did the dancing man wear? (P5) ⭐⭐⭐

(Kilts)

4.(On) What did the woman do for them? (P7) ⭐⭐⭐

(Gave back the map and shared tea with them)

5.(Above) Why was their trip to Scotland unforgettable? (P8) ⭐⭐⭐

(Because they enjoyed Scottish culture and got warm help from locals)
4::visitingscotland.go_description: 左列写 Before（Family exploring Scotland: castle, bagpipes, kilts）；中间写 Unexpected Event（A cheeky sheep ate their map）；右列写 After（A kind woman found the map, shared tea, family had a memorable journey）
4::visitingscotland.go_usage: 帮助学生识别故事中的意外转折，理解善意帮助如何改变旅程走向

4::greatbritain.title: Great Britain
4::greatbritain.teaching_focus: Practice 'can' for possibility/ability + 'enjoy' + a noun phrase; students can swap activities and places to make new sentences and ask/answer simple questions (Can tourists enjoy. . . ?).
4::greatbritain.core_vocab_raw: capital, tourist, tradition, on the hour
4::greatbritain.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What can tourists ride in London? (A red double-decker bus)
• What is Big Ben? (A big clock)
• Name one famous museum in Great Britain. (The British Museum)
• What sport was invented in Great Britain? (Football)
• Name one famous castle. (Windsor Castle)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two famous things to see in London. (Big Ben, red bus, British Museum, Windsor Castle)
• What is Great Britain? (An island with old castles and famous museums)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Great Britain has many famous places and things to see)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think millions of tourists visit Great Britain every year? • (Vocabulary) What does famous mean? • (Fact) Name one famous place in Great Britain. • (Connection) Would you like to visit Great Britain? What would you most like to see?
4::greatbritain.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What can tourists ride in London? (A red double-decker bus)
2. (On) What is Big Ben? (A big clock in London)
3. (On) Name two famous things to see in Great Britain. (Red bus, Big Ben, British Museum, Windsor Castle)
4. (On) What sport was invented in Great Britain? (Football)
5. (Above) Why do you think millions of tourists visit Great Britain every year? (Because it has many famous places, history, and things to do)
4::greatbritain.go_description: 三栏标题：London | Wales | Scotland；各栏填入文中对应特色：London（Double-decker bus, Big Ben）, Wales（Green hills, sheep）, Scotland（Bagpipe music）
4::greatbritain.go_usage: 帮助学生快速定位文中各地区的关键信息，理解非虚构文本的地理分区结构

4::themysterydish.title: The Mystery Dish
4::themysterydish.teaching_focus: Practice sensory contrast with "It tasted [adj], but [surprise]" — building food vocabulary and contrast structures for descriptive writing.
4::themysterydish.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::themysterydish.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "ingredient" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "mild" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "flavor" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::themysterydish.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What did the teacher put on the table at the start of the lesson? (A covered dish with a secret ingredient)
2. (On) How did the flavor surprise Tommy? (It tasted mild at first, but then became warm, sweet, and faintly smoky)
3. (On) Who was the first person to ask for a second portion? (Mia)
4. (On) What was the mystery dish? (Jackfruit curry from Sri Lanka)
5. (Above) Why do you think the teacher did not tell the class what the dish was before they tasted it? (Accept: so they would try it without prejudice / to teach them to be open to new things)
4::themysterydish.go_description: 三节因果链：Nobody dares → Tommy tries first → Everyone wants more; 每节写Cause和Effect
4::themysterydish.go_usage: 用因果链梳理故事三个转折，对应"It tasted…but…"对比句型

4::schoolfoodfair.title: School Food Fair
4::schoolfoodfair.teaching_focus: Practice past simple verb 'tried', food nouns, and saying where things come from using 'from + country' in a simple sentence.
4::schoolfoodfair.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did Mia's school have? (A big bake sale)
• Which food from Japan did the children try? (Sushi)
• How did the sushi taste? (Light and fresh)
• What food did they try from Mexico? (Churros)
• How did the children feel at the end? (Happy and full)

2. Reading Skill:
• Why were some children shy at first? (Because the foods were new to them)
• Name two foods from different countries at the bake sale. (Sushi from Japan, churros from Mexico)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Children try food from different countries at a bake sale and enjoy it)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think trying new foods made the children feel happy? • (Vocabulary) What does culture mean? • (Feelings) How did the children feel before and after trying the new food? • (Connection) Have you ever tried food from another country? What did you think?
4::schoolfoodfair.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Which food from Japan did the children try? (Sushi)
2. (On) How did the sushi taste? (Light and fresh)
3. (On) Why were some children shy at the start? (Because the foods were new to them)
4. (On) Name two foods from different countries at the bake sale. (Sushi from Japan, churros from Mexico)
5. (Above) Why do you think trying new foods made the children feel happy at the end? (Because they discovered food from other countries can be delicious and helped them learn about other cultures)
4::schoolfoodfair.go_description: 四格：Setting（School bake sale）| Problem（Children were shy about new foods）| Climax（Dog grabbed hot dog and ran）| Resolution（Children liked all the foods; bake sale was fun）；附加：在地图旁标注食物与国家对应（Sushi-Japan, Tacos-Mexico, Pizza-Italy…）
4::schoolfoodfair.go_usage: 引导学生识别故事的情节结构，同时将文中各国食物与来源国建立联系

4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.title: Traditional Clothes Around the World
4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.teaching_focus: Practice the sentence pattern: subject 'People' + verb 'wear' + 'a' + clothing + 'in' + place. Students can swap the clothing and place to make many simple sentences for speaking and writing.
4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What do people wear in Japan? (A kimono)
• What is a kimono like? (It has bright colors and many layers)
• Name one other country and its traditional clothing. (India: sari; Scotland: kilt; Mexico: embroidered dress)
• What do traditional clothes show? (Culture)
• How many countries are in the book? (Six)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two countries and their traditional clothes. (Japan: kimono; India: sari; Scotland: kilt; Mexico: embroidered dress)
• What do all the traditional clothes have in common? (They show the culture of their country)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Traditional clothes from around the world show each country's culture)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think people still wear traditional clothes today? • (Vocabulary) What does traditional mean? • (Fact) Name one country and its traditional clothing. • (Connection) Do you have traditional clothing in your country? What is it like?
4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What do people wear in Japan? (A kimono)
2. (On) What is a kimono like? (It has bright colors and many layers)
3. (On) Name two countries and their traditional clothes from the book. (Japan: kimono; India: sari; Scotland: kilt; Mexico: embroidered dress)
4. (On) What do traditional clothes show? (Culture)
5. (Above) Why do you think people still wear traditional clothes today? (To show pride in their culture and to celebrate special occasions)
4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.go_description: 三栏标题：Country | Traditional Clothing | Key Feature；填入：Japan-Kimono-Bright colors and layers, China-Qipao-Elegant, India-Sari-Long and wrapped, Scotland-Kilt-Patterns show family history, Arab countries-Thawb-Loose and cool
4::traditionalclothesaroundtheworld.go_usage: 帮助学生系统对应各国传统服饰及其文化特征，理解服饰如何体现文化

4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.title: How Our Local Government Works
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.teaching_focus: Practice describing roles and responsibilities using "is responsible for", applicable to jobs and community roles.
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.reading_skill: 识别概念与关系 (Identifying Concepts and Relationships)
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "council" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "budget" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "citizen" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What does the council build and fix in the community? (Roads)
2. (On) What does the council use a budget for? (To plan and pay for local services like roads, parks, and safety)
3. (On) What is one right that every citizen has? (The right to vote)
4. (On) Why are laws important in a community? (They keep people safe and help everyone live together fairly)
5. (Above) How could young people get involved in local government decisions? (Accept: attending meetings / voting when old enough / writing to local leaders / joining youth councils)
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.go_description: 从左到右：Citizens → Vote → Council/Mayor → Make Laws → Community Benefits
4::howourlocalgovernmentworks.go_usage: 帮助学生理解地方政府的运作流程，从投票到立法到社区服务的逻辑链条

4::atriponthea380.title: A Trip on the A380
4::atriponthea380.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'went' to describe a past experience, use 'first' to show a new event, and practice 'on' with different vehicles; good for substitution and speaking practice.
4::atriponthea380.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::atriponthea380.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What is special about the A380? (It is a huge plane with two floors)
• How many engines are under each wing? (Two big engines)
• What is inside the plane? (A staircase)
• What did Mia see through the window? (Clouds and tiny houses)
• How did Mia feel on the plane? (Excited and amazed)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is one special feature of the A380 other planes do not have? (Two floors and a staircase inside)
• Name two things Mia noticed on the A380. (The staircase, the big wings, the engines, the view)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Mia takes her first trip on the A380 and is amazed by how big it is)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Mia was so excited to be on the A380? • (Vocabulary) What does engine mean? • (Feelings) How did Mia feel when she looked out of the window? • (Connection) Have you ever been on a plane? How did it feel?
4::atriponthea380.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How many floors does the A380 have? (Two)
2. (On) What is one special feature of the A380 that many other planes do not have? (A staircase inside / two floors)
3. (On) How many big engines are under each wing? (Two)
4. (On) What did Mia see through the window? (Clouds and tiny houses)
5. (Above) Why do you think Mia was so excited to be on the A380? (Because it was her first time on a plane / because it was very big and special)
4::atriponthea380.go_description: 左栏 A380 Features（Long wings, 2 big engines, Staircase, Two decks, Big screen）；右栏 Mia's Experience（Toy fell → Flight attendant helped → Watched cartoon → Ate sandwich）
4::atriponthea380.go_usage: 引导学生区分文本的说明性信息（飞机特征）与叙事性内容（Mia的经历），培养提取关键信息的能力

4::howplaneschangedtheworld.title: How Planes Changed the World
4::howplaneschangedtheworld.teaching_focus: Practice talking about change over time: use 'Since then' + present perfect ('have grown') and the structure 'from X into Y'. Work on plural subjects and simple noun phrases for A2 learners.
4::howplaneschangedtheworld.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who invented the first successful airplane? (The Wright brothers)
• About how long ago did they invent it? (Over a hundred years ago)
• How long could the first plane fly? (Only about twelve seconds)
• Name one way modern planes are different. (They are bigger, faster, carry more people)
• What do planes help people do today? (Travel far and fast)

2. Reading Skill:
• How have planes changed from past to now? (They grew bigger, faster, and carry more people)
• Compare the first plane with the A380. (First plane: tiny, flew 12 seconds; A380: huge, two floors)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Planes have changed a lot over the past hundred years)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) How do you think the Wright brothers felt when their plane flew for the first time? • (Vocabulary) What does invented mean? • (Fact) Name one way planes today are better than the first plane. • (Connection) How do you think planes will change in the future?
4::howplaneschangedtheworld.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Who invented the first successful airplane? (The Wright brothers)
2. (On) How long could the first plane fly? (Only about twelve seconds)
3. (On) How have planes changed from the past to now? (They grew bigger, faster, and carry more people)
4. (On) Name one way modern planes are different from the first plane. (Bigger, faster, carry more people)
5. (Above) How do you think the Wright brothers felt when their plane flew for the first time? Why? (Very excited and proud, because nobody had done it before)
4::howplaneschangedtheworld.go_description: 时间轴标注：100+ years ago（Wright brothers, first airplane）→ Then（Small aircraft → Big jets）→ Now（Jet/propeller engines, 300+ passengers, Pilots train thousands of hours）
4::howplaneschangedtheworld.go_usage: 引导学生综合全文信息，理解飞机技术随时间的演变，并用自己的语言总结变化

4::fivestepstoeatbetter.title: Five Steps to Eat Better
4::fivestepstoeatbetter.teaching_focus: Practice expressing health benefits with "When you [action], your body [result]" — linking behavior to outcome.
4::fivestepstoeatbetter.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "nutritious" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "calorie" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "stir" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::fivestepstoeatbetter.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What does a calorie measure? (The energy your body gets from food)
2. (On) What should you do before eating your yogurt, and why? (Stir it — the fruit settles at the bottom)
3. (On) Why is grilling healthier than frying? (When you grill, your body gets more nutrients and less oil)
4. (On) What is Step 5 and why is it better than juice? (Drink water — juice has a lot of sugar, while water helps your body use food better)
5. (Above) Design your own Step 6 for eating better. What would it be and why? (Accept any creative, reasonable healthy habit with a reason)
4::fivestepstoeatbetter.go_description: 5格编号步骤图：Step 1 Add colour → Step 2 Check calories → Step 3 Stir breakfast → Step 4 Grill, don't fry → Step 5 Drink water; 每格写行动+益处
4::fivestepstoeatbetter.go_usage: 把5个建议转化为可执行步骤，用"When you…your body…"为每步造句

4::tommysfirstroadtrip.title: Tommy's First Road Trip
4::tommysfirstroadtrip.teaching_focus: Practice making past-time clauses ('When they were out of the [place], . . .') and sequencing events; use place words (bakery, park, shop, house) and simple weather/action verbs (rain, snow, get cold) for substitution drills and speaking practice.
4::tommysfirstroadtrip.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who went on the road trip? (Tommy, Mia, and their family)
• What did they see on the way? (Green fields, farms, cows, tall trees, a river, mountains)
• What happened when they left the bakery? (They got a flat tire)
• Who helped fix the tire? (A friendly farmer)
• How did Tommy and Mia help? (They handed Dad the tools)

2. Reading Skill:
• Put these in order: saw mountains / got a flat tire / left the bakery. (Saw mountains → left bakery → got flat tire)
• How did the family solve the flat tire problem? (A friendly farmer helped them fix it)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Tommy and Mia go on their first road trip and have an unexpected adventure)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the farmer stopped to help the family? • (Vocabulary) What does flat tire mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the family felt when they got a flat tire? • (Connection) Have you ever had a surprise or problem on a trip? What happened?
4::tommysfirstroadtrip.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What happened when the family left the bakery? (They got a flat tire)
2. (On) Who helped fix the tire? (A friendly farmer)
3. (On) How did Tommy and Mia help fix the tire? (They handed Dad the tools)
4. (On) Put in order: got a flat tire / saw mountains / left the bakery. (Saw mountains → left bakery → got flat tire)
5. (Above) Why do you think the farmer stopped to help the family? (Because he was kind and saw they needed help)
4::tommysfirstroadtrip.go_description: 记录旅途事件顺序：Set off excited → Saw countryside (fields, farms, mountains) → Stopped at small town (gas + bakery) → Rain started → Flat tire → Mechanic fixed tire → Back on road
4::tommysfirstroadtrip.go_usage: 帮助学生追踪故事的时间顺序，理解'When. . . it started to. . .'句型如何连接事件

4::amazingplacestovisit.title: Amazing Places to Visit
4::amazingplacestovisit.teaching_focus: Students practice making suggestions with 'You can. . .' and saying purpose with 'to + verb', while learning place and activity vocabulary and doing substitution drills.
4::amazingplacestovisit.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::amazingplacestovisit.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Name three places worth visiting from the book. (A beach, a museum, a park, a farm)
• What can you do at a beach? (Play on the sand and swim)
• What can you learn at a museum? (About history, art, and science)
• What can you do at a farm? (See animals and learn how food grows)
• What does a good trip help you do? (Learn new things)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is one thing you can do at a museum that you cannot do at a beach? (Learn about history and art)
• Name two places and one thing to do at each. (Beach: swim; Museum: learn about history; Farm: see animals)
• What is the main idea of the book? (There are many fun places to visit that help you learn new things)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think visiting new places helps you learn? • (Vocabulary) What does museum mean? • (Fact) Name one place from the book and one thing you can do there. • (Connection) Which place from the book would you most like to visit? Why?
4::amazingplacestovisit.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two places worth visiting from the book. (Beach, museum, park, or farm)
2. (On) What can you do at a museum? (Learn about history, art, and science)
3. (On) What can you do at a farm? (See animals and learn how food grows)
4. (On) What does a good trip help you do? (Learn new things)
5. (Above) Why do you think visiting new places helps you learn? (Because you see and experience things you cannot find in a book or classroom)
4::amazingplacestovisit.go_description: 左列 Place（Beach, Mountain, Museum, City, Park, Old town）；右列 Purpose/Activity（Play on sand and swim, Walk in forests and see rivers, Learn about art and history, Try new food and listen to music, Relax and watch birds）
4::amazingplacestovisit.go_usage: 帮助学生理解 'You can visit. . . to. . .' 句型中地点与活动之间的目的关系，并联系自身经历

4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.title: Famous Landmarks and Their Stories
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.teaching_focus: Practice using passive voice in simple past to describe historical construction, and linking landmarks to their historical context.
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "landmark" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "monument" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "symbol" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.rr_questions:
1. (Below) How long is the Great Wall of China? (21,000 kilometers)
2. (On) Why was the Eiffel Tower built? (For the 1889 World Fair in Paris)
3. (On) What does the Statue of Liberty represent? (Freedom)
4. (On) What does the word "engineering" mean in this text? (The skill of designing and building large, impressive structures)
5. (Above) Why do you think people build landmarks and monuments? (Accept: to remember history / to celebrate achievements / to show national pride / to attract visitors)
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.go_description: 每个地标：左格写 建造原因，右格写 历史意义/象征，中间格写 地标名称
4::famouslandmarksandtheirstories.go_usage: 引导学生分析每个地标被建造的目的，以及它在今天代表的意义

4::miashobbies.title: Mia's Hobbies
4::miashobbies.teaching_focus: Practice present simple for routines (subject + plays + object/place) and time expression 'every evening'; work on subject-verb agreement and making short spoken answers.
4::miashobbies.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::miashobbies.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Name two hobbies Mia enjoys after school. (Drawing, painting, playing guitar, baking)
• What happened to the paint tray? (It fell off the edge of the table)
• What did the paint spill on? (The floor)
• What did Mia do instead of feeling upset? (Drew a picture of her blue cat)
• What does Mia's artwork show about her? (She is kind and creative)

2. Reading Skill:
• What clues tell you Mia is a kind person? (She drew her blue cat instead of being upset; she kept painting)
• How do you know Mia loves her hobbies? (She does them every day after school)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Mia loves her hobbies and deals with accidents in a positive way)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Mia drew a picture of her blue cat after the spill? • (Vocabulary) What does hobby mean? • (Feelings) How do you think Mia felt after the paint spilled? • (Connection) What hobby do you enjoy most, and what does it tell people about you?
4::miashobbies.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two hobbies Mia enjoys after school. (Drawing, painting, playing guitar, or baking)
2. (On) What happened to the paint tray? (It fell off the edge of the table)
3. (On) What did Mia do instead of feeling upset about the spill? (Drew a picture of her blue cat)
4. (On) What clues tell you Mia is a kind person? (She kept painting and drew her cat instead of being upset)
5. (Above) Why do you think Mia drew a picture of her blue cat after the paint spilled? (Because she turned the accident into something creative / she stayed positive)
4::miashobbies.go_description: 中心写 Mia，向外分支：Hobbies（Drawing/Painting, Guitar, Baking）| Personality（Creative, Kind-hearted / felt sorry for cat, Humorous / drew funny picture of blue cat）
4::miashobbies.go_usage: 引导学生通过Mia的行动和反应推断她的性格特点，培养从细节推断人物特征的能力

4::theworldofhobbies.title: The World of Hobbies
4::theworldofhobbies.teaching_focus: Practice subject + third-person singular verb (helps), use 'you' + base verb to say benefits, swap different activities and benefits to build vocabulary and fluency; use substitution drills and short speaking prompts (e. g. , 'What hobby helps you. . .').
4::theworldofhobbies.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::theworldofhobbies.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Name two benefits of gardening. (Helps you relax and learn patience)
• What can collectors have thousands of? (Small items like stamps)
• Name one hobby that helps you stay fit. (Running, cycling, or swimming)
• Name one indoor hobby from the book. (Painting, collecting)
• What does the author say about hobbies in general? (They make life fun)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is one benefit of painting? (It is a popular and relaxing hobby)
• What is one benefit of an outdoor hobby? (It helps you stay fit)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Hobbies make life fun and have many benefits)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think hobbies are good for people? • (Vocabulary) What does patience mean? • (Fact) Name one hobby and one benefit it has. • (Connection) What hobby would you like to start, and why?
4::theworldofhobbies.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two benefits of gardening from the book. (It helps you relax and learn patience)
2. (On) What can stamp collectors have thousands of? (Small stamps)
3. (On) Name one outdoor hobby from the book. (Running, cycling, or swimming)
4. (On) What does the author say about hobbies in general? (They make life fun)
5. (Above) Why do you think hobbies are good for people? (They help people relax, stay fit, learn new skills, and enjoy life)
4::theworldofhobbies.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
4::theworldofhobbies.go_description: 中心写 Hobbies make life fun，向外分支写各爱好及其益处：Gardening（Relax, patience）, Collecting（Thousands of items）, Learning online（New friends, new skills）, Music（Express feelings）, Chess（Improves thinking）, Running（Strong and healthy）
4::theworldofhobbies.go_usage: 帮助学生识别文章主旨，并将各段细节归入对应分支，理解每种爱好的独特价值

4::amazingjobs.title: Amazing Jobs
4::amazingjobs.teaching_focus: Practice describing job roles and their purposes using "A [job] [verb] to [purpose]", expanding vocabulary around careers and responsibilities.
4::amazingjobs.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::amazingjobs.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "marine" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "veterinarian" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "journalist" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::amazingjobs.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What job works in hot, smoky places to keep people safe? (A firefighter)
2. (On) How did the class find out about each job? (Each worker gave clues and the class guessed)
3. (On) What does a journalist do? (Asks questions, gathers information, and writes stories for the news)
4. (On) What is the purpose of having a guessing game to introduce the jobs? (To make learning about jobs fun and interactive)
5. (Above) What do all the jobs in the story have in common? (Accept: they all involve helping or serving other people in some way)
4::amazingjobs.go_description: 中心写 "Amazing Jobs"，外圈写各职业名，每个职业连接一个关键职责描述
4::amazingjobs.go_usage: 鼓励学生思考自己梦想的职业并使用句型"A [job] [verb] to [purpose]"造句

4::weekendsportsclub.title: Weekend Sports Club
4::weekendsportsclub.teaching_focus: Practice saying or writing results: use 'The game ended with. . .' + a noun phrase to tell how an event finished; focus on past simple 'ended' and short, concrete endings.
4::weekendsportsclub.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::weekendsportsclub.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where did Tommy, Mia, and their friends meet? (At the sports club)
• What went missing before the game? (The ball)
• Who ran out of the bushes? (A puppy)
• What did the puppy do with the ball? (Ran around with it)
• How did everyone react? (They laughed and clapped for the puppy)

2. Reading Skill:
• What caused the ball to go missing? (A puppy had taken it into the bushes)
• How do you know the children were not angry? (They laughed and clapped for the puppy)
• What is the main idea of the story? (Tommy and Mia go to sports club and a puppy causes a funny surprise)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) How do you think the children felt when the puppy ran out? • (Vocabulary) What does missing mean? • (Feelings) How did everyone feel at the end? • (Connection) Have you ever had a funny surprise during a game or sport? What happened?
4::weekendsportsclub.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What went missing before the game? (The ball)
2. (On) Who ran out of the bushes? (A puppy)
3. (On) What caused the ball to go missing? (A puppy had taken it into the bushes)
4. (On) How did everyone react when the puppy ran out? (They laughed and clapped)
