5. (Above) How do you think the children felt when the puppy ran out with the ball? Why? (Surprised and happy, because it was funny and unexpected)
4::weekendsportsclub.go_description: 链式记录：Ball went missing → Searched near benches, goal, park → Mia found dog in bushes with ball → Dog nudged ball and scored a goal → Everyone laughed → Team won 3-2 → Game ended with puppy trophy
4::weekendsportsclub.go_usage: 引导学生在阅读前预测球的去向，阅读中梳理事件因果链，理解幽默结局的成因

4::baseball.title: Baseball
4::baseball.sentence_pattern: [Subject] is a popular [type] hobby and sport.
4::baseball.teaching_focus: Practice subject + 'is' + a/an + noun phrase to define things; substitute different activity words; short spoken answers and written sentences at A2 level.
4::baseball.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What equipment do players need to play baseball? (A bat, a ball, and a glove)
• What does the pitcher do? (Throws the ball to the batter)
• What does the catcher do? (Waits behind the batter to catch the ball)
• What does the batter try to do? (Hit the ball with the bat)
• What does baseball help players do? (Stay healthy and work as a team)

2. Reading Skill:
• How is the catcher's job different from the batter's? (The catcher catches the ball; the batter tries to hit it)
• What is one way baseball helps people? (It keeps players healthy and teaches teamwork)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Baseball is a popular sport that needs teamwork and special equipment)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think teamwork is important in baseball? • (Vocabulary) What does pitcher mean in baseball? • (Fact) Name one job of a player in baseball. • (Connection) Have you ever played baseball or another team sport? What did you enjoy about it?
4::baseball.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What equipment do players need to play baseball? (A bat, a ball, and a glove)
2. (On) What does the pitcher do in a game? (Throws the ball to the batter)
3. (On) How is the catcher's job different from the batter's? (The catcher catches the ball; the batter tries to hit it)
4. (On) What does baseball help players do? (Stay healthy and work as a team)
5. (Above) Why do you think teamwork is important in baseball? (Because each player has a different job and they all need to work together to win)
4::baseball.go_description: 按比赛顺序标注：Pitcher throws ball → Batter tries to hit → Catcher catches (if miss) → Batter hits → Batter runs the bases → Team scores；附注：Players throw, catch, hit, run
4::baseball.go_usage: 引导学生带着'棒球怎么玩'的问题阅读，并用流程图梳理比赛的基本规则和顺序

4::howdoyoufeeltoday.title: How Do You Feel Today?
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.teaching_focus: Practice expressing feelings with "I felt [emotion] when..." — moving beyond happy/sad to richer emotion words essential for KET writing.
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "relieved" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "frustrated" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "jealous" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What did the teacher ask everyone to do with their journals? (Write real feeling words every day — not just happy or sad)
2. (On) Why did Tommy feel frustrated at the start? (He could not find the right words for what he felt inside)
3. (On) What did Tommy feel when he saw Mia win the art prize? (He felt jealous, and then ashamed for feeling that way)
4. (On) What did Tommy finally write in his journal? (I felt relieved when Mia asked if I was okay)
5. (Above) Why do you think naming a feeling can help make it smaller? (Accept: putting a feeling into words helps you understand and control it / feelings are less overwhelming when you can identify them)
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.go_description: 三列表：Situation | Tommy's Feeling | Evidence; 四行对应四个情绪节点
4::howdoyoufeeltoday.go_usage: 从行动和对话中推断Tommy的情绪，用"I felt [emotion] when…"句型改写每个节点

4::adayintoyland.title: A Day in Toyland
4::adayintoyland.teaching_focus: Use the passive 'are made of' to describe materials. Practice substituting different subjects and materials in speaking and short writing.
4::adayintoyland.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What were the streets made of in the magical land? (colorful bricks)
• What moved on the streets without drivers? (Tiny brick cars)
• What did the narrator carry? (A blue backpack)
• What did the narrator find? (A shiny key)
• What happened at the end? (The narrator woke up — it was a dream)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two things in the magical land different from the real world. (Brick streets, cars without drivers)
• How do you know the land was not a real place? (The narrator woke up at the end — it was a dream)
• What is the main idea of the story? (The narrator has a dream about a magical land made of bricks)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the narrator ran after the shiny key? • (Vocabulary) What does magical mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the narrator felt in the magical land? • (Connection) If you could visit a magical land, what would it look like?
4::adayintoyland.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What were the streets made of in the magical land? (colorful bricks)
2. (On) What moved on the streets without any drivers? (Tiny brick cars)
3. (On) Name two things that were different from the real world. (Brick streets, cars without drivers)
4. (On) How do you know the land was not a real place? (The narrator woke up at the end — it was a dream)
5. (Above) Why do you think the narrator ran after the shiny key? (Because it was interesting and mysterious / they wanted to find out what it opened)
4::adayintoyland.graphic_organizer: Fantasy vs. Reality Chart (奇幻与现实对比图)
4::adayintoyland.go_description: 左列 Fantasy Elements（Streets made of colorful bricks, Tiny brick cars with no drivers, Flying dragon drops a key, Secret door to underground town, Moving trees and colorful rivers）；右列 Real-World Objects（Backpack, Map, Streets, Clown, Door）
4::adayintoyland.go_usage: 帮助学生区分故事中的奇幻元素与现实物品，理解第一人称奇幻叙事的特点

4::howtransportationchangedourworld.title: How Transportation Changed Our World
4::howtransportationchangedourworld.teaching_focus: Practice ordering events with the time words 'At first,' 'Then,' 'Later' and using simple past verbs; learners can swap subjects and verbs to make their own short histories or stories.
4::howtransportationchangedourworld.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What was used for city transport before trains? (Horses pulling carts)
• When did trains begin in many cities? (In the 1800s)
• How many people can one busy subway station handle? (Thousands)
• Name one modern form of city transport. (Subway, electric bus, bicycle)
• What might come in the future? (Driverless buses)

2. Reading Skill:
• Put in order: electric buses / horse carts / trains. (Horse carts → trains → electric buses)
• How has city transport changed from past to now? (From horses to trains to electric buses and subways)
• What is the main idea of the book? (City transport has changed a lot over time and will keep changing)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think driverless buses might be useful in the future? • (Vocabulary) What does subway mean? • (Fact) Name one type of city transport from the past. • (Connection) What type of transport do you use most? Would you like to try a driverless bus?
4::howtransportationchangedourworld.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What was used for city transport before trains? (Horses pulling carts)
2. (On) When did trains begin in many cities? (In the 1800s)
3. (On) Put in order: electric buses / horse carts / trains. (Horse carts → trains → electric buses)
4. (On) How many people can one busy subway station handle? (Thousands)
5. (Above) Why do you think driverless buses might be useful in the future? (They could be safer and more efficient because computers do not get tired)
4::howtransportationchangedourworld.go_description: 时间轴标注：At first（Horses pulled carts）→ Then（Trains / 1800s）→ Later（Subways / 100, 000 people per station）→ Now（Buses, cars, bike lanes, driverless buses planned）→ Future（Flying machines）
4::howtransportationchangedourworld.go_usage: 引导学生综合文中时序词（At first, Then, Later, Now, Someday），在时间轴上完整还原城市交通的发展脉络

4::thelanguageofdolphins.title: The Language of Dolphins
4::thelanguageofdolphins.teaching_focus: Practice indirect speech with "discovered that" to report findings, building academic and science reporting language.
4::thelanguageofdolphins.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thelanguageofdolphins.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "communicate" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "sonar" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "pod" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::thelanguageofdolphins.rr_questions:
1. (Below) Where did Mia and Tommy go to learn about dolphins? (The ocean center)
2. (On) How do dolphins communicate with each other? (Using clicks, whistles, and sonar signals)
3. (On) What does it mean that each dolphin has its own unique signal? (It works like a name — each dolphin can be identified by its signal)
4. (On) What is a pod? (A group of dolphins that live and travel together)
5. (Above) How is dolphin communication similar to human language? (Accept: both use sounds to share information / both can identify individuals / both are learned within a group)
4::thelanguageofdolphins.graphic_organizer: Double Bubble Map (双气泡比较图)
4::thelanguageofdolphins.go_description: 左圆写 Human Communication，右圆写 Dolphin Communication，中间写共同点
4::thelanguageofdolphins.go_usage: 鼓励学生比较人类和海豚的沟通方式，在对比中加深对文本主旨的理解

4::ouramazingamusementparkday.title: Our Amazing Amusement Park Day
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.teaching_focus: Practice using 'There were' for plural situations in the past + 'but' to show contrast. Students can change the noun after 'There were' and make different short contrasting clauses to speak and write simple past contrasts.
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.core_vocab_raw: amusement park, bumper car, give it a try, roller coaster
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What was the first ride the narrator tried? (The roller coaster)
• How did the narrator feel about the roller coaster at first? (Scared)
• Name two other rides from the story. (Ferris wheel, bumper cars, spinning cups)
• What helped the narrator feel brave? (A friend encouraged them)
• How did the narrator feel at the end? (Happy and excited)

2. Reading Skill:
• How did the narrator's feelings change from start to end? (From scared to brave and happy)
• What helped the narrator become brave? (A friend encouraged them)
• What is the main idea of the story? (The narrator overcomes fear at an amusement park with a friend's help)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the narrator was scared of the roller coaster at first? • (Vocabulary) What does brave mean? • (Feelings) How did the narrator feel after riding the roller coaster? • (Connection) Which ride from the story would you most like to try? Why?
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How did the narrator feel about the roller coaster at first? (Scared)
2. (On) What was the first ride the narrator tried? (The roller coaster)
3. (On) What helped the narrator feel brave enough to try the roller coaster? (A friend encouraged them)
4. (On) How did the narrator's feelings change from start to end? (From scared to brave and happy)
5. (Above) Why do you think the narrator was scared of the roller coaster at first? (Because it was fast and they had never tried it before)
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.go_description: 沿时间轴标注情感变化：Arrived → Excited (talked about rides) → Scared (saw roller coaster queue) → Brave (gave it a go) → Thrilled (rode 3 times!) → Relaxed (Ferris wheel + popcorn) → Happy (bumper cars, promised to come back)
4::ouramazingamusementparkday.go_usage: 引导学生联系自身经历，同时追踪叙述者从害怕到勇敢的情感变化，理解克服恐惧的过程

4::acitymarket.title: A City Market
4::acitymarket.teaching_focus: Practice using "The air smells of ___" to describe smells in different places. Students can replace the items after "of" to talk about parks, kitchens, beaches, or markets.
4::acitymarket.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::acitymarket.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who opened Disneyland and when? (Walt Disney opened it in 1955)
• Where is it located? (Near Los Angeles, California)
• About how many people visit every year? (About eighteen million)
• Name two things visitors can do there. (Ride attractions, meet characters, watch parades)
• What does the author suggest? (Arrive early)

2. Reading Skill:
• Why does the author suggest arriving early? (To avoid long queues and see more)
• Name two reasons Disneyland is popular. (Famous characters, exciting rides, magical feeling)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Disneyland is a magical and popular park that people love to visit)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Disneyland is called a 'magical' place? • (Vocabulary) What does memories mean? • (Fact) When did Walt Disney open Disneyland? • (Connection) Would you like to visit Disneyland? What would you most like to do there?
4::acitymarket.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Who opened Disneyland and when? (Walt Disney opened it in 1955)
2. (On) About how many people visit Disneyland every year? (About eighteen million)
3. (On) Why does the author suggest arriving early at Disneyland? (To avoid long queues and see more)
4. (On) Name two things visitors can do at Disneyland. (Ride attractions, meet characters, watch parades)
5. (Above) Why do you think Disneyland is called a 'magical' place? (Because it has famous characters, exciting rides, and makes people feel happy)
4::acitymarket.go_description: 档案格式记录关键数字：Location（Near Los Angeles, CA）| Opened（1955, by Walt Disney）| Visitors/year（~18 million）| Attractions（~60）| Performers per parade（400）| Tip（Arrive early = less waiting）
4::acitymarket.go_usage: 帮助学生从介绍性文本中提取关键数字和事实，理解如何筛选非虚构文本中的重要信息

4::incrediblejourneys.title: Incredible Journeys
4::incrediblejourneys.sentence_pattern: Every year, many [animals] start long journeys.
4::incrediblejourneys.teaching_focus: Practice describing annual events and habitual actions using "Every year" with simple present tense, focusing on distance and purpose.
4::incrediblejourneys.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "migration" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "hibernate" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "navigate" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::incrediblejourneys.rr_questions:
1. (Below) Why do animals migrate every year? (To find food, warmth, and safe places to raise their young)
2. (On) How do monarch butterflies navigate on their long journey? (Accept: they use the sun and stars / they follow instinct)
3. (On) What is the difference between migrating and hibernating? (Migrating means traveling to another place; hibernating means sleeping through winter in the same place)
4. (On) What is a flock? (A group of birds traveling or living together)
5. (Above) What dangers might migrating animals face on their journey? (Accept: predators / bad weather / loss of habitat / exhaustion / crossing roads or cities)
4::incrediblejourneys.go_description: 按季节顺序标注动物迁徙路线：Spring origin → Summer destination → Fall return → Winter rest
4::incrediblejourneys.go_usage: 引导学生将迁徙过程可视化为循环流程图，理解迁徙的季节性规律

4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.title: A Surprising Day at Dad's Office
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.teaching_focus: Practice using a 'When' time clause + past verb (returned) and simple reported speech with 'he asked' to tell sequences and report questions in spoken or written short stories.
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.core_vocab_raw: moment, office, slide, a stack of
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where was the narrator's dad working? (At a tall desk in his office)
• What was on Dad's desk? (A stack of papers)
• What was the small robot doing? (Cleaning near Dad's chair)
• What did the robot bump into? (Dad's desk)
• What happened to the papers? (They fell off the desk)

2. Reading Skill:
• What caused the papers to fall? (The robot bumped into the desk)
• Why do you think Dad laughed when he came back? (Because the mess was caused by the robot / it was funny)
• What is the main idea of the story? (The narrator visits Dad's office and a robot accidentally makes a funny mess)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Dad laughed when he found out what happened? • (Vocabulary) What does accidentally mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the narrator felt when the papers fell? • (Connection) Would you like a robot in your home or classroom? What would you want it to do?
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What was the small robot doing in Dad's office? (Cleaning near Dad's chair)
2. (On) What caused the papers to fall off the desk? (The robot bumped into the desk)
3. (On) What was on Dad's desk? (A stack of papers)
4. (On) What happened when Dad came back and found out? (He laughed)
5. (Above) Why do you think Dad laughed when he found out the robot knocked the papers? (Because it was funny and the robot did not mean to cause trouble)
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.go_description: Cause（Stack of papers slid from dad's desk）→ Effect 1（Papers flew across the room）→ Effect 2（Robot beeped louder and spun around）→ Final（Dad returned, asked about papers → Both laughed and picked them up together）
4::asurprisingdayatdadsoffice.go_usage: 引导学生推断事件的起因，追踪连锁反应，理解故事中幽默结局的形成逻辑

4::jobspeopledo.title: Jobs People Do
4::jobspeopledo.teaching_focus: Practice job vocabulary and the present simple for routines, subject-verb-object order, using A/An with jobs, and substitution drills for speaking practice.
4::jobspeopledo.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where does a farmer sell the food they grow? (At markets)
• Name two jobs that help sick people. (Doctor and nurse)
• What does a baker make? (Bread, early every morning)
• What does a builder use? (Tools to make strong houses)
• What do workers use to make lives better? (Different skills)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two jobs and what each person does. (Teacher: helps children learn; Doctor: cares for sick people; Farmer: grows food)
• What do all the jobs have in common? (They all help people and society)
• What is the main idea of the book? (People do many different jobs that help make our lives better)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the author says workers use 'different skills'? • (Vocabulary) What does uniform mean? • (Fact) Name one job and one thing that person does. • (Connection) Which job would you most like to do when you grow up? Why?
4::jobspeopledo.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where does a farmer sell the food they grow? (At markets)
2. (On) Name two jobs that help sick people. (Doctor and nurse)
3. (On) What does a builder use to make strong houses? (Tools)
4. (On) What do all the jobs in the book have in common? (They all help people and make lives better)
5. (Above) Why do you think the author says workers use 'different skills'? (Because every job needs special knowledge and abilities)
4::jobspeopledo.graphic_organizer: Two-Column Matching Chart (职业工作对应表)
4::jobspeopledo.go_description: 左列 Job（Teacher, Doctor/Nurse, Farmer, Baker, Driver, Builder, Chef）；右列 What They Do（Help children learn, Care for sick people, Grow food and sell at markets, Make bread, Deliver packages, Build strong houses, Cook meals）
4::jobspeopledo.go_usage: 帮助学生将文中各职业与工作内容配对，联系生活中认识的职业，理解不同工作对社会的贡献

4::therainforestrescue.title: The Rainforest Rescue
4::therainforestrescue.teaching_focus: Practice real conditional sentences using "If we [action], we can [result]" to discuss environmental solutions and encourage action.
4::therainforestrescue.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::therainforestrescue.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "endangered" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "habitat" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "conserve" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::therainforestrescue.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What did the scientist point to when Mia and Tommy arrived? (Broken trees and muddy trails — parts of the damaged forest)
2. (On) What does "endanger" mean in this text? (To put animals or plants at risk of disappearing)
3. (On) What did Mia and Tommy plant to help the forest recover? (Seedlings — young plants grown from seeds)
4. (On) What does it mean to "conserve" the rainforest? (To protect and carefully manage it so it is not destroyed)
5. (Above) What is one thing you could do in your own life to help protect natural habitats? (Accept any specific, realistic action)
4::therainforestrescue.go_description: 左格写人类行为（砍树、污染），右格写后果（动物失去家园、物种减少），最后一行写解决方案
4::therainforestrescue.go_usage: 引导学生通过因果图理解环境破坏的连锁反应，并思考"If we..."句型提出解决方案

4::ahomeforbirdy.title: A Home for Birdy
4::ahomeforbirdy.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'used' + naming materials and tools, and express purpose with 'to make. . .' for short, repeatable speaking and substitution drills.
4::ahomeforbirdy.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What had happened to Birdy? (She lost her nest)
• What did they use to make the roof? (A flat piece of wood)
• How did they make the door? (They cut a round hole)
• What did they put inside for Birdy to sleep on? (A sock)
• What did they put near the tree for Birdy to drink from? (A mug filled with water)

2. Reading Skill:
• Put these steps in order: cut a round hole / found a box / added a sock. (Found a box → cut a round hole → added a sock)
• What materials made the house strong? (Twigs and string)
• What is the main idea of the story? (The narrator builds a new home for a bird named Birdy who lost her nest)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) How do you think Birdy felt when she hopped inside her new home? • (Vocabulary) What does bedding mean? • (Feelings) How do you think the narrator felt when Birdy hopped inside? • (Connection) Have you ever made something to help an animal or another person? What did you make?
4::ahomeforbirdy.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did they use for Birdy to sleep on? (A sock)
2. (On) What materials did they use to make the roof and the door? (A flat piece of wood for the roof; they cut a round hole for the door)
3. (On) Put in order: cut a round hole / found a box / added a sock. (Found a box → cut a round hole → added a sock)
4. (On) What did they put near the tree for Birdy to drink from? (A mug filled with water)
5. (Above) How do you think Birdy felt when she hopped inside her new home? Why? (Safe and happy, because she had a warm place to rest after losing her nest)
4::ahomeforbirdy.go_description: 按顺序标注搭建步骤：Step 1 Found a clean box → Step 2 Added wood roof → Step 3 Cut round hole for door → Step 4 Used twigs and string to make it strong → Step 5 Found a sock for bedding → Step 6 Filled a mug with water → Result: Birdy has a warm safe home
4::ahomeforbirdy.go_usage: 引导学生边视觉化每个搭建步骤边阅读，理解 'We used. . . to make. . .' 句型如何描述目的性行动

4::animalhomesandhabitats.title: Animal Homes and Habitats
4::animalhomesandhabitats.teaching_focus: Practice the modal 'must' to show obligation, use 'protect' with a noun, and use 'for' to show who or what benefits. Good for substitution drills and speaking about simple environmental actions.
4::animalhomesandhabitats.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What is a habitat? (The natural home of an animal)
• Name two animals that live in a forest. (Birds, foxes)
• Where do frogs lay their eggs? (In a pond)
• What do camels do in the desert? (Store water and travel long distances)
• What must people do for habitats? (Protect them)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two animals and their habitats. (Birds: forest; Frogs: pond; Camels: desert)
• Why do people also need to protect habitats? (Habitats keep animals safe and provide food for people too)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Animals live in different habitats and we must protect them)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do foxes dig burrows in the forest? • (Vocabulary) What does burrow mean? • (Fact) Name one animal and its habitat. • (Connection) What habitat do you live near? What animals live there?
4::animalhomesandhabitats.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What is a habitat? (The natural home of an animal)
2. (On) Name two animals that live in a forest. (Birds and foxes)
3. (On) What do camels do in the desert? (Store water and travel long distances)
4. (On) Why do foxes dig burrows? (To have a safe home under the ground)
5. (Above) Why does the author say we must protect habitats for both animals and people? (Because animals provide food and keep nature balanced, which helps people too)
4::animalhomesandhabitats.go_description: 顶层写 Animal Habitats，下方分出五个栖息地及对应动物：Forest（Birds, Foxes）, Pond（Frogs, Tadpoles）, Desert（Camels）, Grassland（Wide spaces / tall grass）, Ocean（Waves, reefs, deep water）；底部写保护主题：We must protect habitats
4::animalhomesandhabitats.go_usage: 帮助学生将各栖息地的特征和动物归类整理，理解'保护栖息地'这一核心主张的依据

4::theearthsecosystems.title: The Earth's Ecosystems
4::theearthsecosystems.teaching_focus: Practice expressing dependency and necessity using "Without... cannot..." to describe ecological relationships.
4::theearthsecosystems.reading_skill: 识别概念与关系 (Identifying Concepts and Relationships)
4::theearthsecosystems.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "ecosystem" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "predator" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "biodiversity" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::theearthsecosystems.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What do trees provide for animals in a forest ecosystem? (Food and shelter)
2. (On) How does a predator help keep an ecosystem in balance? (By hunting weaker animals and stopping one species from growing too large)
3. (On) What is a coral reef? (An underwater ecosystem that is home to a huge variety of sea life)
4. (On) What does "enrich" mean in this text? (To improve the quality of something — animals enrich the soil by spreading seeds and breaking down dead plants)
5. (Above) What might happen to an ecosystem if all its predators disappeared? (Accept: the prey population would grow too large / plants would be overeaten / the whole ecosystem could collapse)
4::theearthsecosystems.go_description: 中间写 "Ecosystem Imbalance"，左侧写造成失衡的原因，右侧写失衡的后果
4::theearthsecosystems.go_usage: 帮助学生理解生态系统的连锁反应，强化"Without... cannot..."的学术表达

4::seedsofhope.title: Seeds of Hope
4::seedsofhope.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'saw' with noun phrases and the preposition 'near' to describe where you saw people or things; good for substitution drills and speaking short past descriptions.
4::seedsofhope.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::seedsofhope.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::seedsofhope.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where did the narrator see the homeless man? (Near their school)
• What did the narrator bring him? (A sandwich, warm tea, and a small toy chicken)
• What did the man show the narrator? (A tiny plant growing in a jar)
• What grew from a small hidden seed? (The tiny plant)
• What did the narrator promise to do? (Come back the next day and help plant the seeds)

2. Reading Skill:
• What does the tiny plant tell us about the man? (He loves nature and has hope)
• What does 'his small garden will grow hope' mean? (That kindness and care can bring happiness and a better future)
• What is the main idea of the story? (A child helps a homeless man and learns about hope through a small seed)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the man showed the narrator the plant? • (Vocabulary) What does hope mean? • (Feelings) How did both the narrator and the man feel at the end? • (Connection) Have you ever done something kind for a stranger? How did it make you feel?
4::seedsofhope.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did the narrator bring to the homeless man? (A sandwich, warm tea, and a small toy chicken)
2. (On) What did the man show the narrator? (A tiny plant growing in a jar)
3. (On) What does the tiny plant tell us about the man? (He loves nature and has hope)
4. (On) What did the narrator promise to do? (Come back the next day and help plant the seeds)
5. (Above) What do you think the author means by 'his small garden will grow hope'? (That kindness and care can bring happiness and a better future for the man)
4::seedsofhope.go_description: 左侧记录情感变化：Child（Saw sad man → Wanted to help → Felt connected → Felt hopeful）| Old man（Looked sad → Smiled and laughed → Showed plant with pride）；右侧写象征：The small seed = Hope / New beginning；箭头连接情感与象征
4::seedsofhope.go_usage: 引导学生推断种子在故事中的象征意义，理解叙事如何通过具体物品传达抽象情感

4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.title: People and Their Homes in History
4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.teaching_focus: Students can practice sequencing events with time phrases, using the simple past for actions, and comparing past and present (Today) in short spoken or written sentences.
4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did people long ago live in? (Caves)
• What did people build after caves? (Small huts from wood and straw)
• What did families build after huts? (Bigger houses from stone and brick)
• Who lived in castles long ago? (Kings and rich people)
• Where do many people live today? (In apartments in cities)

2. Reading Skill:
• Put in order: stone houses / caves / huts. (Caves → huts → stone houses)
• How are modern homes different from homes in the past? (Modern homes have lights, water, and heat)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Homes have changed a lot over time but people still need safe places to live)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think stone houses were better than wood huts? • (Vocabulary) What does modern mean? • (Fact) Name one type of home from the past and one from today. • (Connection) What is your home like? How is it different from a cave or a hut?
4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did people long ago use to build small huts? (Wood and straw)
2. (On) What did families build after huts? (Bigger houses from stone and brick)
3. (On) Put in order: stone houses / caves / huts. (Caves → huts → stone houses)
4. (On) How are modern homes different from homes in the past? (They have lights, water, and heat)
5. (Above) Why do you think stone houses were better than wood huts for keeping families safe? (Because stone is stronger and keeps out rain, wind, and cold better than wood and straw)
4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.go_description: 时间轴标注各阶段：Long ago（Caves: cool, safe from rain/wind）→ Later（Huts: wood and straw, warm, easy to fix）→ Then（Stone/brick houses: dry and safe）→ Long ago kings（Castles: tall, strong, many rooms）→ Today（Apartments: lights, water, heat）
4::peopleandtheirhomesinhistory.go_usage: 引导学生综合时序词（Long ago, Later, Then, Today），在时间轴上对比各时期住所的材料与功能，总结住所演变的核心规律

4::thewindweaver.title: The Wind Weaver
4::thewindweaver.teaching_focus: Practice using participial phrases with "making" to describe the effect of one action on another, enhancing descriptive writing.
4::thewindweaver.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "weave" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "gust" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "breeze" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::thewindweaver.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What happened to the town when the storm hit? (Wind tore roofs and scattered hats everywhere)
2. (On) What special ability did the Wind Weaver have? (She could weave and shape the wind with her hands)
3. (On) What is a "gust" of wind? (A sudden, strong burst of wind)
4. (On) How did the Wind Weaver stop the storm? (She wove the wild gusts into a gentle breeze)
5. (Above) What does this story suggest about people who seem different or unusual? (Accept: unusual abilities can be gifts / we should not fear or judge those who are different / differences can help a community)
4::thewindweaver.go_description: Setting / Characters / Problem / 4 Key Events / Resolution — 每格用图画或词汇填写
4::thewindweaver.go_usage: 用视觉化引导学生想象风的场景，从感官细节中提炼故事要素

4::anewhomeforfox.title: A New Home for Fox
4::anewhomeforfox.sentence_pattern: [Animal] lived in a home that did not fit.
4::anewhomeforfox.teaching_focus: Students substitute the animal, the home/place, and the reason it didn't work. Practice: 'Fish lived in a small pond, but it dried up in summer.' / 'Bear lived in a big cave, but it was too dark and cold.' / 'Cat lived in a soft basket, but the dog took it away.' Focus: [subject] + lived in + a + [noun phrase], but + [problem clause]. Teaches 'but' as a contrast connector and past simple 'lived in' for describing where someone stays.
4::anewhomeforfox.reading_skill: 识别因果关系 (Identifying Cause and Effect)
4::anewhomeforfox.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where did Fox live at the start of the story? (In a small hole under a tree)
• What happened one night in the forest? (Heavy rain fell and water rushed into Fox's home)
• Who did Fox visit first? (Bird)
• Why couldn't Fox stay with Bird? (The nest was too small for Fox)
• Who helped Fox find a new home? (Bear)

2. Reading Skill:
• What caused Fox to leave his home? (Heavy rain and flooding)
• What is the problem in this story? (Fox needs a safe new place to sleep)
• How was the problem solved? (Bear helped Fox find a dry cave)

3. Reading Strategy:
• Before reading, what did you predict Fox would do after leaving his hole?
• Were your predictions correct?
• How does this story remind you of another story about animals finding homes?

4. Other Comprehension:
• (Story Elements) Who are the characters? What is the setting? What is the problem? What is the solution?
• (Sequence) Put in order: Fox visits Rabbit / Heavy rain falls / Bear finds cave / Fox leaves his hole
• (Vocabulary) What does 'burrow' mean? Can you point to the sentence where it appears?
• (Inference) How do you think Fox felt when Bear found him sitting in the rain?
• (Connection) Have you ever needed to find a new place to stay? How did it feel?
4::anewhomeforfox.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where did Fox live at the beginning of the story? (In a small hole under a tree.)
2. (On) Why did Fox have to leave his home? (Heavy rain fell and water rushed into his hole.)
3. (On) Why couldn't Fox stay with Rabbit? (Rabbit's burrow was underground, but Fox could not fit inside.)
4. (On) What did Bear do to help Fox? (Bear found a dry cave near the hill and helped Fox carry leaves and sticks inside.)
5. (Above) Why do you think Fox said "This feels like home" at the end of the story? (Because the cave was dry and safe, and he made it comfortable together with Bear — having a friend who helped made it feel like a real home.)
4::anewhomeforfox.go_description: 以故事要素呈现Fox找新家经历的图形组织器：角色（Fox/Bird/Rabbit/Bear）、背景（森林）、问题（暴雨冲走家）、事件（三次寻访）、结局（找到山洞）
4::anewhomeforfox.go_usage: 引导学生填入：Characters（Fox, Bird, Rabbit, Bear）→ Setting（Forest, rainy night）→ Problem（Heavy rain flooded Fox's hole）→ Events（Fox visits Bird: too small; visits Rabbit: can't fit; Bear finds cave）→ Solution（Fox and Bear make the cave into a home）

4::homesthatmove.title: Homes That Move
4::homesthatmove.sentence_pattern: Its [body covering] helps protect it from [danger].
4::homesthatmove.teaching_focus: Students substitute the animal, the body covering, and the two dangers. Practice: 'A turtle's shell protects it from predators and cold water.' / 'A hedgehog's spines protect it from foxes and rough ground.' / 'A crab's claws protect it from bigger fish and strong waves.' Focus: possessive [animal]'s + [body part] + protects + it + from + noun + and + noun. Teaches the verb 'protects' with 'from', possessive apostrophe, and listing two nouns with 'and'.
4::homesthatmove.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::homesthatmove.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Do all animals stay in one place? (No — some carry their homes)
• What does a turtle have on its back? (A hard shell)
• What does the snail's shell protect it from? (Rain and hungry animals)
• Where do hermit crabs find their shells? (Near the sea — they find empty shells)
• What do hermit crabs do when they grow bigger? (They look for larger shells to move into)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is the main idea of this passage? (Some animals carry their homes with them)
• Name three animals that carry their homes. (Turtle, snail, hermit crab)
• What detail supports the idea that a shell is important for protection?

3. Reading Strategy:
• What did you already know about turtles or snails before reading?
• What new information surprised you in this passage?
• What question do you still have about animals that carry their homes?

4. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the most important idea in this passage?
• (Compare) How is the turtle's shell different from the snail's shell?
• (Vocabulary) What is a 'hermit crab'? Find the sentence where it first appears.
• (Inference) Why do you think hermit crabs don't make their own shells?
• (Connection) Can you think of another animal that uses something for protection?
4::homesthatmove.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Name two animals from this passage that carry their homes. (Turtle and snail — or hermit crab.)
2. (On) How does a turtle's shell protect it? (It keeps the turtle safe from danger; when afraid, the turtle hides inside it.)
3. (On) How is a snail's shell different from a turtle's shell? (The snail's shell is smaller and lighter.)
4. (On) What do hermit crabs do differently from turtles and snails? (They don't make their own shells — they find empty shells near the sea and move into them.)
5. (Above) Why do you think it is useful for an animal to carry its home on its body? (Because it always has protection and shelter wherever it goes, without needing to find a new safe place each time it moves.)
4::homesthatmove.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
4::homesthatmove.go_description: 中心为主旨「Animals Carry Their Homes」，三支分别记录 Turtle（hard shell / protects from danger / hides inside）、Snail（smaller lighter shell / protects from rain and predators）、Hermit Crab（finds empty shells / moves to larger shells when it grows）
4::homesthatmove.go_usage: 中心写 "Animal Homes That Move"，分支列出乌龟、蜗牛和寄居蟹的移动式家园特点

4::whatshouldiweartoday.title: What Should I Wear Today?
4::whatshouldiweartoday.teaching_focus: Practice giving reasons with "When the weather is [adj], choose [item] because [reason]" — combining weather and clothes vocabulary with causal reasoning.
4::whatshouldiweartoday.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "fabric" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "casual" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "striped" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::whatshouldiweartoday.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What fabric should you choose on a hot, sunny day? (A light fabric such as cotton or linen)
2. (On) Why should you wear bright or striped clothing on a foggy day? (So drivers can see you more easily and keep you safe)
3. (On) What is the difference between casual and formal clothing? (Casual is relaxed and everyday; formal is smart and worn for special occasions)
4. (On) Why is checking the weather forecast every morning a useful habit? (So you can dress appropriately and stay comfortable and safe all day)
5. (Above) Design an outfit for the worst weather in your city. What would you wear and why? (Accept any creative response with a clear reason)
4::whatshouldiweartoday.go_description: 左圆 Hot weather clothing / 右圆 Cold weather clothing / 交集 All-weather tips; 每圆填2-3个要点，交集填"check fabric, bright colours"
4::whatshouldiweartoday.go_usage: 用韦恩图对比冷热天穿衣建议，找出通用原则，练习because因果句

4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.title: Helpful Animals on the Farm
4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.teaching_focus: Use 'could not stop' + verb-ing to talk about repeated or uncontrollable actions and strong feelings; practice with different subjects and common verbs for speaking and short writing.
4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What could Kelly not find one sunny day? (The feed bag)
• What did the dog do to help? (Sniffed around happily)
• Where was the feed bag hidden? (Under a bush near the old wheelbarrow)
• What was missing from Kelly's foot? (Her left shoe)
• Who had chewed the shoe? (The silly goat)

2. Reading Skill:
• Who found the feed bag first? (The goat, near the wheelbarrow)
• Name two animals that helped Kelly search. (The dog and the cat)
• Why could Kelly not stop laughing? (Because the goat had chewed her shoe)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the goat chewed the shoe? • (Vocabulary) What does clever mean? • (Feelings) How did Kelly feel when she found her shoe was missing? • (Connection) Have you ever had a pet or animal do something silly? What happened?
4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where was the feed bag hidden? (Under a bush near the old wheelbarrow)
2. (On) What did the dog do to help Kelly search? (Sniffed around happily)
3. (On) What was missing from Kelly's foot? (Her left shoe)
4. (On) Who had chewed the shoe? (The silly goat)
5. (Above) Why do you think Kelly could not stop laughing at the end? (Because the goat helped her but also chewed her shoe / it was funny)
4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.go_description: 上方写 Characters（Kelly, dog, cat, goat）和 Setting（small farm, sunny day）；中间写 Problem（Feed bag is missing）；下方写 Solution（Goat found the bag under the bush）和 Twist（Goat also chewed the shoe）
4::helpfulanimalsonthefarm.go_usage: 引导学生梳理故事的搜寻过程和结局转折，练习 'could not stop [verb]-ing' 句型，体会动物的可爱与幽默

4::wildanimalshelp.title: Wild Animals Help
4::wildanimalshelp.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::wildanimalshelp.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What do bees help grow? (Much of our food)
• What do elephants spread through forests? (Seeds)
• How do wolves help deer herds? (By hunting weak animals)
• What do vultures eat? (Dead animals)
• Who is still learning new things about wild animals? (Scientists)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two animals that help plants grow. (Bees and birds)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Wild animals help people and nature in many ways)
• What happens if people protect forests and rivers? (Animals can continue helping the world)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) How do wild animals help the world? • (Inference) Why is it important to protect forests and rivers? • (Vocabulary) What does pollinate mean? • (Fact) Name one animal and what it does for nature. • (Connection) Which wild animal do you think is the most helpful? Why?
4::wildanimalshelp.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What do bees help grow? (Much of our food)
2. (On) How do wolves help deer herds? (By hunting weak animals)
3. (On) What do vultures eat to help keep forests clean? (Dead animals)
4. (On) What is the main idea of the book? (Wild animals help people and nature in many ways)
5. (Above) Why is it important for people to protect forests and rivers? (So animals can continue helping the world and keeping nature balanced)
4::wildanimalshelp.graphic_organizer: Main Idea & Details Web (主旨细节网)
4::wildanimalshelp.go_description: 中心写 Wild Animals Help Us；向外辐射五个分支，各写一种动物及其帮助方式：Bees（pollinate plants）, Elephants（spread seeds）, Wolves（keep herds healthy）, Vultures（clean forests）, Birds（spread seeds far away）
4::wildanimalshelp.go_usage: 引导学生从文中找出每种动物的具体贡献，用主旨细节网归纳，练习 '… help … by [verb]-ing …' 句型替换

4::theboywhocodedaworld.title: The Boy Who Coded a World
4::theboywhocodedaworld.teaching_focus: Practice describing creative use of technology with "used [tool] to [result]", encouraging students to express their own tech-driven ideas.
4::theboywhocodedaworld.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::theboywhocodedaworld.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "code" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "virtual" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "program" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::theboywhocodedaworld.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What did Tommy build inside his computer? (A virtual town with schools, shops, and robots)
2. (On) What happened when Tommy added music to the town? (A bug caused all the robots to play the same song at the same time)
3. (On) What does "debug" mean? (To find and fix a mistake in a computer program)
4. (On) What did Tommy use his coding skills to achieve? (He built a complete virtual world with a school, shops, dancing robots, and a tiny weather system)
5. (Above) What personal qualities do you think helped Tommy succeed at coding? (Accept: patience / determination / creativity / willingness to fix mistakes)
4::theboywhocodedaworld.go_description: 中心写主角名 Leo，外圈写3-4个性格特质（creative, determined, curious, patient），每个特质连一条文中证据
4::theboywhocodedaworld.go_usage: 引导学生从行为和对话中推断人物特质，讨论科技创造力与个人品质的关系

4::alostpuppy.title: A Lost Puppy
4::alostpuppy.teaching_focus: Practice past simple with time phrases, word order (subject + verb + object + place), and telling short past events. Good for substitution drills and speaking/writing simple stories.
4::alostpuppy.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::alostpuppy.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did Mia find near the bakery? (A small puppy)
• What did the puppy wear? (A tiny red collar)
2. Reading for Inference:
• Why did Mia write a note on the fence? (To find the owner / help the puppy get home)
4::alostpuppy.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where did Mia find the puppy? (Near the bakery)
2. (On) Why do you think the woman cried when she saw the puppy?
3. (Beyond) What does this story tell us about helping others?
4::alostpuppy.go_description: 上方写 Character（Mia, puppy, woman）和 Setting（neighborhood, bakery, fence）；中间写 Problem（lost puppy, no one knows the owner）；下方写 Solution（Mia writes a note → owner comes）
4::alostpuppy.go_usage: 引导学生梳理故事的起因、经过与结局，练习 '[Time] + past simple' 叙事句型，讨论如何在社区中帮助他人

4::amazinglysmartanimals.title: Amazingly Smart Animals
4::amazinglysmartanimals.teaching_focus: Practice the structure 'learn to + verb +. . . with + noun' to talk about skills, tools, and ways of learning. Use it for substitution drills (different subjects, verbs, and instruments) and short speaking or writing exercises.
4::amazinglysmartanimals.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What do dogs learn to do with soft brushes? (Paint)
• How many words can parrots learn? (Up to fifty)
• What do crows make? (Simple tools)
• How long does it take an octopus to solve a puzzle? (Just a few minutes)
• What do bees do to show the direction of food? (Dance)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two animals that use tools or solve problems. (Crows and octopuses)
• How do people teach pets new tricks? (Using small treats)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Many animals show amazing skills and intelligence)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do scientists study animal intelligence? • (Vocabulary) What does direction mean? • (Fact or Opinion) Is "animals are smarter than people" a fact or an opinion? • (Connection) Which animal from the book do you think is the smartest? Why?
4::amazinglysmartanimals.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What do bees do to show other bees where food is? (Dance)
2. (On) How many words can parrots learn? (Up to fifty)
3. (On) Name two animals that use tools or solve problems. (Crows and octopuses)
4. (On) How do people teach pets new tricks? (Using small treats)
5. (Above) Why do you think scientists study how smart animals are? (To learn new ideas from animals and understand the world better)
4::amazinglysmartanimals.go_description: 左列标题 Animal，右列标题 Smart Skill；填入文中各动物及对应技能：Dog（paint with brushes）, Parrot（copy sounds, learn words）, Crow（make simple tools）, Octopus（solve puzzles）, Bee（dance to show direction）, Horse（play with balls）, Bird（solve number tests）
4::amazinglysmartanimals.go_usage: 引导学生从文中提取动物技能信息，填入图表，练习 '… learn to … with …' 句型，区分文中事实与意见

4::thebestdealintown.title: The Best Deal in Town
4::thebestdealintown.teaching_focus: Practice expressing deliberate change with "decided to [action] instead of [habit]" — contrasting improved behavior with past mistakes.
4::thebestdealintown.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "receipt" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "refund" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "bargain" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::thebestdealintown.rr_questions:
1. (Below) Why could Tommy not get a refund for the notebook? (He had no receipt)
2. (On) What did Mia explain a receipt is for? (It is proof that you paid — without it, the shop cannot help you)
3. (On) What bargain did Tommy find on his second visit? (Two notebooks for the price of one)
4. (On) What did Tommy decide to do differently after his mistake? (He decided to keep the receipt instead of throwing it away)
5. (Above) What lesson did Tommy learn that day that goes beyond shopping? (Accept: always keep proof of what you do / mistakes are valuable if you learn from them)
4::thebestdealintown.go_description: 两轮：Round 1 Problem→Action→Result(fail). Round 2 Problem→Action→Result(success). 最后加Lesson Learned一行
4::thebestdealintown.go_usage: 对比Tommy两次购物的行为，用"He decided to…instead of…"描述改变

4::miasrobot.title: Mia's Robot
4::miasrobot.teaching_focus: Practice 'could' + base verb to talk about ability, join two actions with 'and', and place the adverb 'easily' at the end; good for substitution drills and speaking practice.
4::miasrobot.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::miasrobot.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Where did Anna build her robot? (In her garage)
• What could the robot do? (Carry tools and open doors easily)
• What was missing from the robot? (The battery)
• Where did Anna find the battery? (Inside a bird's nest in a tree)
• What did the robot do when it started working again? (Blinked its lights and started singing a silly song)

2. Reading Skill:
• What told Anna the battery might be in a tree? (She remembered a bird carrying something shiny)
• Why did Anna check every part carefully? (She could not find the problem)
• How did Anna get the battery back? (She climbed the tree carefully)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) How do you think Anna felt when the robot started working again? • (Vocabulary) What does battery mean? • (Feelings) How did Anna feel when the robot stopped working? • (Connection) Have you ever fixed something that was broken? What did you do?
4::miasrobot.rr_questions:
1. (Under) Where did Anna build her robot? (In her garage)
2. (On) What was missing from the robot? (The battery)
3. (On) Where did Anna find the battery? (Inside a bird's nest in a tree)
4. (On) What told Anna the battery might be in a tree? (She remembered a bird carrying something shiny)
5. (Above) How do you think Anna felt when the robot started working again? Why? (Happy and relieved, because she worked hard to find the battery and fix it)
4::miasrobot.go_description: 左框写 Problem（The robot stopped working; the battery was missing）；中框写 Actions（Checked every part; found a bird took the battery; climbed the tree）；右框写 Solution（Got the battery back; the robot worked again）
4::miasrobot.go_usage: 引导学生追踪 Anna 的推理过程，练习 'could … and … easily' 句型，理解机器人依赖电池运作的基本科学常识

4::digitaldevicesaroundus.title: Digital Devices Around Us
4::digitaldevicesaroundus.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::digitaldevicesaroundus.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Name three digital devices from the book. (Phones, tablets, and laptops)
• What does the screen show? (Pictures and words)
• What does the battery do? (Gives power to the device)
• What do cameras take? (Photos and videos)
• What do schools use technology for? (Lessons and practice)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is the difference between cameras and speakers? (Cameras take photos and videos; speakers play sounds and music)
• How has technology changed what people need? (One smartphone can now do many things at once)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Digital devices have many parts and have changed how people live)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do schools use technology today? • (Vocabulary) What does communicate mean? • (Fact) Name one part of a digital device and what it does. • (Connection) What digital device do you use most? What do you use it for?
4::digitaldevicesaroundus.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What does the screen show? (Pictures and words)
2. (On) What does the battery do? (Gives power to the device)
3. (On) What is the difference between cameras and speakers? (Cameras take photos and videos; speakers play sounds and music)
4. (On) What is the main idea of the book? (Digital devices have many parts and have changed how people live)
5. (Above) How has technology changed the way children learn and play? (They can use one device to do many things, like study, play, and talk to friends)
4::digitaldevicesaroundus.go_description: 画一个简单的平板电脑轮廓，向外引出标注线，分别标注各部件及功能：Screen（shows pictures and words）, Battery（gives power）, Camera（takes photos and videos）, Speaker（plays sounds and music）, Port（connects charger）, Touchscreen（controls device）
4::digitaldevicesaroundus.go_usage: 引导学生用标注图了解设备各部件功能，练习 '…, while …' 对比句型，联系生活中使用数码设备的经验

4::miashealthdiary.title: Mia's Health Diary
4::miashealthdiary.teaching_focus: Practice indirect speech with "The doctor explained that..." — reporting health information accurately, a key KET writing structure.
4::miashealthdiary.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::miashealthdiary.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "symptom" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "checkup" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "prescription" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::miashealthdiary.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What was Mia dreading about her checkup? (Getting a needle / an injection)
2. (On) What three symptoms did Mia describe to the nurse? (Tired eyes, headaches every afternoon, and trouble sleeping)
3. (On) What was written on Mia's prescription? (Sleep eight hours, go outside for thirty minutes, and turn screens off one hour before bed)
4. (On) Why did the doctor say rest was more important than medicine? (Because routine and sleep fix the root cause of the symptoms, not just the effects)
5. (Above) Why do you think many people expect a pill when they go to the doctor? (Accept: people think medicine is the fastest solution / they do not realize that habits can be the real cure)
4::miashealthdiary.go_description: K列: Mia knew before (checkup = needle, she feels unwell). W列: What she wanted to know (why she feels bad). L列: What she learned (screen time, no shot, routine = recovery)
4::miashealthdiary.go_usage: 从Mia的视角填KWL，讨论"了解事实可以减少恐惧"，找出文中回答了W列哪些问题

4::thesciencefairsurprise.title: The Science Fair Surprise
4::thesciencefairsurprise.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'tried' + to-infinitive + 'it' + 'with' + instrument. Good for talking about attempts, tools and short past actions; students can swap subjects and items for speaking practice.
4::thesciencefairsurprise.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thesciencefairsurprise.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What was Tommy building for the science fair? (A paper volcano)
• What did the robot drop? (Glitter)
• What did the glitter do to the volcano? (Blocked the vents)
• What did Tommy use to try to fix it? (Vinegar and baking soda)
• Who helped Tommy fix the problem? (Mia)

2. Reading Skill:
• Why did the eruption not start at first? (The glitter blocked the vents)
• How did the crowd react when the volcano finally worked? (They cheered and clapped loudly)
• What did the principal do at the end? (Smiled at their success)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Tommy felt nervous at the start? • (Vocabulary) What does eruption mean? • (Feelings) How did the crowd feel when the volcano burst? • (Connection) Have you ever had a plan go wrong? What did you do to fix it?
4::thesciencefairsurprise.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did the glitter do to Tommy's volcano? (Blocked the vents)
2. (On) What was Tommy building for the science fair? (A paper volcano)
3. (On) What did Tommy use to try to fix the problem? (Vinegar and baking soda)
4. (On) How did the crowd react when the volcano finally worked? (They cheered and clapped loudly)
5. (Above) Why do you think the principal smiled at the end of the story? (Because Tommy and Mia did not give up and solved the problem together)
4::thesciencefairsurprise.go_description: 左框写 Problem（Glitter blocked the volcano vents; eruption would not start）；中框写 Actions（Tommy tried vinegar and baking soda; Mia helped patiently）；右框写 Solution（Volcano burst with fizz and foam; crowd cheered）
4::thesciencefairsurprise.go_usage: 引导学生梳理科学展上的意外与解决过程，练习 '… tried to fix it with …' 句型，了解火山喷发实验的基本原理（醋与小苏打反应产生气泡）

4::flyingmachines.title: Flying Machines
4::flyingmachines.teaching_focus: Practice 'use + object + to + verb' to talk about purpose. Focus on subject vocabulary (people/jobs), tools, and simple action verbs. Activities: substitution drills, ask-and-answer pairs (Who uses X? What do they use X for?), and short speaking tasks describing tools and their uses.
4::flyingmachines.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• How many rotors do many drones have? (Four)
• What do pilots use to fly drones? (Remote controllers)
• How long can most drones fly today? (About thirty minutes)
• What do delivery drones carry? (Light packages to homes)
• How fast can race drones go? (Over 100 kilometres per hour)

2. Reading Skill:
• Name two ways scientists use drones. (To count animals in national parks and to help researchers in hard-to-reach places)
• What do survey drones do? (Map areas of several square kilometres)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Drones are useful flying machines used in many different ways)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think scientists use drones instead of going on foot? • (Vocabulary) What does remote mean? • (Fact) Name one job that uses drones. • (Connection) What would you use a drone for if you had one? Why?
4::flyingmachines.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How many rotors do many drones have? (Four)
2. (On) What do pilots use to fly drones? (Remote controllers)
3. (On) What do delivery drones carry? (Light packages to homes)
4. (On) Name two ways scientists use drones. (To count animals in parks and to work in hard-to-reach places)
5. (Above) Why do you think scientists use drones instead of going on foot to count animals? (Drones are faster and safer; they do not disturb the animals)
4::flyingmachines.go_description: 顶层写 Drones: Flying Machines；下方分四个分支，按用途分类：Delivery（carry packages to homes）, Survey（map areas）, Science（count animals, help researchers）, Education（schools teach students to build）；底部注明共同特征：four rotors, battery, camera, remote controller
4::flyingmachines.go_usage: 引导学生按用途对无人机进行分类，练习 '[people] use [tool] to [do something]' 句型，联系现实中无人机的应用场景

4::thelifecycleofastar.title: The Life Cycle of a Star
4::thelifecycleofastar.teaching_focus: Practice describing change over time using "over [time], something changes from... to..." for scientific processes.
4::thelifecycleofastar.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "nebula" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "gravity" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "supernova" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::thelifecycleofastar.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What are stars made of when they first form? (Clouds of gas and dust pulled together by gravity)
2. (On) What is a supernova? (A huge explosion that happens when a large star runs out of energy and dies)
3. (On) What can a star become after it collapses following a supernova? (A black hole or a white dwarf)
4. (On) How does a new star cycle begin again after a supernova? (The leftover gas and dust form a new cloud, and gravity pulls it together to make a new star)
5. (Above) What does the life cycle of a star tell us about the universe? (Accept: everything in the universe changes over time / matter is recycled / nothing lasts forever)
4::thelifecycleofastar.go_description: Nebula → Protostar → Main Sequence Star → Red Giant → Supernova → Black Hole/White Dwarf
4::thelifecycleofastar.go_usage: 用流程图帮助学生记忆恒星生命周期的各个阶段，联系文章中每段对应的阶段描述

4::thedreamfairy.title: The Dream Fairy
4::thedreamfairy.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'woke up' + 'feeling' to describe emotions or physical/mental states; students can change the subject and the adjectives to make their own sentences and speak about personal experiences.
4::thedreamfairy.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened to the village at the start of the story? (It forgot how to dream)
• What did the old woman say happened? (The village fairy had lost her song)
• Who walked into the forest to help? (A brave girl named Mina)
• What did the dragon show Mina? (A large oak tree)
• What did Mina find under the tree roots? (A tiny silver bell)

2. Reading Skill:
• How did the dragon help Mina? (It showed her the large oak tree)
• What happened after Mina rang the bell? (Beautiful music filled the air and the fairy returned)
• How did the story end? (Everyone had a wonderful dream that night)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the dragon helped Mina? • (Vocabulary) What does brave mean? • (Feelings) How did the villagers feel at the end? • (Connection) If you could go on a quest like Mina's, what would you look for?
4::thedreamfairy.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did Mina find under the tree roots? (A tiny silver bell)
2. (On) Who walked into the forest to help? (A brave girl named Mina)
3. (On) What happened after Mina rang the bell? (Beautiful music filled the air and the fairy returned)
4. (On) How did the people feel when they woke up at the start? (Strange and empty)
5. (Above) Why do you think the dragon helped Mina find the bell? (Because it wanted the village to be happy again / it was kind)
4::thedreamfairy.go_description: 上方写 Characters（Mina, dragon, fairy, villagers）和 Setting（village, forest, oak tree）；中间写 Problem（Village forgot how to dream; fairy lost her song）；下方写 Solution（Mina found the silver bell; rang it）和 Ending（Fairy returned; everyone dreamed again）
4::thedreamfairy.go_usage: 引导学生边视觉化奇幻场景边填写故事结构图，练习 'woke up feeling …' 句型，体会故事中勇气与善良的主题

4::fablesandfolktales.title: Fables and Folk Tales
4::fablesandfolktales.sentence_pattern: People have shared [things] for a long time.
4::fablesandfolktales.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• How long have fables and folk tales been shared? (For thousands of years)
• What animals do fables often use? (Talking animals)
• What do folk tales tell stories about? (Daily life, traditions, and old customs)
• What does the goat in the fable try to become? (A judge)
• Who still shares fables and folk tales today? (Teachers in classrooms)

2. Reading Skill:
• What is one difference between a fable and a folk tale? (Fables use animals to teach lessons; folk tales are about daily life)
• How did stories travel from country to country? (Families shared them together)
• What is the main idea of the book? (Fables and folk tales teach lessons and have been shared for thousands of years)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the book mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think fables use animals instead of people? • (Vocabulary) What does custom mean? • (Fact) Name one thing fables and folk tales have in common. • (Connection) Do you know a fable or folk tale? What lesson does it teach?
4::fablesandfolktales.rr_questions:
1. (Under) How long have fables and folk tales been shared? (For thousands of years)
2. (On) What do folk tales tell stories about? (Daily life, traditions, and old customs)
3. (On) What is one difference between a fable and a folk tale? (Fables use talking animals to teach lessons; folk tales are about daily life)
4. (On) Who still shares these stories in classrooms today? (Teachers)
5. (Above) Why do you think fables use animals instead of people to teach lessons? (Animals make the story fun and easy to remember / children enjoy animal characters)
4::fablesandfolktales.go_description: 左列标题 Fables，右列标题 Folk Tales；各填入文中特征：Fables（talking animals, teach a lesson, goat-judge example）；Folk Tales（daily life, traditions, old customs, travel between countries）；底部写 Both（shared for thousands of years, still used in classrooms today）
4::fablesandfolktales.go_usage: 引导学生用对比图区分寓言与民间故事的特点，联系已学过的故事，练习 '… have been shared for … years' 句型

4::miasartshow.title: Mia's Art Show
4::miasartshow.teaching_focus: Practice expressing realizations and discoveries using "realized that" to report a change of understanding in a narrative.
4::miasartshow.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::miasartshow.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What happened at the beginning of the story? (Accept reasonable summary)
• Who are the main characters in this story? (Accept character names from text)
• What problem did the characters face? (Accept key conflict from text)
• How did the characters solve the problem? (Accept resolution from text)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find the word "masterpiece" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find the word "auction" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "restore" in a sentence of your own. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What was your favourite part of the story? Why?
• What would you do if you were the main character?
• What lesson can we learn from this story?
4::miasartshow.rr_questions:
1. (Below) Where was Mia at the start of the story? (At the school art show)
2. (On) Why did Mia almost leave before showing her painting? (She thought her painting was too simple compared to the others)
3. (On) What did the art teacher say about Mia's painting? (That it was a true masterpiece — honest and full of feeling)
4. (On) What does "restore" mean in this text? (To clean and repair something old so it looks as good as new)
5. (Above) What does this story teach us about comparing our work to others? (Accept: comparing can make us doubt our own value / simplicity and honesty in art can be more powerful than complexity)
4::miasartshow.go_description: Setting / Characters / Problem / 4 Events / Resolution — 重点标注转折点（发现画作真实价值的那一刻）
4::miasartshow.go_usage: 引导学生思考故事的转折点，以及文中哪些细节暗示了结局

4::thecrowandthepitcher.title: The Crow and the Pitcher
4::thecrowandthepitcher.teaching_focus: Practice past simple 'tried to' + action and use 'but' to show contrast or failure; good for substitution and speaking drills.
4::thecrowandthepitcher.reading_strategy: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thecrowandthepitcher.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did the crow see in the yard? (A pitcher with a little water)
• Why could the crow not drink at first? (She could not reach the water)
• What did the crow pick up with her beak? (Small stones near the fence)
• What happened to the water after each stone fell in? (It rose a little)
• Who watched the crow from the yard wall? (A boy)

2. Reading Skill:
• How did the crow solve her problem? (She dropped stones into the pitcher to raise the water)
• How did the crow feel after she drank? (Much better)
• How did the crow look when she flew away? (Proud)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think the boy was amazed? • (Vocabulary) What does pitcher mean? • (Feelings) How did the crow feel when she finally drank the water? • (Connection) Can you think of a time you used something simple to solve a problem?
4::thecrowandthepitcher.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did the crow drop into the pitcher? (Small stones)
2. (On) Why could the crow not drink at first? (She could not reach the water)
3. (On) What happened to the water after each stone fell in? (It rose a little)
4. (On) How did the crow look when she flew away? (Proud)
5. (Above) Why do you think the boy was amazed when he watched the crow? (Because the crow was very clever and used stones to solve her problem)
4::thecrowandthepitcher.go_description: 左框写 Problem（Crow was thirsty; could not reach the water in the pitcher）；中框写 Steps（Picked up stones; dropped them one by one into the pitcher; water rose slowly）；右框写 Solution（Water reached her beak; she drank and felt better）
4::thecrowandthepitcher.go_usage: 引导学生理解乌鸦用物理方法（投石升水）解决问题的逻辑，练习 '… tried to … but …' 句型，讨论遇到困难时运用创造力的重要性

4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.title: The Goose That Laid the Golden Eggs
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.teaching_focus: Practice expressing preferences and contrasts with 'wanted. . . instead of. . .'. Students can swap the objects or actions (nouns or -ing verbs) to make many simple sentences and talk about choices and consequences.
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.reading_skill: 推断与结论 (Make Inferences and Draw Conclusions)
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• What did the goose lay every morning? (A golden egg)
• What did Tom do with the eggs? (Sold them in town)
• Why did Tom become greedy? (He wanted all the gold at once)
• What did Tom believe was inside the goose? (A lot of gold)
• What did Tom find inside the goose? (Nothing special at all)

2. Reading Skill:
• How did Tom feel at first when he had the goose? (Very happy and thankful)
• What happened after Tom killed the goose? (He had no goose and no more golden eggs)
• What lesson did Tom learn? (That greed can make people lose everything)

3. Other Comprehension:
• (Main Idea) What is the story mostly about? • (Inference) Why do you think Tom felt ashamed at the end? • (Vocabulary) What does greedy mean? • (Feelings) How did Tom feel at the end of the story? • (Connection) The story says greed can make people lose everything. Do you agree? Can you give an example?
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.rr_questions:
1. (Under) What did Tom find inside the goose? (Nothing special at all)
2. (On) What did Tom do with the golden eggs? (Sold them in town)
3. (On) Why did Tom kill the goose? (He believed there was a lot of gold inside)
4. (On) What lesson did Tom learn? (That greed can make people lose everything)
5. (Above) Why do you think Tom felt ashamed at the end? (Because he was greedy and lost the goose and all future eggs)
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.go_description: 三行因果链：Cause（The goose laid one golden egg each day）→ Effect（Tom sold eggs and became rich）；Cause（Tom became greedy and wanted all the gold）→ Effect（He killed the goose）；Cause（He killed the goose）→ Effect（He found nothing; lost the goose and all future eggs）
4::thegoosethatlaidthegoldeneggs.go_usage: 引导学生用因果图追踪贪婪带来的后果，练习 'wanted … instead of …' 句型，讨论知足常乐的道理

4::amorninginmyneighborhood.title: A Morning in My Neighborhood
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.teaching_focus: Practice expressing community value with "Without [person], we could not..." — extending conditional vocabulary and building appreciation for daily-life services.
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.reading_skill: 主旨与支撑细节 (Main Idea and Supporting Details)
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.questions_type:
1. Reading for Details:
• Who are the main characters and where does the story take place? (Accept names + setting from P1)
• What is the main problem or event? (Accept key event from text)
• What was surprising about this story? (Accept surprise/twist)
• How does the story end? (Accept resolution)

2. Vocabulary in Context:
• Find "pharmacy" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Find "routine" in the text. What does it mean? (Accept definition)
• Use "errand" in your own sentence. (Accept original sentence)

3. Personal Response:
• What surprised you most in this story?
• What would you have done if you were the main character?
• What does this story make you think about?
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.rr_questions:
1. (Below) What time does the baker start work? (5:00 a.m.)
2. (On) Why does the bus driver's strict schedule matter to the community? (So that children, workers, and elderly people all arrive on time)
3. (On) What is a pharmacy, and why is it important in a neighborhood? (A shop where you collect medicine; without it, families cannot get the right medicine quickly)
4. (On) What does "routine" mean in this text? (A regular pattern of work done at the same time every day)
5. (Above) Why do you think many community workers go unnoticed by most people? (Accept: they work early or quietly / people take daily services for granted / we only notice things when they stop working)
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.graphic_organizer: Fact File / Profile Cards (人物档案卡)
4::amorninginmyneighborhood.go_description: 4张档案卡：Time | Person | Job | "Without [person], we could not..."; Baker/Bus driver/Pharmacist/Street cleaner
