1::mytwopets.core_vocab_raw: small, cute, play, love, feed, wiggle, nibble, cuddle
1::mytwopets.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on pets → /p/ /e/ /t/ /s/
Blend and segment orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on my.
Read in phrase: “My pets…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what the pets do.”

During Reading:

Prompt Students to Decode (NOT Guess)
Focus on run / play / feed.
Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

Text-Based Questions (Actions)
“What do the pets do?”
Students identify actions (run, play, cuddle, feed).

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“My pets ___.”
Students describe actions.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show run/play/cuddle.”
Students act out actions.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Do you have a pet?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on two / pets.
Identify long oo and short e patterns.
1::mytwopets.reading_strategy: Picture walk; echo reading; pet-action match
1::mytwopets.questions_type: 1. How many pets does the child have? 2. Where do the pets run? 3. What do the pets play with? 4. What does the child do every day? 5. Which words describe the pets? (skill) 6. What is the main idea? (skill) 7. Do YOU have a pet or want one?
1::mytwopets.answers: 1. Two. 2. Around the house. 3. Each other. 4. Feed them. 5. Cute/small/best friends. 6. Pets bring joy and are loved. 7. I have/want a ___.

1::thinkingbrain.title: Thinking Brain
1::thinkingbrain.reader_text: “My brain helps me to think. My brain helps me to learn. My brain helps me to talk. My brain helps me to play. My brain is very powerful. My brain works hard every day. I like using my smart brain.”
1::thinkingbrain.objective: Build foundational science understanding of the brain as a helper; develop metacognition (“how I learn”).
1::thinkingbrain.sor:
Before Reading:

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: brain, think, learn, talk.
Use visuals and simple examples.

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on brain → /b/ /r/ /ai/ /n/
Blend sounds orally.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what the brain helps us do.”

During Reading:

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build rhythm with “My brain helps me…”

Text-Based Questions (Function)
“What does the brain help you do?”
Students identify actions.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on brain / think.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“My brain helps me ___.”
Students express actions.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show think/learn/talk/play.”
Students act out meanings.

Identify the Central Idea
“What does the brain do?”
Students explain simply.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on brain / think.
Identify ai vowel team and th digraph.
1::thinkingbrain.questions_type: 1. What helps you think? 2. What helps you learn? 3. What helps you talk? 4. What does the brain do every day? 5. Which words describe the brain? (skill) 6. What is the main idea? (skill) 7. When do YOU use your brain?
1::thinkingbrain.answers: 1. My brain. 2. My brain. 3. My brain. 4. Works hard. 5. Powerful/smart. 6. The brain helps us do many things. 7. I use my brain when I ___.

1::countryrain.title: Country Rain
1::countryrain.reader_text: “It rains on the old barn. It rains on the dry hay. It rains on the brown cows. It rains all day long. The country is quiet and peaceful. The soft rain feels calm. I like rainy days in the country.”

1::countryrain.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on rain / country.
Read and blend ai and ou patterns.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on on.
Read in phrase: “It rains on…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find where the rain falls.”

During Reading:

Finger Tracking for Print Concepts
Students point to each word.
Reinforce left-to-right reading.

Text-Based Questions (Visualization)
“Where does it rain?”
Students name barn, hay, cows.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“It rains on ___.”
Students describe locations.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show barn/hay/cows.”
Students point or act out.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Where do you see rain?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on rain / country.

1::countryrain.questions_type: 1. What does the rain fall on? 2. What color are the cows? 3. How long does it rain? 4. How does the country feel? 5. Which words help you see and feel the rain? (skill) 6. What is the setting? (skill) 7. What do YOU like about rainy days?

1::farmlife.title: Farm Life
1::farmlife.reader_text: “I see big, brown cows eating. I see fat, yellow ducklings sleeping. I see strong, fast horses running. I see white chickens pecking. Farm life is very busy. There is always something to do. I like watching animals on the farm.”

1::farmlife.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on farm → /f/ /ar/ /m/
Blend sounds orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on see.
Read in phrase: “I see…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what animals do.”

During Reading:

Prompt Students to Decode (NOT Guess)
Focus on run / peck / sleep.
Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

Text-Based Questions (Actions)
“What does the animal do?”
Students identify eat, sleep, run, peck.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I see ___ ___.”
Students describe animal + action.

“Show eat/sleep/run/peck.”
Students act out actions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on farm / life.

1::farmlife.reading_skill: Classifying animals by actions; describing features
1::farmlife.questions_type: 1. What are the cows doing? 2. What are the ducklings doing? 3. What are the horses doing? 4. What are the chickens doing? 5. Which words describe animal actions? (skill) 6. How is the farm busy? (skill) 7. What farm animal would YOU like to see?

1::boatsonthewater.title: Boats on the Water
1::boatsonthewater.reader_text: “I see a white sailboat sailing. I see a very big ship. I see a small, red boat. I see a long, gray ferry. Boats are on the water. They move in different ways. I like to watch them float.”

1::boatsonthewater.sor:
Before Reading:

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: sailboat, ship, boat.
Use visuals and simple examples.

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on boat / water.
Read and blend oa and r-controlled er.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find different boats.”

Text-Based Questions (Comparison)
“Which boat is big? Which is small?”

Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build smooth phrasing.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on boat / ship.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

1::afterschoolfun.title: After School Fun
1::afterschoolfun.reader_text: “After school, I read a book. I eat a yummy, healthy snack. I play outside with my friends. Then I rest at my home. After school is the best. I feel happy after my day. I like my after-school time.”
1::afterschoolfun.objective: Build daily routine vocabulary; reinforce sequence (read → eat → play → rest); develop SEL reflection.
1::afterschoolfun.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on school / fun.
Read and blend long oo and short u.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on I.
Read in phrase: “I read. I play.”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what we do after school.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence)
“What do we do first?”
Students track read → eat → play → rest.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build smooth phrasing.

Monitor Understanding (Feelings)
“How do they feel?”

1::animalroadsigns.title: Animal Road Signs
1::animalroadsigns.reader_text: “Look for deer crossing the road. Look for ducks on the road. Look for cows near the road. Please drive very slow here. These signs keep animals safe. Drivers must watch carefully. We help animals when we slow down.”
1::animalroadsigns.objective: Build safety vocabulary; connect environment signs to meaning; develop inferencing about rules.

Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on road → /r/ /ō/ /d/
Blend sounds orally.

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: cross, slow, drive, sign.

“Let’s find how signs help animals stay safe.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Meaning & Inference)

Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“Look for ___ on the road.”
Students describe signs.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show slow/careful/stop/look.”
Students act out actions.

Identify the Central Idea
“Why do we slow down?”
Students explain safety.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on road / sign.
Identify long oa and silent e patterns.

1::animalroadsigns.questions_type: 1. What animals might cross the road? 2. Why must drivers go slow? 3. What do signs help do? 4. Who must watch carefully? 5. Which words show a rule? (skill) 6. What is the purpose of road signs? (skill) 7. What animals do YOU see near roads?
1::animalroadsigns.answers: 1. Deer/ducks/cows. 2. To keep animals safe. 3. Keep animals safe. 4. Drivers. 5. Drive slow/look for. 6. To warn and protect. 7. I see ___.
1::animalroadsigns.extension: Make road-sign cards; animal-safety role play; matching activity
1::animalroadsigns.phonics_rule: long oa (road), long i (sign silent e), plural -s

1::dressuptime.title: Dress Up Time
1::dressuptime.reader_text: “Today I am a black cat. Now I am a funny clown. Then I am a silver robot. Later I am a white duck. Dress-up is so much fun. I like trying new costumes. I laugh when I dress up.”
1::dressuptime.objective: Build imaginative vocabulary; introduce sequencing language (today/now/then/later); reinforce descriptive color words.

1::upsohigh.title: Up So High
1::upsohigh.reader_text: “I can jump very high. I can swing very high. I can climb up high. I slide down fast. Going high is so exciting. I feel brave when I go high. I will help my dad build a treehouse up high in the big tree. I will climb the ladder. I will look out of the window and see everything down below. Being up high is fun!"

“Show up/down/high/fast.”
Students act out movement.

“Do you like going high?”

Focus on up / high.
Identify short u and long i patterns.

1::upsohigh.questions_type: 1. What can the child jump? 2. What can the child climb? 3. What can the child swing? 4. What happens after going up? 5. Which words show “up” vs. “down”? (skill) 6. What feeling does the child express? (skill) 7. What do YOU like to do up high?
1::upsohigh.answers: 1. High. 2. Up high. 3. Up high. 4. Slide down. 5. Up/high/down/fast. 6. Brave/excited. 7. I like to ___.
1::upsohigh.extension: Movement practice; bravery discussion; slide-path drawing

1::icandoit.title: I Can Do It!
1::icandoit.reader_text: “I can dress myself now. I can eat all my food. I can clean my own room. I can read a big book. I am a big kid now. I learn new things every day. I am proud of myself.”
1::icandoit.objective: Build independence vocabulary; reinforce growth mindset; support sequencing of personal routines.

“Show dress/eat/clean/read.”

1::goodsnacks.title: Good Snacks
1::goodsnacks.reader_text: “I eat a crunchy, red apple. I eat an orange carrot. I eat a piece of cheese. I drink a glass of milk. These are all good snacks. Good snacks help me grow strong. I like to choose healthy snacks.”
1::goodsnacks.objective: Build food vocabulary; categorize healthy snacks; introduce the idea of food-as-energy.

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on snack → /s/ /n/ /a/ /k/
Blend and segment orally.

1::splashdino.title: Splash, Dino!
1::splashdino.reader_text: “Dino jumps in the cool water. Dino splashes all the water. Dino laughs very, very loudly. Dino dries off with a towel. Dino loves to play in water. Dino has fun every time. I like to watch Dino play.”

“Dino ___ in the water.”
Students describe actions.

“First… Next… Then…”
Students describe actions in order.

“Show jump/splash/laugh/dry.”
Students act out actions.

1::catchanddrop.title: Catch and Drop
1::catchanddrop.reader_text: “I throw the red ball up. I drop the ball on the ground. I pick the ball up again. I try one more time now. Practice makes you much better. I keep trying again and again. I get better each time.”

Before Reading:

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on I.

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on drop → /d/ /r/ /o/ /p/
Blend and segment orally.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what happens when we keep trying.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence)
“What happens first?”
Students track throw → drop → pick → try again.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on throw / pick.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I ___ the ball.”
Students describe actions.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then…”
Students describe events.

“Show throw/drop/pick.”
Students act out actions.

1::catchanddrop.questions_type: 1. What does the child throw? 2. Where does the ball drop? 3. What does the child pick up? 4. What helps the child get better? 5. Which words show repeating actions? (skill) 6. What is the cause and effect? (skill) 7. What do YOU practice to get better?
1::catchanddrop.answers: 1. A red ball. 2. On the ground. 3. The ball. 4. Practice. 5. Again/again/one more time. 6. Practice → improvement. 7. I practice ___.
1::catchanddrop.extension: Practice chart; “try again” game; ball-sequence drawing

1::forestwalk.reading_strategy: Picture walk; compare tall/small; echo reading
1::forestwalk.questions_type: 1. Where does the child walk? 2. What tree do they see? 3. What size is the bird? 4. How does the forest feel? 5. Which words describe size and appearance? (skill) 6. What is the setting? (skill) 7. Would YOU like to walk in a forest?
1::forestwalk.answers: 1. A narrow path. 2. A very tall tree. 3. Small and brown. 4. Peaceful. 5. Tall/small/bright/warm/brown. 6. A forest. 7. Yes/No.
1::forestwalk.extension: Forest-scene drawing; size-sorting; “find the detail” game

1::happypuppy.title: Happy Puppy
1::happypuppy.reader_text: “The puppy runs around quickly. The puppy jumps up very high. The puppy licks my face. The puppy rests on its bed. The puppy is so happy. I love to play with the puppy. The puppy makes me smile.”
1::happypuppy.objective: Build animal-action vocabulary; sequence energetic → calm actions; support SEL (joy, affection).
1::happypuppy.core_vocab_raw: puppy, run, jump, lick, rest, quickly
1::happypuppy.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on puppy → /p/ /u/ /p/ /ē/
Blend and segment orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on the.
Read in phrase: “The puppy…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what the puppy does.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Actions & Sequence)
“What does the puppy do first?”
Students track run → jump → lick → rest.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build expression with repeated subject.

Monitor Understanding (Feelings)
“How does the puppy feel?”
Students identify happy.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“The puppy ___.”
Students describe actions.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then…”
Students describe events.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show run/jump/lick/rest.”
Students act out actions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on happy / puppy.
Identify short vowel patterns.
1::happypuppy.questions_type: 1. How does the puppy run? 2. What does the puppy do after running? 3. What does the puppy lick? 4. Where does the puppy rest? 5. Which words show feelings? (skill) 6. What is the main idea? (skill) 7. What do YOU like to do with a pet?
1::happypuppy.answers: 1. Quickly. 2. Jumps up high. 3. My face. 4. On its bed. 5. Happy/smile/love. 6. The puppy is happy and fun. 7. I like to ___.
1::happypuppy.extension: Action cards; pet-care discussion; puppy drawing

1::whereisit.title: Where Is It?
1::whereisit.reader_text: “Where is the small, black cat? It is on the soft, warm bed. Where is the big, brown dog? It is under the wooden table. Where is my red ball? I find it in the box. I am glad I found my things. I put them away carefully now.”
1::whereisit.objective: Build positional language (on, under, in); reinforce color/size descriptors; develop basic locating skills.
1::whereisit.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on where / it.
Read long e and short i words.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on is.
Read in phrase: “It is…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find where things are.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Location)
“Where is the cat?”
Students identify on / under / in.

Prompt Students to Decode (NOT Guess)
Focus on under / find.
Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“It is ___ the ___.”
Students describe location.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show on/under/in.”
Students act out positions.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Where is your toy?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on where / is / it.
Identify vowel patterns and short i.
1::whereisit.reading_strategy: Picture walk; object-placement practice; echo reading
1::whereisit.questions_type: 1. Where is the small cat? 2. Where is the big dog? 3. Where is the red ball? 4. How does the child feel after finding things? 5. Which words tell position? (skill) 6. Which words describe size/color? (skill) 7. Where do YOU put your things?
1::whereisit.answers: 1. On the bed. 2. Under the table. 3. In the box. 4. Glad. 5. On/under/in. 6. Small/big/black/brown/red. 7. I put them ___.
1::whereisit.extension: Positional object hunt; color-sort; “find it fast” game

1::parkpicnic.title: Park Picnic
1::parkpicnic.reader_text: “We go to the big park. We sit on the green grass. We eat our big sandwiches. We drink sweet apple juice. Then we play with a ball. We run and we laugh together. The sun feels warm on our faces. A picnic is a special day for us.”
1::parkpicnic.objective: Build outdoor activity vocabulary; reinforce sequencing; connect sensory details (warm, sweet).
1::parkpicnic.core_vocab_raw: park, picnic, grass, sit, drink, ball, warm, apple juice
1::parkpicnic.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on park → /p/ /ar/ /k/
Blend sounds orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on we.
Read in phrase: “We go… We eat…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what the family does at the park.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence & Details)
“What do they do at the park?”
“What do they eat?”
“What feels warm?”
“What happens after they eat?”

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build smooth phrasing.

Monitor Understanding (Actions)
Students identify sit, eat, drink, play, run.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“We ___ at the park.”
Students describe actions.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then…”
Students describe picnic steps.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show sit/eat/drink/play.”
Students act out actions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on park / picnic.
Identify r-controlled ar and short i.
1::parkpicnic.reading_strategy: Picture walk; sequencing cards; echo reading
1::parkpicnic.questions_type: 1. Where do they sit? 2. What do they eat? 3. What do they drink? 4. What do they do after eating? 5. Which words describe the picnic? (skill) 6. What happens in order? (skill) 7. What do YOU like to take to a picnic?
1::parkpicnic.answers: 1. On the green grass. 2. Big sandwiches. 3. Sweet apple juice. 4. Play with a ball. 5. Big/green/sweet/warm/special. 6. Sit → eat → drink → play → run/laugh. 7. I like to bring ___.
1::parkpicnic.oral_prompts: “Show sit/eat/drink/play.” Tell me three things the family does at the park. What do they eat? What feels warm? What happens after they eat
1::parkpicnic.extension:
Picnic drawing; food-sort; outdoor sensory chart
1. Draw your own picnic and label two foods. 2. Sequence the picnic events using First/Next/Then. 3. Write one sensory sentence: “The sun feels ___.”

1::frogsgrow.title: Frogs Grow
1::frogsgrow.reader_text: Frogs are big, but they start small. An egg comes from a frog. A tadpole comes from an egg. Tadpoles grow. A frog comes from a tadpole. Frogs grow. Animals grow. What else grows?
1::frogsgrow.objective: Frog life cycles; sequence growth stages; compare animal changes
1::frogsgrow.core_vocab_raw: small, big, frog, egg, grow, animal, tadpole,
1::frogsgrow.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on frog / fly.
Read and blend fr- and long i.

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: small, big, frog, egg, grow
Use simple visuals and examples.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find how animals grow.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence)
“What comes after the egg?”
Students track egg → tadpole → frog.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on tadpole / grow.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“A ___ comes from ___.”
Students describe stages.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First ___, then ___.”
Students describe life cycle.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show small/big/grow.”
Students act out or point.

Identify the Central Idea
“What do animals do?”
Students explain growth.
1::frogsgrow.reading_strategy: Picture walk; sequencing cards; echo reading; life-cycle chart
1::frogsgrow.questions_type: 1. What do frogs come from? 2. What comes after eggs? 3. Which words show order? (skill) 4. What changes as animals grow?
1::frogsgrow.answers: 1. Eggs. 2. Tadpoles. 3. Come from/grow/turn into. 4. Their bodies change.
1::frogsgrow.extension: 1. Create a life-cycle wheel. 2. Act out each stage of the life cycle.

1::makethebed.title: Make the Bed
1::makethebed.reader_text: Let's make the bed. First, we get clean sheets. I like clean sheets. Next, we stretch the sheet around the bed. Then, we tuck a sheet over the bed. I like to fold the sheet down. Last, we put clean pillowcases on the pillows. I like to sleep in my bed.
1::makethebed.objective: Follow procedural steps; build independence vocabulary; sequence actions
1::makethebed.sor:
Before Reading:

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on we.
Read in phrase: “We get… We tuck…”

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on bed → /b/ /e/ /d/
Blend and segment orally.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find how to make the bed.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence & Details)
“What is the first step?”
“Where do the dirty sheets go?”
“What makes the bed fresh?”

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build flow with sequence words.

Prompt Students to Decode (NOT Guess)
Focus on sheet / tuck.
Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“First we ___.” / “Next we ___.”
Students describe steps.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then… Last…”
Students explain process.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show stretch/tuck/pillow.”
Students act out actions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on make / bed.
Identify silent e and short e patterns.
1::makethebed.reading_strategy: Picture walk; step-sorting; modeling; echo reading
1::makethebed.questions_type: 1. What do we pull off first? 2. Where do dirty sheets go? 3. What do we put on next? 4. Why do we tuck the sheet? 5. Which words show order? (skill) 6. What is the last step? (skill) 7. Why are fresh sheets nice?
1::makethebed.answers: 1. Dirty sheets. 2. In the hamper. 3. A clean sheet. 4. To make it tight. 5. First/next. 6. Put pillows on the bed. 7. They feel clean and soft.
1::makethebed.oral_prompts: What is the first step? Where do the dirty sheets go? What makes the bed fresh?
1::makethebed.extension: 1. Draw and label the steps of making a bed. 2. Practice giving directions using First/Next. 3. Create a simple chore checklist.
1::makethebed.phonics_rule: long a (make silent e), short e (bed)

1::therace.title: The Race
1::therace.reader_text: Today there is a big race. The horse is ready. The gate opens, and the horse runs fast. Run, horse, run! Will the horse stop? No! Run, horse, run! The people clap! The horse wins the race!
1::therace.objective: Action verbs; sequence race events; identify beginning/middle/end
1::therace.core_vocab_raw: gate, ready, open, end, clap, win, stop
1::therace.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on race → /r/ /ā/ /s/
Blend sounds orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on the.
Read in phrase: “The horse…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what happens in the race.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Beginning/Middle/End)
“What happens when the gate opens?”
“What happens at the end?”
“Why do people clap?”

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build excitement with repeated lines.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on gate / clap.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“The ___ is ready.” / “The horse ___.”
Students describe events.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then… Last…”
Students describe race events.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show ready/open/clap.”
Students act out actions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on race.
Identify long a silent e.
1::therace.reading_strategy: Picture walk; echo reading; action sequencing
1::therace.questions_type: 1. Who is ready? 2. What is open? 3. What does the horse do? 4. What do people do at the end? 5. What happens first? (skill) 6. What happens last? (skill) 7. How does the horse feel?
1::therace.answers: 1. The horse and the man. 2. The gate. 3. Runs fast. 4. Clap. 5. The gate opens. 6. People clap. 7. Happy.
1::therace.oral_prompts: What happens when the gate opens? What happens at the end? Why do people clap?
1::therace.extension: 1. Draw beginning/middle/end of the race. 2. Act out the race using action verbs. 3. Write one sentence about the fastest moment.

1::thestorm.title: The Storm
1::thestorm.reader_text: A storm is coming. I smell rain. I see a flash. I hear thunder. Boom! Boom! The rain falls from the sky. I feel safe in my home. I like to watch the storm with my family.
1::thestorm.objective: Build weather vocabulary; identify sensory details; connect cause/effect
1::thestorm.sor:
Before Reading:

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: rain, flash, thunder.
Use simple visuals and examples.

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on rain.
Read and blend vowel team ai.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what we hear and see in a storm.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sensory Details)
“What do you hear in the storm?”
“What do you see?”
“How does the air feel?”

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Use expression for “Boom! Boom!”

Monitor Understanding (Cause/Effect)
“What happens when the storm comes?”
Students connect rain, thunder, feeling safe.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I hear ___.” / “I see ___.”
Students describe sensory details.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show hear/see/feel.”
Students act out senses.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Do you like storms?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on rain.
Identify vowel team ai.
1::thestorm.reading_strategy: Picture walk; sensory chart; echo reading
1::thestorm.questions_type: 1. What do you hear? 2. What do you see in the sky? 3. What starts to fall? 4. Where does the child feel safe? 5. Which words show sound? (skill) 6. What happens after lightning? (skill) 7. How does the storm feel?
1::thestorm.answers: 1. Thunder. 2. Lightning. 3. Rain. 4. Inside the house. 5. Flash/Crack. 6. Rain falls. 7. Loud and heavy.
1::thestorm.oral_prompts: What do you hear in the storm? What do you see? How does the air feel?
1::thestorm.extension: 1. Complete a senses chart (see/hear/feel/smell). 2. Draw a storm scene with labels. 3. Write one cause/effect sentence.

1::thefight.title: The Fight
1::thefight.reader_text: “It’s my turn!” Tommy was angry. Mia was on the swing. She said, "No, it is my turn." Tommy had an angry face. Mia said, "I feel sad when you are angry with me." Tommy said, "I'm sorry." Mia gave the swing to Tommy. It was his turn. "Thanks, Mia. I feel better." Mia and Tommy are good friends.
1::thefight.objective: Identify emotions; problem/solution structure; build SEL vocabulary
1::thefight.core_vocab_raw: turn, feel, sad, angry, sorry, better
1::thefight.sor:
Before Reading:

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on my / is.
Read in phrase: “It is my turn.”

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on fight → /f/ /ī/ /t/
Blend sounds orally.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what problem they have and how they fix it.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Problem & Solution)
“Why is Tommy angry?”
“What makes it better?”
“What does ‘sorry’ do?”

Echo Reading for Fluency (Dialogue)
Teacher models → students repeat key lines.
Practice expression with feelings.

Monitor Understanding (Emotions)
“How does Mia feel?”
Students identify sad, angry, better.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I feel ___.” / “She says ___.”
Students express emotions or dialogue.

Identify the Central Idea
“How do they fix the problem?”
Students explain apology and sharing.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show sad/angry/sorry.”
Students act out emotions.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on fight.
Identify vowel team igh.
1::thefight.reading_strategy: Picture walk; role-play; echo reading; feelings chart
1::thefight.questions_type: 1. Why is the child angry? 2. What does Susu say? 3. How does the child feel at the end? 4. Are they still friends? 5. What is the problem? (skill) 6. What is the solution? (skill) 7. What should you do when upset?
1::thefight.answers: 1. It was not his turn. 2. Be patient/sorry. 3. Better. 4. Yes. 5. They argue over the swing. 6. They apologize and share. 7. Talk and say sorry.
1::thefight.oral_prompts: Why is Tommy angry? What makes it better? What does “sorry” do?
1::thefight.extension: 1. Role-play solving a problem. 2. Draw the problem and solution. 3. Write a sentence using “I feel ___.”

1::peoplewhohelp.title: People Who Help
1::peoplewhohelp.reader_text: People like to help. Helping can be a good job. Nurses help sick people. A firefighter helps stop a fire. The police help us stay safe. A teacher helps kids learn I want to thank helpers. Who do you want to thank?
1::peoplewhohelp.objective: Identify community helpers; match roles to actions; build civic awareness
1::peoplewhohelp.core_vocab_raw: nurse, firefighter, police, teacher, sick, safe, learn, thank
1::peoplewhohelp.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on people / who.
Read long ee and wh digraph.

Vocabulary Preview (3–4 Words)
Teach: nurse, firefighter, police, teacher.
Use simple visuals and examples.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find who helps us and how.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Roles & Actions)
“Who helps us?”
“What do they do?”
Students match helper → job.

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on nurse / police.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“A ___ helps ___.” / “A ___ is ___.”
Students describe helpers.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show help/teach/save.”
Students act out actions.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Do you help people?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on people / who.
Identify long ee and wh digraph.
1::peoplewhohelp.reading_strategy: Picture walk; helper-action match; echo reading
1::peoplewhohelp.questions_type: 1. Who helps sick people? 2. Who helps kids learn? 3. Who stops fires? 4. Who keeps us safe? 5. Which words show actions? (skill) 6. What is the main idea? (skill) 7. Which helper do you thank?
1::peoplewhohelp.answers: 1. Nurse. 2. Teacher. 3. Firefighter. 4. Police officer. 5. Helps/learn/stops. 6. Helpers make our community safe. 7. I thank ___.
1::peoplewhohelp.oral_prompts: Who helps us? Do you help people?
1::peoplewhohelp.extension: 1 Make a T chart with jobs and how the jobs help us 2. Act out the helping jobs. 3. Think of another helping job. Draw and write about it.

1::lookingforsimba.title: Looking for Simba
1::lookingforsimba.reader_text: “I look for Simba. Is he in the box? Is he on the mat? Is he under the chair? I look and look. I find Simba! He is on the soft bed.”
1::lookingforsimba.objective: Build spatial concept vocabulary (in/on/under); strengthen reading of questions and answers.
1::lookingforsimba.core_vocab_raw: look, box, mat, chair, find, soft, bed, happy
1::lookingforsimba.sor:
Before Reading:

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on is / he.
Read in question: “Is he…?”

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on box → /b/ /o/ /ks/
Blend and segment orally.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find where Simba is.”

During Reading:

Finger Tracking for Print Concepts
Students point to each word.
Notice question marks.

Text-Based Questions (Location)
“Is he in the box?”
“Where is he?”
Students identify in/on/under.

Echo Reading for Fluency (Questions & Answers)
Teacher models → students repeat.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“Is he ___ the ___?”
“He is ___ the ___.”
Students ask and answer.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show above/below/inside/outside.”
Students act out positions.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Where is your toy?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on look / for.
Identify short oo and r-controlled or.
1::lookingforsimba.reading_strategy: Picture walk; location gestures; echo reading
1::lookingforsimba.questions_type: 1. What animal is the child looking for? 2. Where does the child look first? 3. Where else does the child look? 4. Where is Hoppy at the end? 5. Which words show position? (skill) 6. How does the child find Hoppy? (skill) 7. Where would YOU look for a lost pet?
1::lookingforsimba.answers: 1. A rabbit. 2. Above/on the balcony. 3. Below/in the basement; inside the cage; outside near the staircase. 4. On the sofa. 5. Above/below/inside/outside. 6. By hearing a quiet noise. 7. I would look ___.
1::lookingforsimba.extension: Hidden-toy search; positional cards; “Where is Hoppy?” retell

1::intheforest.title: In the Forest
1::intheforest.reader_text: “I walk on a narrow path. I see a very tall tree. I see a small, brown bird. I see the bright, warm sun. A forest walk is very peaceful. I listen to the quiet sounds. I breathe the fresh forest air. I like walking in the woods.”
1::intheforest.objective: Build nature vocabulary; strengthen describing attributes; introduce sensory language; build main-idea thinking (“forest walk”).
1::intheforest.core_vocab_raw: path, tall, peaceful, leaf, air, wood
1::intheforest.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on path → /p/ /a/ /th/
Blend sounds orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on I.
Read in phrase: “I walk… I see…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what we see and hear in the forest.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sensory Details)
“What do you see?”
“What do you hear?”
Students identify sights and sounds.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build calm, smooth phrasing.

Monitor Understanding (Main Idea)
“What is this mostly about?”
Students identify forest walk.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I see ___.” / “I walk ___.”
Students describe actions.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show peaceful/quiet.”
Students act out meaning.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“What do you hear outside?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on forest.
Identify r-controlled or.
1::intheforest.reading_strategy: Picture walk; sensory prompts; echo reading
1::intheforest.reading_skill: Describing; sensory details; identifying main idea
1::intheforest.questions_type: 1. What does the child walk on? 2. What does the child see first? 3. What color is the bird? 4. What sounds does the child hear? 5. Which words describe the forest? (skill) 6. What is the main idea? (skill) 7. What do YOU see in nature?
1::intheforest.answers: 1. A narrow path. 2. A tall tree. 3. Brown. 4. Quiet sounds. 5. Peaceful, quiet, bright, warm, fresh. 6. A forest walk is calm and beautiful. 7. I see ___.
1::intheforest.extension: Draw a forest; nature sound sort; color attributes

1::mybigdreams.title: My Big Dreams
1::mybigdreams.reader_text: “I dream of stars. I dream of fun. I dream of my toys. I dream of my friends. My dreams are great. I like my dreams.”
1::mybigdreams.objective: Introduce the concept of dreaming; reinforce simple present tense (dream); strengthen reading of a highly repetitive sentence pattern.; introduce future-thinking concepts; connect ideas and feelings.
1::mybigdreams.core_vocab_raw: dream, star, fun, toy, friend, great, like
1::mybigdreams.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on dream / big.
Read and blend dr- and short i.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on of.
Read in phrase: “dream of…”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what I dream of.”

During Reading:

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build rhythm with repeated pattern.

Prompt Students to Decode (NOT Guess)
Focus on dream / stars.
Sound → blend → confirm meaning.

Text-Based Questions (Pattern & Meaning)
“What do you dream of?”
Students identify repeated ideas.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“I dream of ___.”
Students create their own ideas.

Identify the Central Idea
“What is this story about?”
Students explain dreams.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show stars/help/hope.”
Students act out or point.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on dreams.
Identify dr- blend and plural -s.
1::mybigdreams.reading_skill: Identifying big ideas; connecting dreams to feelings
1::mybigdreams.questions_type: 1. What does the child dream of in the sky? 2. What does the child dream of doing for others? 3. What is important to the child? 4. What do dreams give the child? 5. Which words show positive goals? (skill) 6. What is the author’s message? (skill) 7. What is YOUR dream?
1::mybigdreams.answers: 1. Bright stars/flying. 2. Helping people. 3. Their dreams. 4. Hope. 5. Dream/hope/better tomorrow. 6. Dreams inspire hope and goals. 7. My dream is ___.
1::mybigdreams.extension: Draw a dream; “dream cloud” chart; acts of kindness list

1::animalcoats.title: Animal Coats
1::animalcoats.reader_text: “A bird has feathers. A fish has scales. A cat has fur. A turtle has a shell. The feathers are soft. The shell is hard. I like all the coats.”
1::animalcoats.objective: Introduce animal coat vocabulary; reinforce simple present tense (has, are); strengthen reading of a highly repetitive sentence pattern.
1::animalcoats.core_vocab_raw: bird, feather, fish, scale, cat, fur, turtle, shell, soft, hard, like, coat
1::animalcoats.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on fur → /f/ /er/
Blend sounds orally.

High-Frequency Word Preview
Focus on has.
Read in phrase: “A ___ has ___.”

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what animals have.”

During Reading:

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build rhythm with repeated pattern.

Text-Based Questions (Attributes)
“What does a bird have?”
“Which coat is soft? Which is hard?”

Integrate Decoding + Meaning
Focus on shell / scales.
Sound → blend → connect to meaning.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“A ___ has ___.”
“The ___ is ___.”
Students describe animals.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Show feathers/scales/fur/shell.”
Students act out or point.

Simple Text-to-Self Connection
“Which animal do you like?”

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on coats.
Identify long oa and plural -s.
1::animalcoats.reading_strategy: Picture walk; attribute sort; echo reading
1::animalcoats.questions_type: 1. What covering does the bird have? 2. What covering does the cat have? 3. What covering does the turtle have? 4. Why are coats special? 5. Which words describe textures? (skill) 6. How do coats help animals? (skill) 7. Which animal coat do YOU like?
1::animalcoats.answers: 1. Feathers. 2. Fur. 3. Shell. 4. They are different and special. 5. Soft/light/shiny/hard/warm. 6. They help animals stay safe. 7. I like ___.
1::animalcoats.extension: Coat-texture match; draw an animal; sort coverings

1::littleredhen.title: Little Red Hen
1::littleredhen.reader_text: The hen sees red seeds. The hen digs a hole. The hen plants the seeds. The seeds grow. The hen smiles.
1::littleredhen.core_vocab_raw: dig, plant, grow, hen, seed, red
1::littleredhen.sor:
Before Reading:

Phonological Awareness Warm-Up
Focus on hen → /h/ /e/ /n/
Blend and segment orally.

Phonics Review (Taught Patterns)
Focus on red / hen.
Read short e words.

Set a Purpose for Reading
“Let’s find what the hen does.”

During Reading:

Text-Based Questions (Sequence)
“What does the hen do first?”
Students track see → dig → plant → grow → smile.

Echo Reading for Fluency
Teacher reads → students repeat.
Build smooth, simple phrasing.

Monitor Understanding (Action)
“What is the hen doing now?”
Students identify actions.

After Reading:

Sentence Production
“The hen ___.”
Students describe actions.

Retell the Story (Sequence)
“First… Next… Then…”
Students describe steps.

Vocabulary Reinforcement
“Point to the hen.”
Students identify key item.

Word Work: Phonics Transfer
Focus on hen / red.
Identify short e pattern.
1::littleredhen.questions_type: 1. What does the hen plant? 2. What grows?
1::littleredhen.extension: Show how to dig and plant.
1::littleredhen.phonics_rule: short i (little), short e (red), short e (hen)

2::countingtogether.title: Counting Together
2::countingtogether.reader_text: We count apples on the tree. One, two, three apples. We count books on the shelf. Four, five, six books. We count toys on the floor. Seven, eight, nine toys. Counting together is fun. We count to ten.
2::countingtogether.objective: Develop number word fluency 1-10 and strengthen the concept of quantity through predictable patterned sentences.
2::countingtogether.core_vocab_raw: count, apple, tree, book, shelf, toy, floor, together, fun, ten
2::countingtogether.sor: Before: Conduct a structured picture walk to activate background knowledge about counting real objects. Orally rehearse number sequence 1-10 with finger tracking. Quick phonics warm-up: segment and blend CVC words (ten, fun) and review initial consonant sounds /t/ (ten, tree). Pre-teach vocabulary using visuals (shelf, together). Set a clear purpose: “We will match number words to objects.” During: Model one-to-one correspondence by pointing to each word while students choral-read predictable frames (“We count…”). Explicitly connect printed number words to spoken quantities. Pause to track number sequence and ask students to verify quantities in illustrations. Highlight the repeated syntax pattern to support fluency and orthographic mapping. After: Students retell the counting sequence orally using sentence frame “We count ___.” Conduct quick retrieval: write and read number words 1-10. Have students draw and label a counted group to reinforce quantity-word mapping.
2::countingtogether.reading_strategy: Picture walk, point-and-count, choral repetition of predictable frames.
2::countingtogether.reading_skill: Identifying quantities; tracking a number sequence; matching number words to objects.
2::countingtogether.questions_type: 1. What do they count first? (literal) 2. What numbers come after four? (skill: number sequence) 3. What pattern do you notice in the story? (skill: pattern recognition) 4. Why is counting together helpful? (inferential) 5. What new items could you count around you? (critical thinking)
2::countingtogether.answers: 1. Apples. 2. Five and six. 3. The sentence 'We count…' repeats each time. 4. It helps us check and learn together. 5. Answers vary.
2::countingtogether.oral_prompts: Count the objects I point to. "Show me five fingers."
2::countingtogether.extension: Create a counting book; draw ten objects and label them; sort pictures by number groups.
2::countingtogether.syntax_focus: Present progressive structure (“We are counting…”); compound subject awareness

2::countwithme.title: Count with Me
2::countwithme.reader_text: I see one red book. I see two blue kites. I see three small birds. I see four white clouds. I see five yellow flowers. I see six, seven, eight. I see nine, ten. I can count them all!
2::countwithme.objective: Strengthen color-object vocabulary and predictable phrasing while reinforcing number fluency to ten.
2::countwithme.core_vocab_raw: see, kite, bird, cloud, flower, count, six, seven, eight, nine, ten
2::countwithme.sor: Before: Picture walk focusing on color + object pairing. Review color words and number words orally. Phonics warm-up: blend long vowel patterns (see) and CVC words (red). Pre-teach plural forms (birds, kites). Set purpose: “We will use colors to help us count carefully.” During: Echo read the repeated frame “I see…” while tracking print. Prompt students to match adjectives (red, blue) to nouns in illustrations. Reinforce syntax pattern recognition and plural noun pronunciation. Pause for think-aloud: “The pattern helps me predict the next sentence.” After: Students generate original sentence using frame “I see ___.” Conduct quick dictation: “I see five birds.” Retrieval practice: orally count backward from ten.
2::countwithme.reading_strategy: Repeated phrase tracking; echo reading; color identification.
2::countwithme.reading_skill: Using visual details to identify attributes and match text to illustrations.
2::countwithme.questions_type: 1. What does the child see first? (literal) 2. What color are the kites? (literal) 3. How does the repeated phrase support your reading? (skill: pattern recognition) 4. Why do you think the author uses colors in every sentence? (inferential) 5. What new sentence could YOU make with 'I see…'? (creative)
2::countwithme.answers: 1. A red book. 2. Blue. 3. It repeats the same starting words to help predict the sentence. 4. To help describe and identify items more clearly. 5. Answers vary.
2::countwithme.oral_prompts: Point to something blue in the room. "Point to something round you can see."
2::countwithme.extension: Make an 'I see…' booklet; practice color labeling; draw and count objects around the home.
2::countwithme.syntax_focus: Imperative invitation structure (“Count with me.”); prepositional phrase (with me)
2::countwithme.morphology_focus: None new (reinforce base verb form vs. -ing contrast)

2::wherearemyshoes.title: Where Are My Shoes?
2::wherearemyshoes.reader_text: I look for my shoes. I look on the rug. I look under the bed. I look by the door. I look in the closet. I find my shoes in my bag! Now I can go outside.
2::wherearemyshoes.objective: Strengthen use of prepositions and develop independence by tracking a repeated search pattern.
2::wherearemyshoes.core_vocab_raw: look, shoe, rug, bed, door, closet, bag, outside
2::wherearemyshoes.sor: Before: Picture walk emphasizing positional words (on, under, in, by). Physically model each preposition with classroom objects. Phonics warm-up: review long vowel /oo/ (shoes) and CVC words (rug, bag). Set purpose: “We will follow a search pattern.” During: Track repeated sentence “I look…” and highlight prepositions in print. Have students gesture each location as it is read. Pause to predict next location using pattern recognition. Emphasize sequence order. After: Students retell using sentence frame “I look ___.” Retrieval: Ask students to place an object under/in/on and describe it orally. Write one prepositional phrase together.
2::wherearemyshoes.reading_strategy: Predicting based on patterns; picture scanning.
2::wherearemyshoes.reading_skill: Recognizing positional words; using repetition to follow a search sequence.
2::wherearemyshoes.questions_type: 1. Where does the child look first? (literal) 2. Where are the shoes found? (literal) "Where did Mia find her shoes" 3. How does the pattern help you follow the search? (skill: pattern & sequence) 4. Why do people sometimes lose things? (inferential) 5. What strategy would YOU use to find something lost? (critical thinking)
2::wherearemyshoes.answers: 1. On the rug. 2. In the bag. 3. The same sentence structure repeats each time. 4. Because we move items or forget where we put them. 5. Answers vary.
2::wherearemyshoes.extension: Make a lost-and-found chart; write a new search page using 'I look…'
2::wherearemyshoes.syntax_focus: Question structure (“Where are…?”); linking verb are

2::busybugs.title: Busy Bugs
2::busybugs.reader_text: Worms dig. Bees buzz. Ants march. Beetles crawl. Bugs move in many ways. Bugs are busy everywhere!
2::busybugs.objective: Develop foundational science vocabulary and movement verbs connected to insect behavior.
2::busybugs.core_vocab_raw: worm, dig, bee, buzz, ant, march, beetle, crawl, bug, busy
2::busybugs.sor:
Before Reading:

Set a Meaning-Based Purpose for Reading
SoR Component: Comprehension Monitoring
Ask: “Let’s find out how bugs move.”
Follow-up: “Listen for what each bug does.”

Vocabulary Preview (Tier 2 + Tier 3 Words)
SoR Component: Vocabulary + Oral Language
Teach 3–4 words such as: dig, buzz, march, crawl
Use gestures and simple definitions.
Have students repeat and act out each word.
Use a quick model sentence: “Ants march.”

During Reading:

Decode → Clarify Meaning Loop
SoR Component: Word Recognition + Meaning
When encountering key words:
Decode
Clarify meaning
Connect to image or action
Reread sentence

Mental Imagery and Movement
SoR Component: Language Comprehension
Ask: “Can you picture how the bug is moving?”
“Show me how it moves.”
Students act out digging, buzzing, marching, and crawling.

Text-Based Questions
SoR Component: Knowledge Building
“What do worms do?”
“What do ants do?”
Focus on answers from the text.

After Reading:

Text-to-Self Connection
SoR Component: Oral Language + Comprehension
Ask: “Have you seen a bug move like this?”
“Which bug movement do you like?”

Vocabulary Deepening with Sentence Frame
SoR Component: Syntax + Oral Language
Sentence Frame: “They ___.”
Students say: “They crawl.” “They march.”
Encourage one new sentence using a movement word.

Extension (Optional)
Act out different bug movements.
Draw a bug and show how it moves.
2::busybugs.reading_strategy: Verb gestures; classify & label; compare creatures.
2::busybugs.reading_skill: Identifying how insects move using precise verbs.
2::busybugs.questions_type: 1. What do worms do? (literal) 2. What do ants do? (literal) 3. How do the movement words help you understand the insects? (skill: verb meaning) 4. Why do you think bugs move in different ways? (inferential) 5. Invent a new bug movement and describe it. (creative)
2::busybugs.answers: 1. Dig. 2. March. 3. They give clues about actions and behaviors. 4. They have different bodies and needs. 5. Answers vary.
2::busybugs.oral_prompts: Show me how a beetle crawls; show how a bee buzzes.
2::busybugs.extension: Sort insects by movement; draw a bug doing an action.

2::weloveberries.title: We Love Berries
2::weloveberries.reader_text: We pick red berries. We eat sweet berries. We make jam with berries. Berries are good food. We love berries!
2::weloveberries.objective: Develop simple food vocabulary, taste descriptions, and process language.
2::weloveberries.core_vocab_raw: berry, pick, red, sweet, eat, jam, good, food

“Do you like berries?”

High-Frequency Word Preview

good

“What do they make?”

After Reading:

SoR Component: Comprehension

“What fruit do you like?”

Extension (Optional)

2::weloveberries.questions_type: 1. What color are the berries? (literal) 2. What do the characters make? (literal) 3. How does the author help you understand what berries are like? (skill: attribute identification) 4. Why do you think berries are good food? (inferential) 5. If you could invent a new berry dish, what would it be? (creative)
2::weloveberries.answers: 1. Red. 2. Jam. 3. Words like red and sweet. 4. They are healthy and tasty. 5. Answers vary.
2::weloveberries.oral_prompts: Show me something sweet; show me something red.
2::weloveberries.extension: Create a berry recipe card; draw steps for picking/making/eating berries.

2::shapesathome.title: Shapes at Home
2::shapesathome.reader_text: I see square windows. I see a round clock. I see triangle roofs. Shapes are everywhere at home.
2::shapesathome.objective: Identify and classify basic shapes in real-world environments.
2::shapesathome.core_vocab_raw: square, window, round, clock, triangle, roof, shape, everywhere
2::shapesathome.sor:
Before Reading:

Set a Meaning-Based Purpose for Reading
SoR Component: Comprehension Monitoring
Examples:
“Let’s see how many shapes we can find.”
“What shapes can you see around you?”

Vocabulary Preview (3–5 Words): 
SoR Component: Vocabulary + Oral Language
Teach Tier 2 + story-critical Tier 3 words using:
simple definitions; pictures or gestures; a brief example sentence

During Reading:

Text-to-Self or Text-to-Text Connections
Supports oral language and comprehension:
“What shapes are in your home?”
“Let's draw some shapes.”

Reread Sections for Fluency and Comprehension
Students reread to:
improve smoothness
improve expression
confirm meaning

After Reading:

Build Automaticity With Phonics Patterns From the Text

phoneme substitution

use them in a new oral sentence

Vocabulary Deepening
Revisit the pre-taught words and add:
simple sentence frames
2::shapesathome.reading_strategy: Shape hunt; visual scanning; picture labeling.
2::shapesathome.reading_skill: Matching shapes to real objects; identifying attributes.
2::shapesathome.questions_type: 1. What shape are the windows? (literal) 2. What object is round? (literal) 3. How does the text help you identify shapes? (skill: environmental print → shapes) 4. Why do you think shapes are everywhere in homes? (inferential) 5. What shapes can YOU find in your house? (critical/creative)
2::shapesathome.answers: 1. Square. 2. A clock. 3. It names the shapes and shows examples. 4. Because objects are made with basic shapes. 5. Answers vary.
2::shapesathome.oral_prompts: Point to something round in the room; point to something square.
2::shapesathome.extension: Create a shape map of your home; label shapes in pictures; sort objects by shape.
2::shapesathome.phonics_rule: long a silent e (shapes), long o silent e (home)

2::mysistersball.title: My Sister’s Ball
2::mysistersball.reader_text: My sister has a red ball. She rolls it to me. I kick it back. We play together in the yard. Playing together is fun.
2::mysistersball.objective: Support understanding of turn-taking, reciprocity, and action vocabulary.
2::mysistersball.core_vocab_raw: ball, red, roll, kick, play,together, yard
2::mysistersball.sor:
Before Reading:
Set a Meaning-Based Purpose for Reading
SoR Component: Comprehension Monitoring
Examples:
“Let’s find out how the ball moves back and forth.”
“Let’s watch who has the ball each time.”

Vocabulary Preview (3–5 Words): 
SoR Component: Vocabulary + Oral Language
Teach Tier 2 + story-critical Tier 3 words using:
simple definitions; pictures or gestures; a brief example sentence
Target words: roll, kick, together, yard, ball

During Reading:

Decode → Clarify Meaning Loop
SoR Component: Phonics / Decoding + Language Comprehension
After decoding a key word, ask:
“What does that mean on this page?”
“Does that fit the picture?”
Reread the sentence to confirm meaning

Reread Sections for Fluency and Comprehension
Students reread to:
improve smoothness
improve expression
confirm meaning

After Reading:

Build Automaticity With Phonics Patterns From the Text
Choose 3–5 words with the target pattern:
sound-by-sound blending
decoding in and out of context
Focus: r-controlled er in sister

High-Frequency Word Review and Application
Students:

read it in isolation
use it in a new oral sentence: “It is my ___.”

Vocabulary Deepening
Revisit the pre-taught words and add:

“My sister has a ball.”
“We play together.”
2::mysistersball.reading_skill: Identifying who does what and tracking simple event sequences.
2::mysistersball.questions_type: 1. Who has the red ball? (literal) 2. What does the sister do with the ball? (literal) 3. How does the text show turn-taking? (skill: sequence & action analysis) 4. Why is playing together fun? (inferential) 5. What new game could you play with a ball? (creative)
2::mysistersball.answers: 1. The sister. 2. She kicks it back. 3. Each person takes a turn rolling/kicking. 4. Because they share and interact. 5. Answers vary.
2::mysistersball.oral_prompts: Show rolling vs. kicking with your hands or feet.
2::mysistersball.extension: Draw a ball game; create a partner play sequence; list action verbs.

2::friendsplay.core_vocab_raw: run, fast, kick, ball, roll, laugh, fall, stand, smile, friend,
2::friendsplay.sor:
Before Reading:

Set a Meaning-Based Purpose for Reading
SoR Component: Comprehension Monitoring
Examples:
“Let’s see how many shapes we can find.”
“What shapes can you see around you?”

Vocabulary Preview (3–5 Words):

Teach Tier 2 + story-critical Tier 3 words using:
simple definitions; pictures or gestures; a brief example sentence

During Reading:

Text-to-Self or Text-to-Text Connections
Supports oral language and comprehension:
“What shapes are in your home?”
“Let's draw some shapes.”

Reread Sections for Fluency and Comprehension
Students reread to:
improve smoothness
improve expression
confirm meaning

After Reading:

Build Automaticity With Phonics Patterns From the Text
Choose 3–5 words with the target pattern:
word building
phoneme substitution
decoding in and out of context
Example: shine, ship, shop, shut

High-Frequency Word Review and Application
Students:
find these words in the text
read them in isolation
use them in a new oral sentence

Vocabulary Deepening
Revisit the pre-taught words and add:
simple sentence frames
2::friendsplay.reading_skill: Using action words to understand character interactions.
2::friendsplay.questions_type: 1. What do Tommy and Ali kick? (literal) 2. What happens to the ball? (literal) 3. What verbs describe how they move? (skill: identifying action words) 4. Why do you think they laugh when the ball rolls away? (inferential) 5. Invent a new friend activity they could play. (creative)
2::friendsplay.answers: 1. A big ball. 2. It rolls far away. 3. Run, kick, roll, fall. 4. It is fun and surprising. 5. Answers vary.
2::friendsplay.oral_prompts: Show me how to run fast; show me how to kick.
2::friendsplay.extension: Draw two friends playing; create an action-verb mini-book.
2::friendsplay.phonics_rule: long a (play), long i silent e (smile)

2::tryitout.title: Try it Out
2::tryitout.reader_text: My mom says to try new foods. My dad says to play the game. The teacher says to do my best. I will try a new food. I will try the game. I will try my best. I will try it out.

Text-to-Self Connection

(skill: SEL/inferential) 4. When has your teacher asked you to try your best? 5. What new food/ game would you like to try out? (creative/critical)

2::tryitout.extension: Draw a new goal; practice the phrase ‘I will try’ with actions.

2::wormsinthegarden.answers: 1. Brown. 2. Soil. 3. They break up soil so plants can grow. 4. It is moist and safe for them. 5. Answers vary.

2::wormsinthegarden.extension: Draw soil layers; sort garden helpers vs. pests.

2::iamgrowing.title: I Am Growing!

2::mybigbreakfast.title: My Big Breakfast
2::mybigbreakfast.reader_text: I am visiting my grandma. She likes to make a big breakfast. Grandma says that breakfast is an important meal. She makes healthy food for me to eat. I eat everything on my plate. Grandma smiles. She puts more food on my plate. I feel strong when I eat Grandma's food.
2::mybigbreakfast.objective: Strengthen food vocabulary and explain energy-for-the-body concepts.

Decode
Clarify meaning
Connect to picture
Reread sentence

SoR Component: Comprehension

Quick Decision
SoR Component: Comprehension

SoR Component: Oral Language
“Why does Grandma smile?”

Sentence Frame Practice

Apply Thinking
SoR Component: Oral Language
“What helps your body feel strong?”

2::fixandreuse.title: Fix and Reuse
2::fixandreuse.reader_text: My old bike had a flat tire. My dad fixed the tire. Now my bike looks new again! We clean it together. We use it every day. Fixing things is good. We do not throw it away. Fixing things helps the Earth.

2::pastandpresent.title: Past and Present
2::pastandpresent.reader_text: My grandpa rode a bike to school. Now I ride a big bus. My grandma wrote letters on paper. Now I send texts on a phone. Things change over time. But family love stays the same. We learn from the past.
2::pastandpresent.objective: Teach before/after comparisons and simple historical reasoning.
2::pastandpresent.core_vocab_raw: grandpa, ride/rode, bike, school, bus, grandma, write/wrote, letter, text,phone, change, love, past

Before Reading

Build Understanding of Change Over Time

Activate Background Knowledge
SoR Component: Language Comprehension
Ask: “What is something old and something new?”
Show 2–3 simple images (past vs. now) to build schema.

Vocabulary Preview (Tier 2 + Tier 3 Words)
SoR Component: Vocabulary + Oral Language
Teach 3–5 words such as: past, now, change.
Use visuals, gestures (point back/forward), and simple definitions.
Have students repeat and segment sounds in key words.

During Reading

Decode → Clarify Meaning Loop
SoR Component: Word Recognition + Meaning
When encountering content words, guide students to decode, clarify meaning, connect to the image, and reread the sentence.

Text-Based Observation Questions

Ask: “What happened in the past?”
“What happens now?”
“How do you know?”
Encourage students to answer using evidence from the text or picture.

After Reading

SoR Component: Comprehension
Identify differences between past and present (bike vs. bus, letters vs. texts).
Reinforce understanding of change over time.

“In the past, ___ / Now, ___.”

Explain Your Thinking
SoR Component: Language Comprehension
“Why do things change over time?”
Encourage students to use examples from the text.

2::pastandpresent.answers: 1. Rode a bike. 2. Rides a bus. 3. Rode/wrote vs. send/ride. 4. New inventions and needs. 5. Answers vary.

2::candles.title: Candles
2::candles.reader_text: We light candles. The candles make the night bright. Our family cuddles under warm blankets. We share yummy popcorm. The night is full of joy. It is a happy time. The night feels magical. The candle flickers.

2::familyroadtrip.title: Family Road Trip
2::familyroadtrip.reader_text: We pack our bags for a trip. Dad drives the car. Mom reads the map. We see tall mountains. We see a wide river. The trip is long but fun. We sing songs in the car. I love road trips!
2::familyroadtrip.objective: Teach travel vocabulary and descriptive settings.

2::swirlsinnature.title: Swirls in Nature
2::swirlsinnature.reader_text: A seashell has a spiral shape. A snail's shell is a spiral too. Some flowers have swirls in them. We see swirls in the sky. We see swirls in the sea. Swirls are everywhere in nature! I like finding swirls everywhere.

2::lightandshadows.title: Light and Shadows
2::lightandshadows.reader_text: When the sun shines bright, it makes shadows. If I move, my shadow moves too. My shadow is long in the morning. My shadow is short at noon. At night, it is dark and my shadow is gone. Shadows change with the sun.
2::lightandshadows.objective: Teach early physics concepts (light, shadow movement).
2::lightandshadows.core_vocab_raw: sun, shine, shadow, move, long, morning, short, noon, night, dark, gone

Build Understanding of Light and Shadow

Activate Background Knowledge
SoR Component: Language Comprehension

Show 2–3 images of shadows at different times of day to build schema.

Vocabulary Preview (Tier 2 + Tier 3 Words)
SoR Component: Vocabulary + Oral Language

Use visuals, gestures, and simple definitions.

Decode → Clarify Meaning Loop

“When is it short?”
“How do you know?”
Encourage students to answer using evidence from the text or picture.

Reinforce understanding of simple cause and effect.

Oral Explanation Using Sentence Frames

“My shadow is ___.”
Support syntax and academic language.

Explain Your Thinking
SoR Component: Language Comprehension

Encourage students to use ideas about light and darkness.
Build reasoning and early science understanding.

2::lightandshadows.answers: 1. Morning. 2. Noon. 3. By linking movement to light. 4. No sunlight = no shadow. 5. Answers vary.
2::lightandshadows.oral_prompts: Make a shadow with your hand.

2::makinggoodchoices.title: Making Good Choices
2::makinggoodchoices.reader_text: We make good choices every day. We help our friends when they are sad. We share our toys with others. We listen to our teacher. We say kind words. Good choices make everyone happy. Good choices make our day brighter. I try to make good choices too.

2::makinggoodchoices.extension: Create class choice posters; role-play scenarios.

2::unusualcreatures.title: Unusual Creatures
2::unusualcreatures.reader_text: Some animals look strange. This one has very long legs. That one has a huge nose. Some look funny to me. But all animals are special. They are all different in their own way. I enjoy learning about strange animals. They make the world interesting.

2::frozenwater.reader_text: Water is a liquid. When it gets very cold, it freezes. It turns into hard ice. The ice is slippery. When the sun comes out, the ice melts. It turns back into water again. Water changes with temperature.

Draw your own treasure map and label where each clue leads.

2::grandpasoldmap.questions_type: 1. What does the map show? (literal) 2. Where do they look? (literal) 3. What pattern does the story use? (skill: repeated frame) 4. Why is treasure hunting exciting? (inferential) 5. Draw your own treasure map. (creative)
2::grandpasoldmap.answers: 1. A treasure. 2. Basement, roof, window. 3. ‘We look…’ repeated. 4. Surprise, mystery. 5. Answers vary.
2::grandpasoldmap.oral_prompts: Point ‘up’ (roof), ‘down’ (basement).” Make treasure maps; act out the search."
2::grandpasoldmap.extension: This story reinforces directional vocabulary with a fun, structured adventure.

2::downtownadventures.title: Downtown Adventures
2::downtownadventures.reader_text: We go downtown. It is a busy place. There are tall buildings. We see buses and cars. We walk past the shops. We buy a snack. I like going downtown. There is always something happening.
2::downtownadventures.objective: Teach vocabulary for busy city environments and movement.
2::downtownadventures.core_vocab_raw: city center, busy, tall, building, bus, car, shop, downtown, happening

2::waysanimalsstaysafe.title: Ways Animals Stay Safe
2::waysanimalsstaysafe.reader_text: Animals have many ways to stay safe. A turtle hides in its shell. A skunk sprays a bad smell. A porcupine has sharp quills. These things protect them from danger. Animals use their bodies in smart ways. Staying safe helps them survive.
2::waysanimalsstaysafe.objective: Teach defense mechanisms and survival vocabulary.
2::waysanimalsstaysafe.core_vocab_raw: turtle, hide, shell, skunk, spray, smell, porcupine, quill, protect, danger, survive

Before Reading

SoR Component: Knowledge Building
“What is the same about all these animals?”

2::waysanimalsstaysafe.answers: 1. Hides in shell. 2. Sprays smell. 3. Each protects itself. 4. To survive danger. 5. Answers vary.
2::waysanimalsstaysafe.extension: Draw animal defenses; create creature cards.
2::waysanimalsstaysafe.phonics_rule: long a (ways ay), long a (stay ay)

2::myfastshoes.title: My Fast Shoes
2::myfastshoes.reader_text:
My new shoes are shiny.
They are squishy.
They make my feet feel good.
I hop up and down in my shoes.
I stretch in my shoes.
I jog in my shoes.
I run in my shoes.
I run and I run.
My shoes help me run so fast.
I can’t wait to race with my friends at school!
2::myfastshoes.objective: Describe clothing using adjectives; identify action verbs; understand cause/effect (shoes → fast running).

Before Reading

2::myfastshoes.reading_strategy: Picture walk; TPR (hop/jog/run); echo reading; verb chart

2::myfastshoes.answers: 1. Shiny and squishy. 2. Hop, stretch, jog, run. 3. Good and excited. 4. At school. 5. Shiny, squishy. 6. Hop, stretch, jog, run. 7. They help the child run fast.
2::myfastshoes.oral_prompts: Act out “hop,” “stretch,” “jog,” and “run.” “Which word tells how the shoes feel?” “Show me how you would race.”
2::myfastshoes.extension: Create an action chart with verbs from the story; draw your own “fast shoes” and label them with adjectives; sequence the actions in order.

2::mywigglytooth.title: My Wiggly Tooth
2::mywigglytooth.reader_text:
Wiggle. Wiggle.
My tooth is wiggly.
I wiggle it with my finger.
I wiggle it in my mouth.
I wiggle it all day.
It wiggles.
It twists.
Pop! It falls out!
I lost my tooth!
My tooth is so small.
It is white and sharp.
The teacher gives me a tiny box.
I put my tooth inside.
The box looks like a treasure chest.
I feel proud that I lost my tooth.

My Lucky Day

I woke up in a good mood.
The birds were singing outside.
I felt happy.
I hopped out of bed.
Mom made my favorite breakfast.
On my way home from school, I saw a kitten.
It was sleeping under a bush.
“Mew. Mew.”
I picked the kitten up and held it close to my heart.
I showed Mom the kitten and asked her if we could keep it.
Mom said, “Yes!”
It was my lucky day!

2::myluckyday.title: My Lucky Day
2::myluckyday.reader_text:
I woke up in a good mood.
The birds were singing outside.
I felt happy.
I hopped out of bed.
Mom made my favorite breakfast.
On my way home from school, I saw a kitten.
It was sleeping under a bush.
“Mew. Mew.”
I picked the kitten up and held it close to my heart.
I showed Mom the kitten and asked her if we could keep it.
Mom said, “Yes!”
It was my lucky day!

2::changingseasons.title: Changing Seasons
2::changingseasons.reader_text: The leaves turn red and yellow in fall. In winter, snow covers the ground. In spring, flowers bloom. In summer, the sun shines bright. The seasons change all year.
